robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a multi-room building, reading labels, sorting varied items, operating elevators/doors, and placing items in correct departmental mailboxes. Multi-step generalization, label reading, and unstructured navigation remain major blockers for current humanoids.

task: Provide jury escort to restaurant and other areas outside of courtroom to prevent jury contact with public.
human_time_rationale: One escort instance = walking jury group from courtroom to destination (e.g., restaurant) and back, including waiting/monitoring at destination; ~15–25 min typical.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating public spaces, managing group dynamics, recognizing and intercepting unauthorized contact, and exercising legal/social judgment — far beyond current robot generalization, perception of intent, or authority to intervene.

task: Treat diseases of female organs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient consultation + treatment session (exam, diagnosis, procedure/prescription). A competent gynecologist/physician typically spends 30–60 min per patient.
robot_success_rationale: Treating disease requires medical diagnosis, clinical judgment, pharmacological knowledge, fine dexterous procedures, and legal licensure. Far beyond any 2026 general-purpose humanoid's capabilities in perception, reasoning, and dexterity.

task: Fit insulation around obstructions, and shape insulating materials and protective coverings as required.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fitting and shaping insulation around a single obstruction/section. A skilled insulator takes ~30–60 min to measure, cut, shape, and secure material around pipes, beams, or fixtures.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement, cutting, and custom shaping of flexible/rigid materials around irregular 3D obstructions in cramped spaces. Demands high dexterity, tool use (knives, snips), and spatial reasoning far beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Call emergency responders or the proper authorities and provide motorist assistance, such as giving directions or helping jump start a stalled vehicle.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one motorist assist event (e.g., jump-start or directions). Includes assessing the situation, communicating with motorist, and completing the action; ~10–15 min typical.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured mobility, natural conversation, calling emergency services, and fine dexterous tool-use (jumper cables on car battery). Current humanoids lack reliable outdoor navigation, phone/radio use, and complex multi-step vehicle interaction.

task: Set up exhibitions of artwork for display or sale.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a single exhibition (hanging, placing, labeling artwork in a gallery space). A competent art handler typically takes 3–5 hours for a modest show.
robot_success_rationale: Requires aesthetic judgment, precise wall-hanging with varied hardware, careful handling of fragile/irregular art objects, spatial planning, and label placement. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, perception, and generalization capabilities.

task: Check adhesives to ensure that they will work and will remain durable.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting adhesives on one workpiece/batch; involves visual check, tactile peel/press test, and verifying cure status or spec compliance.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced tactile sensing (peel strength, tack), visual judgment of cure state, and contextual knowledge of adhesive specs. Current robots lack reliable force-tactile feedback and domain reasoning for pass/fail decisions.

task: Build, test, and modify product prototypes, using working models or theoretical models constructed with computer simulation.
human_time_rationale: One instance = building and testing a single prototype iteration. Skilled engineers typically spend 2–5 hours per cycle including physical assembly, bench testing, and recording results.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative engineering judgment, computer simulation, iterative design decisions, and multi-tool dexterity across highly varied setups. Far beyond current robot autonomy—demands abstract reasoning and generalization robots cannot yet provide.

task: Care for and treat women during prenatal, natal, and postnatal periods.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single prenatal, natal, or postnatal care visit/session. A typical midwife/OB appointment or postnatal check runs ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires advanced clinical judgment, fine dexterous examination, emotional caregiving, sterile procedures, and real-time crisis response. Far beyond any current general-purpose robot's medical, dexterous, and social capabilities.

task: Mix sealing compounds by hand or with portable electric mixers.
human_time_rationale: One batch: open containers, combine components, mix by hand or with a handheld electric paddle mixer until uniform. Typical 5–10 min depending on volume and compound type.
robot_success_rationale: Requires opening containers, measuring/pouring viscous materials, operating a handheld mixer with sustained force control, and judging mix consistency — all highly challenging for current humanoids due to force regulation, tool handling, and sensory feedback limits.

task: Prepare surfaces for insulation application by brushing or spreading on adhesives, cement, or asphalt, or by attaching metal pins to surfaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one typical surface section (e.g., a wall or pipe run) for insulation. Includes spreading adhesive/cement or attaching pins, ~30–60 min for a competent insulation worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating varied construction environments, applying adhesives/asphalt with consistent coverage, and attaching small metal pins to irregular surfaces. Poor generalization to unstructured job sites, messy materials, and varied surface geometries limit current robots severely.

task: Apply, remove, and repair insulation on industrial equipment, pipes, ductwork, or other mechanical systems such as heat exchangers, tanks, and vessels, to help control noise and maintain temperatures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = insulating one section of pipe or equipment (e.g., a 10-ft pipe run or one small vessel). A competent insulator typically takes 60–120 minutes per unit section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires cutting, fitting, and fastening flexible/rigid insulation materials around irregular 3D geometries in cluttered industrial spaces. Demands high dexterity, force control, tool use (knives, adhesives, jacketing), and spatial reasoning far beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Measure and cut insulation for covering surfaces, using tape measures, handsaws, power saws, knives, or scissors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = measuring and cutting insulation for a single surface/section. A competent worker measures, marks, and cuts one piece in ~10–15 minutes depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement, marking, and controlled cutting of flexible/compressible insulation materials with varied tools (saw, knife, scissors). Flexible material handling, tool switching, and accurate dimensional cutting far exceed current humanoid robot dexterity and generalization.

task: Install metal molding at wall corners to secure wallboard.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing metal corner molding on one wall corner (~8 ft). A skilled tradesperson measures, cuts, positions, and fastens the bead in ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement, tin-snip cutting of metal, alignment along a plumb corner, and consistent nailing/screwing—multi-step fine dexterity with tools. Current humanoids lack the tool-use chaining and force control needed for acceptable trade quality.

task: Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one machine serviced. Typical PM (lubrication, filter checks, tightening, minor fixes) takes ~30–60 min for a competent technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diverse fine dexterity (fasteners, hoses, filters), tool use, fault diagnosis, and adaptation to varied machine types/layouts. Current humanoids lack reliable tool manipulation, diagnostic reasoning, and generalization across unstructured mechanical environments.

task: Seal joints between plasterboard or other wallboard to prepare wall surfaces for painting or papering.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sealing joints in a standard room (~10–15 linear metres of joints). A competent taper applies joint compound, tape, and feathers edges in ~45 min (excluding drying time).
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise compound application, embedding paper tape, and feathering thin layers over irregular seams — demanding fine dexterity, consistent force control, and tool-use generalization far beyond current humanoid robots' demonstrated capability.

task: Inspect problem roofs to determine the best repair procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one problem roof. A competent roofer walks the roof, assesses damage, and determines repair procedures — typically 30–60 minutes for a residential roof.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating steep, sloped, unstable outdoor surfaces — well beyond current humanoid locomotion. Also demands expert-level diagnosis and repair planning. Outdoor unstructured terrain and high fall risk make autonomous success nearly impossible.

task: Lock doors and gates of entrances and exits to secure buildings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = locking a single door or gate. A competent worker walks to the entrance, checks the door/gate, and locks it (key, deadbolt, padlock, or latch) in ~2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can navigate to doors and grasp handles at ~50–70%, but operating keys, deadbolts, padlocks, and varied latch mechanisms requires fine dexterity and generalization that current humanoids struggle with. Outdoor/uneven terrain near gates adds locomotion difficulty.

task: Monitor transit areas and conduct security checks to protect railroad properties, patrons, and employees.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patrol shift segment or security round of a transit area; a competent officer completes a structured circuit in ~60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires crowd navigation, threat judgment, social interaction, authority communication, and dynamic decision-making in unpredictable public spaces. Current robots lack reliable social reasoning, de-escalation, and legal authority to act autonomously as security agents.

task: Cover exposed nailheads with roofing cement or caulking to prevent water leakage or rust.
human_time_rationale: One instance = covering all exposed nailheads on a typical roof section. A roofer with cement/caulk gun takes ~20 min to locate and seal nailheads on one roof area.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor roof locomotion on sloped, uneven surfaces (very high fall/stability risk), precise detection of small nailheads, and dexterous application of caulk/cement — all beyond current humanoid capabilities in unstructured outdoor environments.

task: Perform cesarean sections or other surgical procedures as needed to preserve patients' health and deliver babies safely.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one cesarean section procedure. A typical C-section takes ~45–75 minutes from incision to closure for a competent surgical team.
robot_success_rationale: Surgical robotics (e.g., da Vinci) assist but are not autonomous; general-purpose humanoids lack the surgical precision, sterile technique, real-time clinical judgment, and fine dexterity required. Autonomous surgery on humans is far beyond current frontier robots.

task: Prescribe or administer treatment, therapy, medication, vaccination, and other specialized medical care to treat or prevent illness, disease, or injury.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient treatment/care encounter (e.g., administering medication, giving a vaccination, or performing a therapy session). Median across routine to moderately complex encounters.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, diagnosis, and legally regulated decision-making far beyond current robots. Fine dexterity (injections, IV lines), patient interaction, and adaptive reasoning in unstructured medical settings are all well outside 2026 frontier capabilities.

task: Cover, seal, or finish insulated surfaces or access holes with plastic covers, canvas strips, sealants, tape, cement or asphalt mastic.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sealing/finishing one insulated surface or access hole. A skilled insulation worker typically spends ~15–25 minutes applying covers, tape, mastic, or sealant to a single section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires varied materials (mastic, tape, canvas, cement), precise surface contact, and force-controlled spreading/application in cluttered construction environments. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, tool-use versatility, and unstructured-environment generalization needed for reliable autonomous execution.

task: Correct deformities by means of plaster casts and strapping.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying a corrective plaster cast or strapping to one patient. A competent orthotist/clinician typically takes 30–60 minutes including assessment, molding, and securing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert clinical judgment, precise force-controlled manipulation on a human body, fine dexterity with wet plaster/bandaging, and real-time biomechanical assessment. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities; safety risks are extreme.

task: Surgically treat conditions such as corns, calluses, ingrown nails, tumors, shortened tendons, bunions, cysts, or abscesses.
human_time_rationale: One surgical procedure (e.g., bunion correction or ingrown nail removal) for a competent podiatric surgeon, including prep and closure; typical minor foot surgeries run 30–90 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Surgical precision demands sub-millimeter dexterity, tactile force control, sterile technique, real-time adaptive decision-making, and patient safety protocols far beyond any current general-purpose humanoid robot's capabilities.

task: Perform ventilation tasks, such as hanging ventilation curtains and tubes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = hanging one ventilation curtain or tube run in a mining/industrial setting. A competent worker spends ~30–60 min positioning, securing, and adjusting the curtain or ducting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working in confined, unstructured underground/industrial spaces, overhead reaching with large flexible materials, anchoring to irregular surfaces, and interpreting ventilation plans. Current humanoids lack the reach, payload, and contextual judgment for this.

task: Dust rocks after bolting.
human_time_rationale: One instance = dusting loose rock debris/swarf from a bolted surface or rock face after drilling/bolting. A worker uses a brush or air nozzle for ~3 minutes per bolt location.
robot_success_rationale: Brushing/blowing dust from a surface is a moderate manipulation task, but rough/uneven rock surfaces, variable debris distribution, confined underground environments, and correct tool use reduce success. Locomotion on uneven terrain also a challenge.

task: Drill test holes and test bolts for specified tension, using torque wrenches.
human_time_rationale: One instance = drilling one test hole and torque-testing one bolt. A competent worker sets up, drills, inserts bolt, and applies torque wrench check in ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise drill alignment, switching tools (drill → torque wrench), applying calibrated rotational force, and reading torque values. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, tool-change fluency, and force-controlled drilling needed for reliable autonomous execution.

task: Space and fasten together rods in forms according to blueprints, using wire and pliers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assembling and wire-tying a single rebar form section per blueprint specs; a competent ironworker typically takes 30–60 minutes per form section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading blueprints, precise 3D rod spacing, repetitive wire-twisting with pliers under tension, and working on uneven construction sites. Current robots lack the blueprint interpretation, fine dexterous tool-use with pliers, and outdoor terrain adaptability needed.

task: Locate and supply materials to masons for installation, following drawings or numbered sequences.
human_time_rationale: One instance = locating and delivering one batch/set of materials (e.g., bricks, stones, mortar) to a mason at the worksite per numbered sequence or drawing section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading/interpreting construction drawings, navigating unstructured outdoor/construction terrain, identifying and handling heavy/irregular masonry materials, and coordinating with human workers — far beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Perform facilities maintenance work, such as constructing or repairing structures or controlling weeds or pests.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single maintenance job (e.g., patching a wall, fixing a fence, or applying pest treatment). A competent worker typically takes 60–120 min per discrete job.
robot_success_rationale: Highly unstructured tasks spanning construction, repair, and chemical application require tool-use generalization, outdoor mobility, problem-solving, and safety judgment far beyond current humanoid capabilities. Extremely low success rate.

task: Place veneers onto teeth to conceal defects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = bonding veneers onto one patient's teeth (typically 4–8 veneers). A skilled dentist takes ~45–90 minutes including etching, bonding, and curing per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme sub-millimeter dexterity inside a small, wet, curved oral cavity on a moving patient. Beyond any current humanoid robot's fine motor, force-sensing, and perception capabilities. No viable platform exists.

task: Fill blower hoppers with insulating materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = filling one blower hopper with bagged insulation material (e.g., cellulose or fiberglass). A worker opens bags and loads material until the hopper is full, typically 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires lifting/tearing bulky bags, pouring loose fibrous material into a specific hopper opening, and navigating a cluttered job site. Current robots struggle with bag manipulation, dusty/unstructured environments, and material flow control.

task: Install partially overlapping layers of material over roof insulation surfaces, using chalk lines, gauges on shingling hatchets, or lines on shingles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = shingling one standard roof square (100 sq ft). A skilled roofer lays one square in ~1.5–2 hours including alignment, nailing, and overlap checks.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor uneven sloped-surface locomotion, precise repetitive alignment with chalk lines/gauges, handling flexible shingle material, and nailing — all combined at height. No current humanoid handles sloped rooftop work reliably.

task: Remove old insulation, such as asbestos, following safety procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing old/hazardous insulation from a single room or section. A certified abatement worker typically takes 2–4 hours including setup, containment, removal, and bagging.
robot_success_rationale: Requires specialized hazmat protocols, respirators, full containment setup, and regulatory compliance. Robots cannot don PPE, seal containment zones, handle friable asbestos safely, or meet legal abatement standards. Extreme liability and safety constraints.

task: Prepare surfaces for insulation application by brushing or spreading on adhesives, cement, or asphalt, or by attaching metal pins to surfaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a typical surface section (e.g., a wall or pipe run) for insulation. Brushing/spreading adhesives or attaching pins over a standard work area takes ~30–60 min for a skilled insulator.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sustained force-controlled spreading of adhesives/asphalt on irregular, often vertical or overhead surfaces, plus precise pin attachment. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, tool-use adaptability, and surface-following control needed for acceptable quality.

task: Clean and lubricate bicycle parts.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning and lubricating key parts (chain, derailleurs, brakes) on one bicycle. A competent mechanic takes ~25–35 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to apply lubricant precisely, navigate complex 3D geometry of chain/gears, avoid over-lubing brakes, and reposition around an irregular freestanding object. Well beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity limits.

task: Identify and treat spots on garments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and spot-treating a single garment. A skilled laundry worker identifies stains and applies appropriate treatment in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires subtle visual perception to detect varied stains on patterned/textured fabric, stain-type classification, precise product selection, and controlled application of treatment without damaging fabric. Current robots lack reliable stain-detection vision and the nuanced dexterous manipulation needed.

task: Install new tires and tubes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = mounting a new tire and tube on one wheel (e.g., bicycle or light vehicle). A competent mechanic takes ~20–40 min including bead seating and inflation.
robot_success_rationale: Tire/tube installation requires high-force, precise bead manipulation, use of specialized levers, and inflation tools. Current humanoid robots lack the grip strength, force control, and tool-use generalization needed for this deformable, high-resistance task.

task: Perform minor maintenance on emergency medical services vehicles, such as ambulances.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one minor maintenance session on a single EMS vehicle (e.g., checking fluids, tire pressure, lights, cleaning, restocking). A competent technician/EMT typically takes 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating around/under a large vehicle, operating specialized automotive tools, checking fluid levels, inspecting tires, and restocking medical supplies — highly varied, unstructured tasks across locomotion, dexterity, and tool-use that far exceed 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Cut and fit wire mesh or fabric, using hooked rods, and position fabric or mesh in concrete to reinforce concrete.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cutting/fitting and placing mesh/fabric into one formed section (e.g., a wall panel or slab bay). A competent ironworker takes ~20–30 min per section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires cutting stiff wire mesh, manipulating large flexible sheets, using hooked rods precisely in a dynamic construction site. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, tool-use, and unstructured outdoor terrain capability for this multi-step task.

task: Cover and line structures with blown or rolled forms of materials to insulate against cold, heat, or moisture, using saws, knives, rasps, trowels, blowers, or other tools and implements.
human_time_rationale: One instance = insulating one standard wall/ceiling section or duct run (~10–20 linear ft). A skilled insulator cuts, fits, and secures material in ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized tools (blowers, saws, trowels) in cluttered, cramped, unstructured construction environments with overhead/confined work, precise fitting around irregular structures — far beyond 2026 humanoid capability.

task: Clean common areas, change light bulbs, and make minor property repairs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one common area (lobby, hallway, or lounge). Sweeping, mopping, wiping surfaces, emptying trash for a typical shared space.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can sweep, mop, and wipe flat surfaces reasonably well, but unstructured common areas with furniture, clutter, and varied layouts reduce reliability. Trash handling and edge-cleaning remain challenging.

task: Check the stability of roof and rib support systems before mining face areas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting roof and rib supports along one mining face section. A competent miner checks bolts, straps, and ribs systematically, taking ~15–25 minutes per section.
robot_success_rationale: Underground mines are extremely unstructured, dark, dusty, low-clearance, and hazardous. Current humanoids lack the mobility, sensor fusion, and domain-specific knowledge to safely navigate and assess support stability autonomously.

task: Treat clients in professional settings or travel to clients' offices and homes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one client session/visit (in-office or at client location), typically 45–90 minutes; 60 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires autonomous travel (driving) to client sites, nuanced social/professional interaction, and adaptive caregiving judgment — all far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities. Cannot drive in traffic; cannot conduct professional client sessions.

task: Clean and wash rigs, ambulances, or equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning/washing one vehicle (ambulance/rig). A competent worker uses hoses, brushes, and cleaning agents to wash exterior and sanitize interior in ~30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating around large vehicles, wielding hoses/brushes, scrubbing varied surfaces, and accessing interior compartments. Humanoids lack reliable tool-use for high-pressure washing and struggle with unstructured outdoor/garage environments and large-scale coverage tasks.

task: Patrol an assigned area by vehicle or on foot to ensure public compliance with existing parking ordinance.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patrol shift/circuit of an assigned area. A typical parking enforcement patrol covers a zone over ~60 minutes before cycling back.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving a vehicle in traffic (humanoids cannot) or extended outdoor locomotion over varied terrain, reading meters/signs, issuing citations, and exercising legal judgment. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capability.

task: Search for and recapture escapees.
human_time_rationale: One instance = locating and recapturing one escapee. Involves searching an area, tracking, pursuit, and physical restraint/containment — typically 1–2 hours for trained personnel.
robot_success_rationale: Requires long-range outdoor pursuit, adversarial human tracking, real-time decision-making under unpredictable conditions, and safe physical restraint. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in perception, speed, and judgment.

task: Operate on patients to remove, repair, or improve functioning of diseased or injured body parts and systems.
human_time_rationale: One surgical procedure; median operation ranges from ~1 hr (minor) to 6+ hrs (major). ~3 hrs reflects a typical general surgery case (e.g., appendectomy, hernia repair).
robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-millimeter dexterity, sterile field management, adaptive clinical judgment, haptic feedback, and crisis response far beyond any 2026 general-purpose humanoid. Even specialized surgical robots (da Vinci) require surgeon teleoperation.

task: Inspect government property, such as construction sites or public housing, to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one site inspection visit. A competent inspector typically spends 60–120 minutes walking a construction site or public housing unit, reviewing conditions, and documenting findings.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating complex, dynamic construction/outdoor environments, interpreting blueprints and legal specs, exercising regulatory judgment, and producing compliant documentation. Far beyond current robot generalization, judgment, and outdoor mobility capabilities.

task: Check tire pressure and levels of fuel, motor oil, transmission, radiator, battery, or other fluids, adding air or fluids as required.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full pre-trip vehicle fluid/pressure check on one vehicle. A competent worker checks all fluid reservoirs, reads tire pressures, and tops off as needed in ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating a vehicle (hood latch, dipsticks, reservoir caps, tire pressure gauge, air hose, fluid bottles) across highly varied vehicle layouts. Current humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity, fluid-handling precision, and vehicle-specific tool-use generalization needed for autonomous completion.

task: Place blocks under rebar to hold the bars off the deck when reinforcing floors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = placing a single block (chair/spacer) under one rebar bar. A worker positions and sets one block in ~1–2 minutes across a floor pour.
robot_success_rationale: Outdoor/rough concrete deck terrain is challenging for locomotion. Grasping small plastic/concrete chairs and placing them precisely under rebar in cluttered, uneven job-site conditions strains current dexterity and generalization. Heavy debris and safety hazards further limit autonomy.

task: Set up scaffolding to provide safe access to roofs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = erecting a standard scaffolding structure for a typical residential/small commercial roof. A crew of 2–3 competent workers typically takes 1.5–3 hours; median ~2 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires heavy lifting of long poles/boards, precise interlocking of metal couplers at height, outdoor uneven terrain, load-bearing safety compliance, and multi-step structural assembly. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Countersink nails or screws below surfaces of walls before applying sealing compounds, using hammers or screwdrivers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = countersinking a single nail or screw below the wall surface. A skilled worker uses a hammer or screwdriver to do this in ~1–3 minutes per fastener.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise force-controlled tool use (hammer strike or screwdriver torque) on a small, specific wall target. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, controlled impact force, and reliable visual targeting needed for consistent countersinking without damaging the wall surface.

task: Cut rods to required lengths, using metal shears, hacksaws, bar cutters, or acetylene torches.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cutting one rod to a specified length; includes measuring, marking, securing, and cutting. Typical duration ~5 min for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating dangerous, high-force tools (shears, torches, hacksaws) with precise measurement and secure workholding. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, force control, and tool-use capability for safe autonomous operation.

task: Patrol assigned areas by car, boat, airplane, horse, or on foot to enforce game, fish, or boating laws or to manage wildlife programs, lakes, or land.
human_time_rationale: One patrol shift/instance covers a large assigned area; a typical patrol circuit by a game/fish warden is 3–5 hours. 4 hours (240 min) is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating cars, boats, aircraft, or horses across vast, unstructured outdoor terrain — all beyond 2026 humanoid capability. Autonomous enforcement judgment, multi-modal vehicle operation, and rugged outdoor navigation compound failure modes severely.

task: Use complementary aids, such as infrared lamps, wet compresses, ice, and whirlpool baths to promote clients' recovery, relaxation, and well-being.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying a single complementary aid session (e.g., ice pack, compress, or infrared lamp) to one client. Setup, application, monitoring, and removal average ~20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced client interaction, precise thermal/pressure sensing, safe positioning of fragile humans, and operating specialized therapy equipment (whirlpool, infrared lamp). Current robots lack compliant caregiving dexterity, reliable client-safety feedback, and generalization across modalities.

task: Remove deceased remains from place of death.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing a single deceased person from their place of death (e.g., home or care facility) including body bagging, documentation, and loading onto a transport gurney/vehicle. Median ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme whole-body dexterity to handle limp, heavy, irregularly-shaped remains; legal/documentation compliance; navigating varied environments; loading into vehicles. Far beyond current humanoid capabilities. Safety and regulatory constraints also prohibit autonomous operation.

task: Waterproof or damp-proof walls, floors, roofs, foundations, or basements by painting or spraying surfaces with waterproof coatings or by attaching waterproofing membranes to surfaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = waterproofing a single surface/section (e.g., one basement wall or roof section). Includes surface prep, mixing/loading materials, applying coating or membrane, and inspecting coverage — ~90 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating unstructured construction sites, handling viscous coatings/sprayers, applying membranes with consistent pressure across irregular surfaces, and quality inspection. Current robots lack the generalization, tool-use dexterity, and surface-adaptation needed for acceptable quality.

task: Perform safety checks on equipment before operating.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a pre-operation safety check on a single piece of equipment (e.g., forklift, machine tool). A competent operator typically takes 10–20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires varied inspection actions: visual checks, fluid level reading, testing controls, checking gauges, spotting leaks or damage. Perception of subtle defects, fluid levels, and contextual judgment about pass/fail are well beyond reliable 2026 robot capability. Locomotion around equipment is feasible, but nuanced multi-modal inspection with reliable pass/fail judgment is not.

task: Cover roofs or exterior walls of structures with slate, asphalt, aluminum, wood, gravel, gypsum, or related materials, using brushes, knives, punches, hammers, or other tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = covering one standard residential roof section (~1 square, 100 sq ft). A competent roofer takes ~3–4 hours per square including material handling, fastening, and trimming.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working on steep, uneven, outdoor elevated surfaces with varied materials and tools. Current humanoids lack the balance, load-bearing stability, and multi-tool dexterity for unstructured roofing. Fall-risk and safety constraints make autonomous operation essentially infeasible in 2026.

task: Collect and report information on populations or conditions of fish and wildlife in their habitats, availability of game food or cover, or suspected pollution.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single field survey visit covering a defined area/habitat. A competent wildlife technician typically spends 1–3 hours per site transect, sampling, and logging findings.
robot_success_rationale: Requires traversing rough outdoor/aquatic terrain, identifying diverse species, assessing habitat quality, and detecting subtle pollution signs — far beyond current robot generalization, terrain mobility, and ecological perception capabilities.

task: Cut felt, shingles, or strips of flashing to fit angles formed by walls, vents, or intersecting roof surfaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = measuring, marking, and cutting one piece of felt/shingle/flashing to fit one angled roof junction. A skilled roofer completes this in ~10–15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working on a sloped, outdoor roof surface (poor footing/stability), precise measurement of irregular angles, fine cutting tool use, and judgment about fit — well beyond current humanoid locomotion, dexterity, and unstructured-environment generalization.

task: Make repairs to robots or peripheral equipment, such as replacement of defective circuit boards, sensors, controllers, encoders, or servomotors.
human_time_rationale: One repair instance: diagnosing and replacing one faulty component (e.g., circuit board or servomotor) on a robot/peripheral. Typically 30–90 min for a competent technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous manipulation (connectors, screws, PCBs), diagnostic reasoning, tool use, and system-specific knowledge. Current humanoids lack the precision, fault-diagnosis cognition, and specialized tool-handling for autonomous electronics repair.

task: Assist other restaurant workers by serving food and beverages, or by bussing tables.
human_time_rationale: One instance = serving a single table's order or bussing one table; a competent worker completes this in roughly 2–4 minutes on average.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a crowded, dynamic restaurant floor, carrying loaded trays with precision, interacting with patrons, and handling varied dishware. Current humanoids lack reliable tray balancing, crowd navigation, and social interaction needed for acceptable quality.

task: Inspect, repair, or replace brake systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and performing a typical brake job (pads + rotor check/replacement) on one axle of a vehicle. A competent mechanic takes ~60–120 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating a lift, using torque wrenches, bleeding lines, diagnosing wear—all under a vehicle. High-force tool use, confined unstructured space, and safety-critical precision far exceed 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Bleach discolored teeth to brighten and whiten them.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single in-office tooth bleaching session. A dental professional applies bleaching agent, activates it (light/laser), and monitors for ~45–75 min total.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a patient's mouth, precise chemical application to soft/hard tissue boundaries, real-time patient response monitoring, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Treat deformities using mechanical methods, such as whirlpool or paraffin baths, and electrical methods, such as short wave and low voltage currents.
human_time_rationale: One treatment session (e.g., whirlpool bath or electrical stimulation) for a single patient, including setup, active treatment, monitoring, and teardown — typically 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, patient assessment, individualized parameter adjustment, safe skin contact, and therapeutic monitoring. Current humanoids lack medical training integration, fine clinical dexterity, and regulatory clearance for autonomous patient treatment.

task: Protect and preserve native wildlife, plants, or ecosystems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one field session (e.g., surveying a habitat patch, removing invasives, or restoring a planting site). Typical skilled ecologist/conservation worker spends ~2 hrs per site visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires rough outdoor terrain navigation, species-level visual identification, fine manipulation of plants/animals, and adaptive judgment in unstructured environments — all well beyond current humanoid robots' reliable capabilities.

task: Spray roofs, sidings, or walls to bind, seal, insulate, or soundproof sections of structures, using spray guns, air compressors, or heaters.
human_time_rationale: One instance = spraying one standard residential wall/roof section (~200–400 sq ft). A competent worker sets up equipment, applies coating evenly, and cleans up in ~90 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor/elevated terrain navigation, precise spray-gun tool use, consistent coverage judgment, hose management, and compressor/heater setup. Current humanoids lack the mobility, reach, and specialized tool-use needed for rooftop or siding spray work at acceptable quality.

task: Mix mortar, plaster, and grout, manually or using machines, according to standard formulas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one batch of mortar, plaster, or grout. A competent worker measures, combines, and mixes a standard batch in ~15–25 minutes depending on volume and method.
robot_success_rationale: Requires measuring dry/wet materials, pouring, operating a mixer or manual mixing tool, and judging consistency. Fluid/paste handling, precise measurement, and quality assessment (texture, viscosity) are well beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Take prisoners into custody and escort to locations within and outside of facility, such as visiting room, courtroom, or airport.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one escort trip (e.g., cell to visiting room or courthouse transport). Includes custody intake, restraint application, transit, and handoff; ~30–60 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal authority, physical restraint application on a resisting human, situational judgment, interpersonal communication, vehicle operation for off-site transport, and navigation in highly dynamic/unstructured environments — all far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Cement or nail flashing strips of metal or shingle over joints to make them watertight.
human_time_rationale: One instance = flashing one roof joint (e.g., chimney, valley, or dormer). A skilled roofer typically spends 30–60 min cutting, fitting, cementing/nailing, and sealing a single flashing location.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working on steep, sloped, outdoor roof surfaces — severe mobility and balance challenge. Task demands precise cutting, bending, cementing, and nailing of metal strips in cramped, unstructured, high-risk environments. Far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Calibrate instrumentation, such as meters, gauges, and regulators, for pressure, temperature, flow, and level.
human_time_rationale: One instance = calibrating a single instrument (meter, gauge, or regulator). A competent technician typically spends 30–60 minutes per instrument including setup, adjustment, verification, and documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous manipulation of calibration equipment, precise tool use on varied instrument types, reading analog/digital displays, interpreting specifications, and making iterative micro-adjustments — far beyond current humanoid robot generalization and dexterity.

task: Provide and maintain life support and airway management and help prepare patients for emergency surgery.
human_time_rationale: One instance = stabilizing one patient from initial assessment through airway management and pre-op prep; typically 20–45 min for a trauma/emergency case.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (intubation, IV placement), real-time clinical judgment, sterile technique, and adaptive response to unpredictable patient physiology — far beyond current frontier humanoid capabilities.

task: Use weapons, handcuffs, and physical force to maintain discipline and order among prisoners.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single disciplinary intervention (e.g., restraining/handcuffing a non-compliant prisoner). Median incident from recognition to resolution ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires real-time threat assessment, legally/ethically constrained use of force, deploying handcuffs on a resisting human, and weapon handling. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, judgment, and legal authorization for autonomous use of force on humans.

task: Inspect commercial operations relating to fish or wildlife, recreation, or protected areas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one commercial operation inspection (e.g., a fishing charter, wildlife tour, or park concessionaire). A competent wildlife/park inspector typically spends ~1–2 hrs on-site reviewing licenses, gear, compliance, and writing notes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal authority, nuanced judgment, interviews, document review, outdoor/vehicle mobility, and adaptive social interaction. Current humanoids lack legal standing, reliable outdoor navigation, and the cognitive flexibility for regulatory compliance assessment.

task: Erect scaffolding or other installation structures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = erecting a standard single-bay/section of scaffolding. A 2-person crew takes ~2 hours for a typical small-to-mid residential scaffold setup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires heavy lifting of long poles/frames, precise outdoor coordination, climbing, working at height, and complex multi-step assembly with varied hardware. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, strength, and safety margins.

task: Position patient on operating table to maximize patient comfort and surgical accessibility.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning one patient on an OR table, including body alignment, limb placement, padding, and safety straps. Typically 5–15 min with a team.
robot_success_rationale: Requires safe, precise manipulation of a fragile, uncooperative/anesthetized human body; clinical judgment for pressure points and nerve protection; coordinated multi-person teamwork; and specialized OR table controls. Far beyond current robot capability.

task: Apply gravel or pebbles over top layers of roofs, using rakes or stiff-bristled brooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = spreading gravel/pebbles over one standard roof section (~100 sq ft). A competent roofer with rake/broom takes ~60 minutes per section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires locomotion on an unstable, sloped rooftop surface — well outside humanoid capability anchors. Tool use (rake/broom) on uneven terrain with heavy material distribution adds further dexterity and balance challenges. Near-zero feasibility in 2026.

task: Inspect construction work on site to ensure its adherence to the design plans.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one site inspection walkthrough cross-referencing work against design plans; a competent inspector typically spends ~60 min per visit on a standard site.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating unstructured, hazardous outdoor terrain, interpreting complex 2D/3D design plans, comparing them to physical work, exercising engineering judgment, and communicating findings — far beyond 2026 frontier robot capabilities.

task: Conduct methane gas checks to ensure breathing quality of air.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single gas-check sweep of a defined area (e.g., a confined space or utility vault). Includes donning detector, scanning perimeter, logging readings, and reporting — ~15–25 min total.
robot_success_rationale: Robot can carry a gas sensor and navigate indoors, but must access confined/hazardous spaces, operate detection equipment precisely, interpret multi-point readings, and communicate results — generalization and tool-use still limited for this workflow.

task: Punch holes in slate, tile, terra cotta, or wooden shingles, using punches and hammers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = punching one hole in a single shingle. A skilled roofer positions the punch and strikes with a hammer in seconds; ~1 minute accounts for positioning and quality check.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise two-hand coordination (hold punch, swing hammer), controlled impact force on brittle materials (slate, terra cotta crack easily), and typically done on a sloped roof surface — poor mobility, dexterity, and force-control for current humanoids.

task: Massage and knead muscles and soft tissues of the body to provide treatment for medical conditions, injuries, or wellness maintenance.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a standard therapeutic massage session. Most clinical and wellness massages run 60 minutes; 30- and 90-min sessions also common, so 60 min is the competent-worker median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced, continuously adaptive force control across an entire human body, real-time pain/comfort feedback interpretation, precise soft-tissue palpation, and safe skin contact. Far beyond 2026 general-purpose humanoid capabilities; dedicated massage robot prototypes exist but are highly specialized, not general-purpose.

task: Prescribe or administer treatment, therapy, medication, vaccination, and other specialized medical care to treat or prevent illness, disease, or injury in infants and children.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient encounter (exam + prescription/treatment/vaccination). A typical pediatric visit runs 20–40 minutes for a competent physician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires medical licensure, clinical reasoning, diagnosis, fine dexterous procedures (injections, auscultation), and safe interaction with children. Far beyond current general-purpose robot autonomy; legal, safety, and dexterity barriers are prohibitive.

task: Mop or pour hot asphalt or tar onto roof bases.
human_time_rationale: One instance = mopping/pouring hot asphalt over one standard roof section (~100 sq ft). A competent roofer typically covers this area in ~45 minutes including material handling.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling extremely hot, hazardous molten material with large tools (mops, kettles) on uneven, sloped outdoor surfaces. Current humanoids lack heat-safe manipulation, dynamic balancing on rooftops, and safe tool-use for molten asphalt.

task: Distribute insulating materials evenly into small spaces within floors, ceilings, or walls, using blowers and hose attachments, or cement mortars.
human_time_rationale: One instance = insulating one standard wall/floor/ceiling cavity section (~8–10 ft run). A competent insulation worker sets up hose, blows fill evenly, and checks coverage in ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating a blower machine, managing long hoses in cramped/unstructured construction environments, sensing fill density, and applying cement mortar precisely. Current humanoids lack the tool-use integration, situational awareness, and confined-space mobility needed.

task: Apply alternate layers of hot asphalt or tar and roofing paper to roofs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying alternating layers of hot asphalt/tar and roofing paper to one standard roof section (~100 sq ft square). A competent roofer takes ~2 hours per square including heating, spreading, and laying paper.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating a hot tar kettle, navigating steep/sloped outdoor rooftop terrain, managing extremely hot hazardous materials, and precisely rolling out large paper sheets — far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured outdoor environments.

task: Pull down loose rock that cannot be supported.
human_time_rationale: One instance = scaling a section of loose rock in a mine/excavation with a bar tool; a trained miner typically takes 15–25 min per area.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating hazardous, unstructured underground terrain, assessing rock stability via touch/sound/sight, and applying forceful prying with a scaling bar — far beyond current humanoid dexterity, perception, and safety handling in dynamic unstable environments.

task: Inspect mail for the presence of contraband.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one piece of mail (envelope or package). A trained officer physically examines, probes, or scans one item, typically taking 1–5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires specialized scanning tools (X-ray, chemical sniffers), fine manipulation of mail items, and nuanced judgment about suspicious contents. Humanoids lack integrated forensic sensor suites and reliable anomaly recognition for contraband; current robots are far from autonomous deployment in this domain.

task: Apply finger and hand pressure to specific points of the body.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single session of acupressure/massage targeting specific body points. A typical session lasts ~30 minutes for a competent practitioner.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise anatomical landmark identification, calibrated force modulation on a compliant human body, and safe contact with skin. Current robots lack the fine tactile sensitivity, compliant control, and safety guarantees needed for therapeutic body-point pressure on a human.

task: Identify vehicles in violation of parking codes, checking with dispatchers when necessary to confirm identities or to determine whether vehicles need to be booted or towed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = checking one parked vehicle for violations, reading plates, consulting dispatcher if needed, and deciding on action (ticket/boot/tow). ~5–10 min per vehicle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading license plates, interpreting signage/context, communicating with a dispatcher via radio/phone, and making enforcement decisions. Current robots lack reliable outdoor mobility, speech communication, and multi-step procedural judgment in unstructured environments.

task: Perform tests to determine if methane gas is present.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one area/site for methane presence; includes fetching detector, scanning, reading, and logging. ~10–20 min for a competent technician.
robot_success_rationale: Robot can carry a methane sensor and navigate an area, but attaching/operating handheld detectors, interpreting ambiguous readings, navigating confined/cluttered spaces, and logging results reliably is beyond current general-purpose humanoid capability.

task: Conduct fire, safety, and sanitation inspections.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full inspection of a typical commercial premises, covering fire, safety, and sanitation checkpoints. A trained inspector typically takes 45–90 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert domain knowledge, regulatory judgment, reading gauges/signage, opening panels, and documenting violations. Current humanoids lack the cognitive reasoning, specialized sensor suite, and autonomous compliance assessment needed for accepted inspection quality.

task: Cut materials to specified sizes for installation, using power saws or tile cutters.
human_time_rationale: One instance = measuring, marking, and cutting one piece of material to a specified dimension. A skilled tradesperson typically takes 5–10 min per cut including setup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement, marking, safe operation of high-speed power saws or tile cutters, and material handling. Current humanoids lack the tool-use dexterity, force control, and safety compliance needed for reliable autonomous cutting.

task: Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on meters.
human_time_rationale: One instance = servicing a single meter (e.g., utility gas/electric/water meter). A competent technician typically spends 20–40 minutes per unit for inspection, cleaning, minor adjustments, or small part replacement.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous manipulation of small components, use of specialized tools, diagnosis of meter faults, and outdoor/varied environments. Current humanoids lack the precision, tool-use generalization, and domain-specific sensing needed for autonomous meter maintenance.

task: Apply plastic coatings, membranes, fiberglass, or felt over sloped roofs before applying shingles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying underlayment (felt, membrane, etc.) over a typical residential sloped roof (~1,500 sq ft). A two-person crew takes ~2 hours to roll, cut, overlap, and fasten material.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing/navigating steep, sloped, uneven outdoor roof surfaces — far beyond current humanoid locomotion limits. Large material handling, precise overlapping, fastening on an incline, and outdoor unstructured environment make autonomous completion essentially infeasible in 2026.

task: Assemble meal trays with foods in accordance with patients' diets.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assembling one patient meal tray. A trained dietary aide typically takes 3–5 minutes to read the diet order, select correct foods/portions, and arrange them on a tray.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading diet orders, identifying correct food items, portioning, and precise placement—combining language understanding, fine dexterous manipulation of varied soft/liquid foods, and dietary compliance logic. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capability in an unstructured kitchen/cafeteria setting.

task: Draw samples of raw materials, intermediate products, or finished products for validation testing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = drawing a sample from a single material/product lot. Includes locating the source, using appropriate tools/containers, labeling, and logging. Typically 10–20 min per sample.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating varied industrial environments, selecting correct sampling tools, handling liquids/powders/solids with precision, labeling containers, and logging data — well beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity limits in unstructured settings.

task: Smooth rough spots to prepare surfaces for waterproofing, using hammers, chisels, or rubbing bricks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one surface section (~10 sq ft). A skilled tradesperson identifies and smooths rough spots with hand tools in roughly 45 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires forceful, precise tool use (chisel, hammer, rubbing brick) on irregular surfaces, tactile feedback to judge smoothness, and adaptive force control. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, haptic sensing, and tool-wielding strength control needed reliably.

task: Respond to medical emergencies by administering basic first aid or by obtaining assistance from paramedics.
human_time_rationale: One instance = responding to a single medical emergency, from onset to handoff or stabilization. Covers assessment, basic first aid (e.g., CPR, bandaging), and/or calling paramedics — typically 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires rapid situational assessment, nuanced judgment, fine dexterity (CPR compressions, bandaging, airway management), verbal communication with dispatch, and safe interaction with an injured person — all far beyond current humanoid capability in unstructured emergency scenarios.

task: Install various types of bolts, including truss, glue, and resin bolts, traversing entire ceiling spans.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a full bolt pattern across one ceiling span (~10–20 bolts of mixed types). Experienced worker takes ~30–60 min including drilling, resin/glue setting, and tensioning.
robot_success_rationale: Requires overhead drilling and bolt installation in confined, uneven underground/construction ceilings, use of specialized injection tools (resin/glue), high-torque tensioning, and navigating unstable terrain. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Correct surface imperfections or fill chipped, cracked, or broken bricks or tiles, using fillers, adhesives, or grouting materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = repairing a single chipped/cracked brick or tile area: clean surface, apply filler/adhesive/grout, shape, and smooth. ~20–30 min for a competent tradesperson.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to mix, apply, and feather fillers/grout precisely; visual inspection of subtle surface imperfections; tactile feedback for smoothing. Far beyond current humanoid manipulation capability at acceptable quality.

task: Mark tires of parked vehicles with chalk and record time of marking, and return at regular intervals to ensure that parking time limits are not exceeded.
human_time_rationale: One instance = marking one vehicle's tire with chalk and logging the time. A parking enforcement officer takes ~1–2 minutes per vehicle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured mobility, precise chalk marking on curved tire surfaces at ground level, and time-logging. Current humanoids struggle with outdoor terrain, low ground-level dexterous tasks, and coordinating return patrols autonomously.

task: Prepare and blend oils and apply the blends to clients' skin.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting, blending oils, and applying to one client's skin (e.g., massage/aromatherapy session). Typical prep ~5 min, application ~15 min for a body area.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise liquid measurement/blending, safe contact with a human body, gentle adaptive pressure on variable skin surfaces, and social/sensory feedback. Current humanoids lack safe compliant touch, precise fluid handling, and client-responsive caregiving at acceptable quality.

task: Observe and report hazardous conditions, such as missing traffic signals or signs, and street markings that need to be repainted.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patrol route segment or intersection inspection. A competent inspector walks/drives a defined stretch, identifies and logs hazards in ~30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor mobility on varied terrain, multi-modal perception (sign recognition, faded markings, signal status), structured reporting, and safe navigation in live traffic — well beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Glaze top layers to make a smooth finish or embed gravel in the bitumen for rough surfaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = finishing one standard roof section (~100 sq ft); a skilled roofer applies glaze coat or embeds gravel in bitumen in ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working on sloped/outdoor roof surfaces, handling hot bitumen, spreading liquid glaze or broadcasting loose gravel uniformly — extreme generalization, outdoor terrain, and precision coating far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Modify material moving, mixing, grouting, grinding, polishing, or cleaning procedures, according to installation or material requirements.
human_time_rationale: One instance = reviewing requirements and adjusting a specific procedure for a single material/installation context; a skilled tradesperson typically takes 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading specs, interpreting material/installation requirements, and making cognitive procedural judgments — not a physical manipulation task. Current humanoid robots lack the reasoning, domain knowledge, and adaptive decision-making needed for autonomous procedure modification.

task: Collaborate with choreographers to refine or modify dance steps.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single collaborative session with a choreographer to review, discuss, and refine a set of dance steps. A typical working session runs ~60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative judgment, aesthetic interpretation, verbal/nonverbal communication, and nuanced body-movement refinement. Current humanoids lack the social cognition, artistic creativity, expressive motor control, and collaborative dialogue needed for choreographic co-creation.

task: Remove snow, water, or debris from roofs prior to applying roofing materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing one average residential roof (~1,500–2,000 sq ft). A competent roofer with tools (broom, squeegee, snow rake) takes ~30–60 min depending on debris load.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing onto a sloped, slippery roof surface — extreme locomotion challenge for humanoids. Outdoor uneven terrain, fall risk, tool handling (broom/rake) on incline, and variable debris types make autonomous success nearly impossible with 2026 systems.

task: Perform simple vehicle maintenance procedures, such as checking oil and gas, and report mechanical problems to supervisors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = checking oil level, fuel level, and noting any obvious mechanical issues on a single vehicle. A competent worker completes this in ~10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires opening hood latches, locating and reading dipsticks, interpreting fluid levels, and verbally/digitally reporting findings. Current humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity for dipstick handling, fluid-reading perception, and unstructured vehicle bay navigation. Reporting also requires communication integration.

task: Install, repair, or replace single-ply roofing systems, using waterproof sheet materials such as modified plastics, elastomeric, or other asphaltic compositions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing/repairing a single-ply roof section (~1,000 sq ft). A crew of 2–3 competent roofers typically completes this in a full workday (~8 hrs).
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rooftop locomotion on sloped, uneven, slippery surfaces; precise membrane seaming/heat-welding; heavy material handling; and situational judgment about weatherproofing quality — all far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Investigate crop, property, or habitat damage or destruction or instances of water pollution to determine causes and to advise property owners of preventive measures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single site investigation visit, including travel to site, assessing damage/pollution, identifying cause, and advising the property owner. Typically 1.5–2.5 hours for a competent field inspector.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires expert-level reasoning across multiple domains (agronomy, ecology, hydrology, law), unstructured outdoor terrain navigation, water/soil sampling, and nuanced verbal communication with stakeholders — well beyond 2026 robot capabilities.

task: Load or unload materials, vehicles, or passengers from vessels.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading or unloading one vessel (e.g., a ferry, barge, or small cargo boat). A competent worker typically takes 30–60 min depending on cargo volume.
robot_success_rationale: Vessels involve dynamic, unstable environments (water movement, gangways, uneven decks), heavy/irregular cargo, and passenger coordination. Current humanoids lack marine mobility, heavy-lift capacity, and situational safety judgment for such unstructured settings.

task: Drive passenger vehicles and trucks used to transport inmates to other institutions, courtrooms, hospitals, and work sites.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one transport trip (e.g., institution to courthouse/hospital). Median round-trip including loading/unloading is roughly 60–120 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot operate passenger vehicles or transport trucks in traffic. No general-purpose robot has autonomous driving capability on public roads, and the task requires real-world navigation, judgment, and safety/security responsibilities far beyond current robotics.

task: Apply grout between joints of bricks or tiles, using grouting trowels.
human_time_rationale: One instance = grouting a typical tile surface (e.g., ~10 sq ft section). A skilled mason/tiler takes ~30–60 min including mixing, applying, and wiping excess.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise trowel force/angle control, consistent joint filling without smearing tile faces, diagonal strokes, and timely cleanup. Current humanoids lack fine compliant tool-use and surface-sensitive feedback needed for acceptable grout quality.

task: Move or position materials such as marble slabs, using cranes, hoists, or dollies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = moving/positioning one marble slab. Includes rigging, operating crane/hoist/dolly, and final placement. Typically 15–25 minutes for a skilled worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating cranes, hoists, or dollies — specialized heavy equipment a humanoid cannot drive or control. Rigging heavy, fragile slabs demands force sensing, precision, and safety judgment far beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Purchase and stock supplies of feed and medicines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single purchasing-and-stocking cycle (ordering supplies and physically stocking them in storage). Includes browsing/ordering (~30 min) and receiving/shelving items (~60 min).
robot_success_rationale: Purchasing requires internet browsing, decision-making, and vendor interaction — beyond current humanoid capability. Physical stocking of varied, fragile medicine containers and bulk feed bags in unstructured storage is also very challenging. No current robot handles the full end-to-end task autonomously.

task: Provide assistance to motorists needing help with problems, such as flat tires, keys locked in cars, or dead batteries.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one roadside assist job (e.g. tire change, jump-start, or lockout). Median across job types for a competent roadside technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor/roadside mobility, heavy tool use (jack, lug wrench, jumper cables), vehicle-specific access, and situational safety judgment. Current humanoids lack the strength, generalization, and outdoor robustness for these unstructured tasks.

task: Provide assistance in the preparation, installation, repair, or rebuilding of tile, brick, or stone surfaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting a tradesperson on a typical tile/brick/stone job segment (e.g., prepping a surface, mixing mortar, handing materials, grouting a section). ~90 min per work segment.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise force-controlled manipulation on irregular surfaces, mixing viscous materials, aligning heavy/fragile tiles, and adapting to varied job-site layouts. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, tool-use generalization, and structural judgment needed for acceptable quality.

task: Examine animal licenses, and inspect establishments housing animals for compliance with laws.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one establishment inspection. A competent animal control/licensing officer typically spends ~1 hour examining licenses, touring facilities, and documenting compliance findings.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading/validating legal documents, navigating varied facilities, assessing animal welfare conditions, exercising regulatory judgment, and interacting with people — all beyond current frontier robot generalization and legal/social authority.

task: Arrange or store materials, machines, tools, or equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = organizing/storing a set of materials or tools in a workspace (e.g., a workbench, storage room, or shelf). A competent worker typically takes ~15 min per session.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick-and-place common objects but struggle with varied shapes, weights, and designated storage logic. Identifying correct locations, handling tools of irregular geometry, and navigating cluttered workspaces reduce reliability significantly.

task: Transport materials, tools, or machines to installation sites, manually or using conveyance equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = transporting a load of materials/tools to an installation site. Median trip including loading, moving, and unloading estimated at ~20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoids can carry objects and navigate indoors, but varied payloads, conveyance equipment operation (forklifts, dollies on rough terrain), and unstructured job-site environments pose major challenges for autonomous generalization.

task: Exercise animals to keep them in healthy condition.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one exercise session for one animal (e.g., walking a dog or running a horse). A typical session lasts ~30 minutes for a competent handler.
robot_success_rationale: Requires leading, handling, and safely guiding live animals outdoors over varied terrain. Animals are unpredictable; robots lack reliable animal-behavior perception, appropriate physical control, and safe generalization across species and environments.

task: Clean laboratory facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one laboratory room (benches, floors, sinks, equipment surfaces). A competent lab cleaner typically takes ~60 minutes per lab room.
robot_success_rationale: Labs have cluttered, hazardous surfaces with fragile/expensive equipment, chemical residues, and strict protocols. Robots lack the perception, dexterity, and domain-specific safety judgment needed. General cleaning robots score higher, but lab-specific constraints drop success significantly.

task: Examine and clean patients' ear canals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = examining and cleaning both ear canals of one patient, including otoscope inspection, irrigation or curettage, and documentation. Typically 10–20 minutes for a competent clinician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-millimeter dexterous tool insertion into a sensitive, irregularly-shaped canal on a moving patient, real-time otoscopic interpretation, and safe force control. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and perception; risk of patient harm is prohibitive.

task: Make arrangements for illegally parked or abandoned vehicles to be towed, and direct tow-truck drivers to the correct vehicles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing one tow request: locating/confirming the vehicle, contacting dispatch, documenting details, and directing the driver. Typically 10–20 minutes per vehicle.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires radio/phone communication, judgment on vehicle legality, coordinating with human drivers, and navigating outdoor/road environments. Current humanoids lack reliable outdoor mobility, communication interfaces, and the situational authority to autonomously dispatch tows.

task: Align machines or equipment, using hoists, jacks, hand tools, squares, rules, micrometers, lasers, or plumb bobs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = aligning a single machine/equipment unit to spec. A skilled millwright typically spends 30–90 min measuring, adjusting, and verifying alignment with precision tools.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-millimeter precision with specialized tools (micrometers, lasers, jacks), heavy manipulation, and expert judgment to interpret readings and make iterative adjustments. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and tool-use generalization.

task: Maintain assigned equipment and supplies, such as hand-held citation computers, citation books, rain gear, tire-marking chalk, and street cones.
human_time_rationale: One instance = daily equipment check/maintenance session; inspecting, cleaning, restocking, and organizing citation gear, rain gear, chalk, and cones takes ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying worn/low supplies, operating a handheld computer interface, stowing varied gear in proper locations, and making judgment calls about equipment condition — beyond reliable autonomous capability for frontier humanoids in 2026.

task: Collect coins deposited in meters.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting coins from one parking meter: unlock vault, remove/empty coin canister, re-secure. Typically 1–3 minutes per meter.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor mobility, using a specialized key/tool to unlock meters, handling loose coins, and re-securing the vault. Key insertion, coin canister removal, and outdoor terrain navigation are beyond reliable 2026 humanoid capability.

task: Perform traffic control duties such as setting up barricades and temporary signs, placing bags on parking meters to limit their use, or directing traffic.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a typical traffic control zone (barricades, signs, meter bags, or directing traffic for a shift segment). ~30–60 min is reasonable for a standard setup or control period.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured terrain, heavy/bulky object handling, dynamic traffic interaction, gestural communication with drivers, and real-time situational judgment. Current humanoids lack traffic-safe outdoor autonomy and social signaling capability.

task: Bathe and groom animals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = bathing and grooming one medium-sized animal (e.g. a dog). Includes wetting, shampooing, rinsing, drying, brushing, and finishing; ~30 min for a competent groomer.
robot_success_rationale: Live, unpredictable animals require extreme adaptability, gentle variable-force control, and safe restraint. Current humanoids lack the compliant dexterity, animal-behavior perception, and wet-environment robustness needed. High injury/escape risk.

task: Monitor and record food temperatures to ensure food safety.
human_time_rationale: One instance = checking and logging the temperature of a single food item/container using a probe thermometer; includes inserting probe, reading, and recording the value.
robot_success_rationale: Robot must grasp a thermometer, insert probe precisely into food/liquid, read the display, and log the value. Fine dexterous tool-use and interpreting varied food containers in unstructured kitchen environments are major limitations for current humanoids.

task: Capture and remove stray, uncontrolled, or abused animals from undesirable conditions, using nets, nooses, or tranquilizer darts as necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = locating, capturing, and removing one stray/uncontrolled animal. Includes approach, tool use, and transport; median ~45 min accounting for variable animal behavior.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor/unstructured navigation, rapid unpredictable animal tracking, precise net/noose/dart deployment on a moving target, and situational judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot generalization and dexterity in dynamic outdoor environments.

task: Maintain or repair studio facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single maintenance/repair visit to a studio facility (e.g., fixing lighting rigs, replacing equipment, patching walls). A competent technician typically spends ~90 min per visit.
robot_success_rationale: Studio maintenance involves highly varied, unstructured tasks: electrical work, carpentry, equipment handling, climbing ladders, and diagnosing faults. Current humanoids lack the tool-use versatility, domain knowledge, and safe generalization needed for autonomous success.

task: Install vapor barriers or layers of insulation on flat roofs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing vapor barrier/insulation on a typical flat roof section (~1,000 sq ft). A competent roofer takes ~2–4 hours including material handling, cutting, and fastening.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rooftop locomotion on uneven/sloped surfaces, heavy material handling, precise cutting and fastening, and working at height — all extremely challenging for current general-purpose humanoids. No commercial deployment exists for this task.

task: Euthanize rabid, unclaimed, or severely injured animals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = euthanizing one animal. Includes assessment, preparation of injection or equipment, restraint, administration, and confirmation of death.
robot_success_rationale: Requires veterinary-grade fine dexterity (IV injection), safe restraint of a distressed/rabid animal, clinical judgment on dosing and confirmation, and legal/ethical authority. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Clean work areas and equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning a single work area/station and its associated equipment (wiping surfaces, tools, machinery). Competent worker takes ~15–25 min.
