robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity to guide thin, flexible yarn/thread through tiny guides, eyes, and needle holes. Current robots lack the fingertip precision, tactile feedback, and fine manipulation needed for such delicate, high-tolerance threading tasks.

task: Apply finishes to objects being crafted.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying a finish (e.g. stain, varnish, paint, lacquer) to one crafted object. A competent worker preps, applies, and smooths in ~15–25 min depending on size/complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity for brush/spray control, consistent pressure, edge masking, surface prep detection, and quality judgment. Current humanoids lack the tactile feedback and precision finishing skill; high failure risk on uneven or irregular craft surfaces.

task: Take action to contain any hazardous chemicals that could catch fire, leak, or spill.
human_time_rationale: One instance = identifying, securing, and containing hazardous chemicals in a single work area. A trained worker inspects containers, seals leaks, moves materials to safe storage, and applies containment measures.
robot_success_rationale: Requires chemical identification (labels, sensors), risk assessment, fine manipulation of varied containers/valves, safe transport of unstable materials, and contextual judgment. Current humanoids lack chemical sensing, safe-handling protocols, and reliable dexterous manipulation for this safety-critical, unstructured task.

task: Change or rotate window displays, interior display areas, or signage to reflect changes in inventory or promotion.
human_time_rationale: One instance = updating a single display area (window, interior display, or signage). A competent visual merchandiser typically spends ~30–60 min rearranging products, swapping signage, and dressing the space.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative spatial judgment, aesthetic decision-making, handling diverse irregular props/signage, climbing fixtures, and interpreting promotional briefs. Far beyond current robot generalization, dexterity, and unstructured environment capabilities.

task: Measure wind, temperature, and humidity in the upper atmosphere, using weather balloons.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing, launching, and monitoring one weather balloon (radiosonde). Covers unpacking, instrument checks, balloon inflation with gas, payload attachment, and release. Typical NWS launch takes ~45–75 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling large, buoyant balloon with helium gas hose, connecting fragile radiosonde payload, managing tether lines, and coordinating a safe outdoor launch. High dexterity, force control, and situational awareness demands exceed current humanoid capabilities; outdoor wind conditions add unpredictability.

task: Set up, or set up and operate textile machines that perform textile processing and manufacturing operations such as winding, twisting, knitting, weaving, bonding, or stretching.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a single textile machine (threading, tensioning, loading bobbins/yarn, calibrating settings) before a production run; ~30–60 min for a competent operator.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (threading fine yarn through guides/needles, tensioning), tool-specific knowledge of varied machine types, precise force control, and generalization across machine configurations — well beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Add chemicals, such as ammonia, chlorine, or lime, to disinfect and deodorize water and other liquids.
human_time_rationale: One instance = treating a single batch/tank of liquid. Human measures dosage, handles chemical containers, adds chemicals, and verifies mixing — typically 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise chemical measurement, handling hazardous/liquid chemicals safely, operating valves and dosing equipment, and verifying results. Frontier humanoids lack reliable fine fluid handling, chemical sensing, and process-control integration in unstructured water-treatment settings.

task: Dust evidence or portions of fire scenes for latent fingerprints.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single fire scene area/section for latent prints; a competent forensic tech takes ~30–60 min to dust, lift, and document prints from a defined scene portion.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous brush control, judgment on surface type/condition, navigating unstable/debris-laden fire scenes, and forensic decision-making on where to dust. Current robots lack the dexterity, perception, and domain reasoning needed.

task: Test material samples for tolerance under tension, compression, and shear to determine the cause of metal failures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one material sample under tension, compression, and shear, including fixture setup, test execution, and data recording. ~90 min for a competent materials technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise specimen mounting in specialized test fixtures (e.g., universal testing machine grips), fine force calibration, tool changes between test modes, and expert interpretation of failure data — far beyond current humanoid robot dexterity and domain generalization.

task: Cut, shape, fit, join, mold, or otherwise process materials, using hand tools, power tools, or machinery.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a single material-processing operation (e.g., cutting a board, shaping a part). A competent worker averages ~45 min per meaningful work unit across trade contexts.
robot_success_rationale: Highly varied task requiring tool selection, setup, force control, and precision across unstructured environments. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use generalization, fine manipulation, and safety compliance for powered machinery operation.

task: Start machines, monitor operations, and make adjustments as needed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one machine startup-to-stable-operation cycle, including startup, monitoring for ~20 min, and minor adjustments. Typical for industrial operators.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading machine-specific controls, interpreting varied status indicators, and making context-sensitive adjustments. Generalization across machine types, fault diagnosis, and corrective action far exceed current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and to obtain product samples.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and running one test cycle on a machine, collecting a sample, and verifying adjustments. Typically 15–30 minutes for a competent operator.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized industrial machines, interpreting controls, recognizing acceptable output, and adapting to varied equipment types. Current humanoids lack machine-specific tool-use, contextual judgment, and reliable fine manipulation for industrial controls.

task: Visit site and record information about access, drainage and topography, and availability of utility services.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one site visit. A competent surveyor/engineer walks the site, assesses access routes, drainage patterns, topography, and utility hookups, and records findings. Typically 60–120 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor terrain navigation, expert interpretation of drainage/topography/utility infrastructure, and structured documentation. Current robots lack domain-expert judgment, reliable outdoor mobility on unstructured sites, and autonomous reporting capability.

task: Arrange properties, furniture, merchandise, backdrops, or other accessories, as shown in prepared sketches.
human_time_rationale: One instance = arranging a full set/scene per a prepared sketch (e.g., retail display, photo backdrop, stage set). A competent human typically takes 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires interpreting 2D sketches into 3D spatial layouts, handling highly varied objects (furniture, backdrops, props), precise aesthetic placement, and generalization across unstructured environments — far beyond current robot capability.

task: Operate and adjust controls on equipment to purify and clarify water, process or dispose of sewage, and generate power.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single operator control round: checking panels, adjusting valves/controls, and logging readings at a water/wastewater treatment or power generation facility. Typically done every 1–2 hours per shift.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized industrial control panels, valves, and SCADA systems across a large facility. Humanoids lack the domain-specific tool interfacing, mobility over industrial terrain, and autonomous decision-making needed for safety-critical utility operations.

task: Preserve, process, and analyze items of evidence obtained from crime scenes and suspects, placing them in proper containers and destroying evidence no longer needed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single piece of evidence (log, bag, label, store). A competent forensic tech typically takes 30–60 min per item including chain-of-custody documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (swabbing, sealing biohazard containers), forensic judgment (contamination avoidance, classification), legal chain-of-custody documentation, and contextual decision-making on destruction — far beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Operate machines to cut multiple layers of fabric into parts for articles such as canvas goods, house furnishings, garments, hats, or stuffed toys.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and cutting one batch/lay of multiple fabric layers into parts. A competent operator spends ~20–30 min spreading, aligning, marking, and cutting one lay.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating industrial cutting machines (straight-knife, band-knife, die-press), handling large limp fabric lays, precise pattern alignment, and quality judgment. Current humanoids lack machine-operation dexterity, fabric-handling skill, and tool integration for this task.

task: Adjust cutting techniques to types of fabrics and styles of garments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting and applying the appropriate cutting technique for one garment piece/fabric type. A skilled tailor/cutter assesses fabric, selects method, and cuts one panel in ~20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert tactile discrimination of fabric type, fine dexterous scissor/rotary-cutter control on compliant materials, style-based judgment, and adaptive technique selection — all far beyond current humanoid robot capability.

task: Clean, oil, and lubricate machines, using air hoses, cleaning solutions, rags, oil cans, or grease guns.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning, oiling, and lubricating a single machine. A competent maintenance worker typically takes 20–40 minutes per machine depending on size and complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-tool use (air hose, oil can, grease gun, rags), precise lubrication point targeting, and unstructured machine geometry. Tool-switching, fluid control, and force regulation are beyond reliable 2026 humanoid capability.

task: Construct or assemble displays or display components from fabric, glass, paper, or plastic, using hand tools or woodworking power tools, according to specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assembling a single display unit (e.g., retail fixture or signage panel). A competent display builder typically takes 45–90 minutes depending on complexity; 60 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-material handling (fabric, glass, plastic, wood), interpreting varied specs, using power tools safely, and fine dexterous joining. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use, multi-material dexterity, and spec-reading generalization for this complexity.

task: Secure deceased body and obtain evidence from it, preventing bystanders from tampering with it prior to medical examiner's arrival.
human_time_rationale: One instance = securing a single scene from discovery until ME arrival. Typical wait is 30–60 min; officer must establish perimeter, document evidence, and manage bystanders throughout.
robot_success_rationale: Requires law-enforcement authority, nuanced social crowd control, verbal deterrence, legal judgment, and dynamic outdoor/indoor navigation. Current humanoids lack authority, reliable speech interaction, crowd management, and autonomous legal decision-making.

task: Conduct inspections and acceptance testing of newly installed fire protection systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full inspection & acceptance test of one newly installed fire protection system (e.g., sprinkler or suppression system in a typical building); covers walk-through, component checks, flow/pressure tests, and report.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert judgment, reading gauges/labels, operating test valves, traversing multi-story unstructured environments, interpreting code compliance, and generating documentation. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity limits.

task: Place prices or descriptive signs on backdrops, fixtures, merchandise, or floor.
human_time_rationale: One instance = placing a single price tag or sign on one surface/fixture. A competent retail worker reads the label, positions it correctly, and affixes it in ~1–3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle small signs/tags, reading text for correct placement, and navigating varied fixture heights and surfaces. Perception of correct location and peel-and-stick manipulation remain challenging for 2026 humanoids.

task: Note, mark, and photograph location of objects found, such as footprints, tire tracks, bullets and bloodstains, and take measurements of the scene.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single crime scene. A competent forensic technician typically spends 1–3 hours documenting, photographing, marking, and measuring a scene of moderate complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires detecting subtle, highly varied evidence (bloodstains, tire tracks, footprints) in unpredictable environments, precise placement of markers, professional photography, and accurate spatial measurement—all far beyond current general-purpose robot reliability. Perception, fine manipulation, and contextual judgment are major blockers.

task: Participate or assist in raids and arrests.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one raid or arrest operation, from briefing/staging through execution and securing the scene. Typical operations run 1–3 hours total.
robot_success_rationale: Requires real-time threat assessment, split-second judgment, complex social/verbal interaction, unpredictable environments, use of force decisions, and legal accountability. No current humanoid robot can operate autonomously in live law-enforcement scenarios safely or legally.

task: Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single machine (e.g., industrial press, conveyor, motor). A competent technician walks around it, checks sounds, visuals, and readings in ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can capture visual/thermal images but lack tactile sense, contextual judgment, and domain expertise to diagnose subtle faults (unusual sounds, vibration feel, worn seals). Generalization across machine types is very limited.

task: Observe woven cloth to detect weaving defects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one standard bolt/panel of woven cloth (~1–2 meters). A trained textile inspector visually scans and identifies defects in roughly 3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Stationary machine-vision systems do this well, but a general-purpose humanoid lacks specialized optics, controlled lighting, and fine-grained textile perception. Generalization to varied fabrics, defect types, and lighting is limited in 2026 frontier robots.

task: Test sites and materials to establish facts, such as burn patterns and flash points of materials, using test equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one site/material set. Involves setup, controlled burns, flash point tests, data recording, and initial analysis — typically 1–2 hours for a competent fire investigator.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert forensic judgment, precise handling of flammable materials, operating specialized test equipment (e.g., flash point testers), interpreting burn patterns, and navigating hazardous environments — far beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Install decorations, such as flags, banners, festive lights, or bunting on or in building, street, exhibit hall, or booth.
human_time_rationale: One instance = decorating a single booth or building facade. A competent worker typically spends ~45 min unboxing, positioning, and securing flags, banners, lights, or bunting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing ladders, anchoring to varied surfaces, handling flexible/tangled materials (lights, bunting), and working at height outdoors. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, mobility, and tool-use limits in unstructured environments.

task: Dress mannequins for displays.
human_time_rationale: One instance = dressing one mannequin with a complete outfit. A skilled retail worker takes ~10–15 min to undress, position, and redress a mannequin with garments and accessories.
robot_success_rationale: Requires disassembling/reassembling mannequin limbs, handling soft/deformable clothing, fine dexterity for zippers/buttons, and aesthetic judgment. Far beyond reliable 2026 robot capability in unstructured retail settings.

task: Secure persons at scene, keeping witnesses from conversing or leaving the scene before investigators arrive.
human_time_rationale: One instance = securing and holding witnesses at a scene until investigators arrive; typical wait is 20–40 minutes depending on response time.
robot_success_rationale: Requires authoritative verbal communication, social compliance enforcement, reading human behavior/intent, and legal authority. Current robots lack credible presence, nuanced social interaction, and de-escalation capability needed to keep people from leaving.

task: Inspect and test fire protection or fire detection systems to verify that such systems are installed in accordance with appropriate laws, codes, ordinances, regulations, and standards.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting/testing a fire protection/detection system in a typical commercial building. A competent inspector spends ~2 hours checking sprinklers, alarms, panels, detectors, and documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading technical documentation, operating control panels, interpreting alarm signals, navigating complex multi-floor environments, interfacing with specialized equipment, and making code-compliance judgments — far beyond current robot generalization and cognitive capability.

task: Install, level, and align machine components such as gears, chains, guides, dies, cutters, or needles to set up machinery for operation.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up one machine with its components (gears, dies, etc.). A skilled machinist/setup technician typically takes 30–60 min per setup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise torquing, alignment to tight tolerances, tool-switching, and verification with gauges. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and force-control precision; no commercial deployment exists for this task.

task: Operate pumps connected to high-pressure hoses.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up, activating, monitoring, and shutting down a pump for a single job cycle. Typical duration 15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: High-pressure hoses require firm coupling, valve manipulation, pressure monitoring, and hazard awareness. Humanoids lack the grip strength, specialized tool-use, and safety judgment needed for high-pressure systems; risk of catastrophic failure is high.

task: Photograph damage and evidence related to causes of fires or explosions to document investigation findings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = photographing a single fire/explosion scene. A skilled investigator spends ~30–60 min systematically capturing damage, evidence points, and causation indicators.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert-level scene interpretation to know *what* to photograph, navigation over rubble/hazardous debris, and professional camera control. Current robots lack investigative reasoning, unstructured terrain mobility, and contextual judgment needed.

task: Thread yarn, thread, and fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines for weaving, knitting, or other processing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = threading a single machine (loom, knitting machine, etc.). A competent textile worker typically takes 10–20 minutes to route yarn/thread through all guides, needles, and rollers correctly.
robot_success_rationale: Extremely fine dexterity required to handle thin, flexible, deformable filaments through tiny needle eyes, tight guides, and rollers. Current humanoids lack the precision finger control, tactile feedback, and thread-tension sensing needed. High failure risk.

task: Inspect products to ensure that specifications are met and to determine if machines need adjustment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single product/batch sample against specs. A competent worker typically takes 2–5 minutes to visually/physically check dimensions, finish, and tolerances.
robot_success_rationale: Vision-based defect detection is maturing but generalizing across varied specs, tolerances, and surface types is hard. Deciding whether a machine needs adjustment adds reasoning and actuation complexity beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Arrange for the replacement of defective fire fighting equipment and for repair of fire alarm and sprinkler systems, making minor repairs such as servicing fire extinguishers when feasible.
human_time_rationale: One instance = identifying defective equipment, arranging replacement/repair coordination, and performing a minor repair (e.g., servicing one fire extinguisher). A competent technician typically takes ~30 min per unit/visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-step coordination (phone/email, vendor liaison), fine dexterous servicing of extinguishers (pressure checks, pin/seal replacement), navigating varied facilities, and judgment calls on defect severity — far beyond 2026 robot autonomy.

task: Conduct fire exit drills to monitor and evaluate evacuation procedures.
human_time_rationale: One drill cycle: pre-drill briefing, alarm activation, monitoring all exits, timing occupants, post-drill debrief and report. Typical mid-size building takes ~45–75 minutes end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-floor navigation, crowd monitoring, judgment on compliance/bottlenecks, communication with occupants, triggering systems, and writing evaluative reports — far beyond current autonomous robot capabilities in unstructured social/safety contexts.

task: Block or rope off scene and check perimeter to ensure that entire scene is secured.
human_time_rationale: One instance = securing a single scene perimeter; placing barriers/rope, walking the perimeter, and confirming all access points are blocked typically takes ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires carrying and deploying physical barriers/rope, reasoning about dynamic scene boundaries, decision-making on access points, and communication with personnel. Manipulation of rope/barriers plus situational judgment far exceeds current humanoid generalization and reliability.

task: Take pictures of individuals, families, and small groups, either in studio or on location.
human_time_rationale: One portrait session (individual, family, or small group). Includes setup, directing subjects, and shooting. Typical portrait session runs 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social/verbal direction of humans, aesthetic composition judgment, rapport-building, and camera operation in varied environments. Current robots lack natural language subject direction, social presence, and creative framing judgment needed for acceptable portrait quality.

task: Prepare cash receipts after establishments close, and make bank deposits.
human_time_rationale: One instance = counting/reconciling one day's cash receipts and preparing/transporting one bank deposit. Typically 20–40 min for a competent cashier/manager.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous currency handling, counting bills/coins, filling out deposit slips, navigating to a bank, and operating a deposit terminal — multi-step, multi-location task far beyond current autonomous robot capability.

task: Inspect equipment or monitor operating conditions, meters, and gauges to determine load requirements and detect malfunctions.
human_time_rationale: One inspection round of a typical equipment station: visually checking meters, gauges, and operating conditions, noting readings, and flagging anomalies — roughly 10–20 minutes per round.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can navigate to equipment and read clear digital displays, but analog gauges, varied meter layouts, dim/cluttered environments, and nuanced malfunction interpretation challenge current perception and generalization. Autonomous fault diagnosis remains weak.

task: Drive vehicles or patrol specific areas to detect law violators, issue citations, and make arrests.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patrol shift (~8 hours); officer drives/patrols, monitors for violations, issues citations, and makes arrests throughout the shift.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving in traffic (humanoids cannot), nuanced legal judgment, physical arrests, and public interaction. Far beyond current robot autonomy in dexterity, social reasoning, mobility, and legal authority.

task: Check victims for signs of life, such as breathing and pulse.
human_time_rationale: One instance = checking one victim. A competent responder takes ~1–3 minutes to assess breathing (look/listen/feel) and pulse (carotid/radial check).
robot_success_rationale: Requires subtle tactile sensing (pulse detection), nuanced visual/auditory perception of faint breathing, and safe physical contact on a human body in unstructured emergency settings — all well beyond current humanoid reliability. No deployed system handles this robustly.

task: Use knives, cleavers, meat saws, bandsaws, or other equipment to perform meat cutting and trimming.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cutting/trimming a single primal or sub-primal cut. A skilled butcher typically spends 10–20 minutes per piece depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise force-controlled cutting through irregular, deformable meat with sharp blades, switching between tools (saws, cleavers, knives), navigating bone and sinew — far beyond current robot dexterity and safety limits.

task: Monitor, note, report, and investigate suspicious persons and situations, safety hazards, and unusual or illegal activity in patrol area.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patrol shift cycle or patrol loop (~1 hour), during which a guard monitors, notes, and reports any incidents encountered.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social judgment to identify "suspicious" behavior, context-aware threat assessment, dynamic outdoor/indoor mobility, and autonomous incident reporting — all far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities. Perception and reasoning gaps are severe.

task: Patrol specific area on foot, horseback, or motorized conveyance, responding promptly to calls for assistance.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patrol shift (~8 hours). Security/law enforcement patrols are typically measured per shift, covering a designated area continuously.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating unpredictable outdoor/indoor terrain, operating vehicles or horses, real-time situational judgment, responding to emergencies, and engaging with the public — far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities. Horseback and motorized conveyance operation are essentially impossible autonomously.

task: Start machines, monitor operation, and make adjustments as needed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = starting a machine, monitoring a typical operational cycle, and making one round of adjustments. A competent operator typically completes this in ~30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can press buttons and read displays, but interpreting machine state, diagnosing anomalies, and making correct adjustments across varied industrial machines requires generalization, contextual judgment, and tool-use well beyond current frontier capability.

task: Photograph or draw diagrams of crime or accident scenes and interview principals and eyewitnesses.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one scene visit including photography/diagramming and witness interviews. A competent investigator typically spends 60–120 minutes on-site for a typical crime/accident scene.
robot_success_rationale: Requires natural-language interviewing, social cognition, contextual judgment, operating cameras/sketching tools, navigating chaotic outdoor scenes, and earning witness trust — far beyond current humanoid capabilities. No viable autonomous system exists for this composite task.

task: Fabricate patterns or templates to guide craft production.
human_time_rationale: One instance = creating one pattern/template (e.g., sewing or woodworking template). A competent craftsperson spends ~30–60 min measuring, drafting, cutting, and verifying a single pattern.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative design judgment, precise fine-motor drafting/cutting of irregular shapes, material handling of flimsy media (paper/card), and domain-specific craft knowledge. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity.

task: Position mold along guidelines of wall, press mold in place, and remove mold and paper from wall.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning, pressing, and removing one mold/paper section on a wall. A skilled worker aligns, presses, and peels in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise vertical/horizontal alignment to guidelines, sustained bimanual pressing force across a surface, and careful peeling without tearing — demanding fine force control, spatial accuracy, and generalization that current humanoids lack reliably.

task: Provide for public safety by maintaining order, responding to emergencies, protecting people and property, enforcing motor vehicle and criminal laws, and promoting good community relations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full patrol shift (~8 hours). A competent officer maintains order, responds to emergencies, enforces laws, and engages the community throughout a standard shift.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal authority, social/emotional judgment, de-escalation, vehicle operation, use-of-force decisions, and dynamic community interaction — far beyond any 2026 general-purpose robot's autonomy, dexterity, or social cognition capabilities.

task: Search for and collect evidence, such as fingerprints, using investigative equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single crime scene or evidence area. A competent forensic investigator typically spends 30–60 minutes searching, collecting, and packaging evidence from one scene.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous tool use (brushes, swabs, lifting tape), latent print detection under varied lighting, unstructured environments, chain-of-custody handling, and expert judgment — all far beyond current humanoid robot capability.

task: Install booths, exhibits, displays, carpets, or drapes, as guided by floor plan of building or specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a single booth or exhibit unit. A competent installer typically takes 1–2 hours per booth, including carpet laying, display assembly, and drape hanging per floor plan.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading floor plans, navigating cluttered convention spaces, handling heavy/bulky modular panels, laying carpet, hanging drapes, and precise spatial placement — far beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity limits in unstructured environments.

task: Stop machines when specified amounts of product have been produced.
human_time_rationale: One instance = stopping a machine when a production quota is reached. Worker monitors output, recognizes the threshold, and actuates a stop control — typically takes 1–3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robot must read/interpret production counters (vision/sensor), navigate to machine, and actuate a stop control (button/switch). Counter reading and button-pressing are feasible but generalization across varied machines and control panels lowers reliability.

task: Tend machines with multiple winding units that wind thread onto shuttle bobbins for use on sewing machines or other kinds of bobbins for sole-stitching, knitting, or weaving machinery.
human_time_rationale: One instance = attending one winding cycle: loading thread/bobbins, monitoring tension, clearing jams, and unloading finished bobbins across a multi-unit machine. ~8 min per cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine thread handling, threading small eyelets, detecting tension anomalies, and managing multiple machine units simultaneously. Current robots lack the dexterity for thin thread manipulation and real-time fault sensing needed here.

task: Assist children with disabilities or children with psychological, emotional, or behavioral issues with boarding and exiting the school bus.
human_time_rationale: One instance = helping one child board or exit the bus. A competent aide typically spends 3–12 minutes per child depending on mobility aids, behavioral needs, and safety checks.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic physical support, behavioral sensitivity, unpredictable child movements, emotional reassurance, and safe navigation of bus steps with mobility aids. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and safe caregiving capability.

task: Select set props, such as furniture, pictures, lamps, and rugs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting all props for a single set/scene. A competent set decorator reviews script, sources, and curates furniture, art, lamps, rugs, etc. — typically 1–2 hours per set.
robot_success_rationale: Task is primarily cognitive/creative: interpreting script tone, period, character, and aesthetic to select props. Requires taste, domain knowledge, vendor navigation, and judgment — far beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Repair or replace worn or defective needles and other components, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = replacing/repairing one worn component (e.g. a needle on an industrial sewing/knitting machine). A skilled technician typically takes 10–20 minutes including diagnosis and reassembly.
robot_success_rationale: Needles and small machine components demand extreme fine dexterity, precise force control, and tool use in tight spaces. Current humanoids lack the finger precision and situational judgment required for reliable autonomous repair of such small, fragile parts.

task: Cut out designs on cardboard, hardboard, or plywood, according to motif of event.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cutting a single design piece. Involves marking, securing material, and cutting with a jigsaw/scroll saw. A competent human takes ~45 min per piece depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating power cutting tools (jigsaw, scroll saw) with high precision along complex curves, securing workpieces, and interpreting a creative motif. Current humanoids lack the tool-use dexterity, force control, and task planning needed for safe, accurate cutting of rigid sheet materials.

task: Examine looms to determine causes of loom stoppage, such as warp filling, harness breaks, or mechanical defects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = diagnosing cause of one loom stoppage. An experienced weaver inspects, identifies the fault (broken thread, harness, mechanical), and reaches a diagnosis in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine visual perception to identify broken threads or harness faults among dense, visually complex textile machinery, precise tactile inspection, and mechanical fault reasoning — all well beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured industrial textile environments.

task: Patrol and guard courthouses, grand jury rooms, or assigned areas to provide security, enforce laws, maintain order, and arrest violators.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full security patrol shift (8 hours) covering assigned courthouse/grand jury areas, the standard unit a supervisor would measure.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social authority, legal judgment, dynamic crowd interaction, arrest capability, and adaptive threat response. Current humanoids lack law-enforcement authority, nuanced situational judgment, and safe restraint of non-compliant persons.

task: Operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and to obtain product samples.
human_time_rationale: One instance = running a test cycle on a machine, collecting samples, and verifying adjustments. Typically takes 15–30 minutes for setup, run, and sample retrieval.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating highly varied, specialized industrial machines with complex controls, interpreting results, and making judgment calls about sample quality. Current humanoids lack machine-specific tool-use, domain knowledge, and reliable generalization across industrial equipment.

task: Maintain, repair, and lubricate equipment, using hand tools and power tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single maintenance/repair/lubrication job on one piece of equipment. A competent technician typically spends ~45 min per job including diagnosis, tool use, and lubrication.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diagnosing faults, selecting correct tools, applying precise torque, navigating tight spaces, and applying lubricant accurately. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-switching, fault diagnosis, and fine dexterous manipulation for unstructured repair tasks.

task: Install, level, and align components, such as gears, chains, guides, dies, cutters, or needles, to set up machinery for operation.
human_time_rationale: One setup instance on a single machine; a skilled technician typically spends 30–60 min installing, leveling, and aligning multiple precision components.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity for precision alignment, use of specialized tools (dial indicators, torque wrenches), force-feedback judgment, and task-specific knowledge of machinery tolerances — well beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Place bobbins on spindles and insert spindles into bobbin-winding machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = placing one bobbin on a spindle and inserting it into the machine. A trained textile worker completes this in ~1–3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise alignment of small cylindrical parts onto spindles and correct insertion into a machine slot. Dexterity and fine positional accuracy are challenging for current humanoids; small-parts handling and tight-tolerance insertion reduce reliability significantly.

task: Execute arrest warrants, locating and taking persons into custody.
human_time_rationale: One instance = locating and apprehending one subject. Includes travel, surveillance, approach, and custody procedures. Median ~90 min for a typical warrant execution.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal authority, complex social/threat assessment, unpredictable human resistance, outdoor/indoor navigation, restraint application, and high-stakes judgment calls far beyond any current humanoid robot's capabilities.

task: Adjust machine heating mechanisms, tensions, and speeds to produce specified products.
human_time_rationale: One instance = adjusting heating, tension, and speed settings on a single machine for a product changeover or setup; a skilled operator typically takes 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading machine-specific dials/panels, fine-tuning analog or digital controls, interpreting product specs, and verifying output quality. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation of varied industrial controls and contextual judgment for multi-parameter tuning.

task: Test equipment prior to use to ensure that it is in good working order.
human_time_rationale: One instance = pre-use check of a single piece of equipment (e.g., power tool, meter, or machine). A competent worker typically takes 5–15 minutes for a standard functional check.
robot_success_rationale: Requires recognizing equipment type, powering on, interpreting indicators/displays, and judging pass/fail—demanding perception, generalization across device types, and contextual reasoning. Current robots lack reliable multi-device testing workflows and result interpretation.

task: Thread yarn, thread, or fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = threading a single machine (e.g., industrial sewing or knitting machine). A skilled textile worker typically takes 3–7 minutes per machine.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity to guide thin yarn/thread through tiny needle eyes, tight guides, and rollers in precise sequence. Current humanoids lack the finger precision, tactile feedback, and visual acuity needed for reliable success.

task: Design and produce displays and materials that can be used to decorate windows, interior displays, or event locations, such as streets and fairgrounds.
human_time_rationale: One instance = designing and producing one display/decorative material set. A competent visual merchandiser or display artist typically spends 1–3 hours conceiving, constructing, and assembling a single display piece or themed material set.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative design judgment, aesthetic decision-making, fine craft manipulation (cutting, painting, gluing, draping), and adaptation to varied materials and contexts. Far beyond current robot generalization, dexterity, and creative autonomy.

task: Render aid to accident survivors and other persons requiring first aid for physical injuries.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assessing and treating one injured person on-scene (e.g., bandaging, stabilizing, CPR) until handoff to EMS; typical first-aid episode is 10–30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic unstructured environments, nuanced injury assessment, adaptive dexterity (bandaging, CPR), and real-time clinical judgment. Current humanoids lack medical perception, safe force control on injured bodies, and emergency generalization.

task: Transport or escort prisoners and defendants en route to courtrooms, prisons or jails, attorneys' offices, or medical facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one prisoner transport trip (e.g., facility to courthouse). Median trip including loading, transit, and handoff is roughly 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving vehicles in traffic, managing restrained individuals, navigating secure facilities, and legal authority/judgment. No humanoid robot can operate transport vehicles or safely escort combative detainees autonomously in 2026.

task: Measure bobbins periodically, using gauges, and turn screws to adjust tension if bobbins are not of specified size.
human_time_rationale: One instance = measuring a single bobbin with a gauge and adjusting tension screw if needed. A skilled worker completes this check-and-adjust cycle in ~3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous gauge handling, precise reading of measurement instruments, and delicate screw micro-adjustments. Current humanoids lack the fine motor precision and feedback loops needed for reliable gauge use and tension calibration.

task: Design and build scale models of set designs, or miniature sets used in filming backgrounds or special effects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one complete miniature set model. A competent model maker typically spends 4–12 hours depending on complexity; 8 hours is a reasonable median for a moderately detailed film miniature.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (cutting, sculpting, painting tiny details), creative design interpretation, multi-material craftsmanship, and aesthetic judgment. Far beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capabilities in unstructured creative fabrication.

task: Locate and confiscate real or personal property, as directed by court order.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one court-ordered property seizure. Includes travel to location, identifying/verifying property, paperwork, and physical confiscation. Median ~90 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal authority, judgment, human interaction, negotiation, situational awareness in unpredictable environments, vehicle operation, and complex paperwork. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Clean and maintain fire stations and fire fighting equipment and apparatus.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a routine daily cleaning and equipment check of a fire station and its apparatus; a competent crew typically completes this in ~90 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Task spans vastly different sub-tasks: mopping floors, washing large vehicles, inspecting/testing specialized hoses, SCBA gear, and pumps. Robots lack the reach, tool-use, domain-specific inspection judgment, and outdoor/vehicle-washing capability needed across all sub-tasks.

task: Employ a variety of specialized photographic materials and techniques, including infrared and ultraviolet films, macro photography, photogrammetry and sensitometry.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a single specialized photographic session (e.g., one IR/UV shoot or macro/photogrammetry capture set). A competent photographer takes ~45 min including setup, calibration, and capture.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert selection of films/filters, precise manual camera adjustments, sensitometry calibration, and creative compositional judgment. Current robots lack the nuanced perception, specialized tool-use, and domain expertise these techniques demand.

task: Inspect products to verify that they meet specifications and to determine whether machine adjustment is needed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single product/batch at a workstation. A competent worker visually checks, measures, and judges conformance in ~2–5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Vision-based defect detection is mature, but combining perception, measurement tool use, judgment on spec conformance, AND deciding on machine adjustment requires multi-step generalization. Current humanoids lack reliable fine-measurement tool handling and contextual machine-adjustment decisions.

task: Perform maintenance tasks necessary to keep equipment working properly.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one routine maintenance session on a single piece of equipment (e.g., lubricating, replacing filters, tightening fasteners). Typical skilled technician takes ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Maintenance is highly varied: diagnosis, tool use, tight-space access, torque control, fluid handling, and judgment. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use generalization, fine-force feedback, and contextual fault reasoning needed for autonomous success.

task: Tend spinning frames that draw out and twist roving or sliver into yarn.
human_time_rationale: One instance = tending a set of spinning frames for one monitoring/intervention cycle (~30 min), including piecing broken ends, doffing full bobbins, and watching for faults.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous piecing of broken yarn ends, rapid traversal along long frame rows, and detecting subtle thread breaks — well beyond current robot dexterity and perception reliability in fast, unstructured textile environments.

task: Select and assemble equipment and required background properties, according to subjects, materials, and conditions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting and assembling equipment/props for a single shoot setup. A competent photographer/technician takes ~20–30 min to gather, arrange, and verify all items.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-level semantic reasoning to match equipment to subject/conditions, navigating varied storage spaces, and making aesthetic/contextual judgments. Far beyond current robot generalization and planning capability.

task: Replace depleted supply packages with full packages.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing one depleted package and placing a full replacement in its location. A competent worker completes this in ~5 minutes including retrieval.
robot_success_rationale: Requires locating depleted vs. full packages (perception), navigating to storage, grasping and carrying packages of varying sizes/weights, and precisely placing them. Variability in package types and environments limits reliability for current humanoids.

task: Collect and test water and sewage samples, using test equipment and color analysis standards.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting samples from a site and running standard lab/field tests. A trained technician typically spends 30–60 min per sampling event including collection, testing, and logging.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating to varied outdoor/industrial sites, manipulating valves/sampling ports, handling liquids without spills, operating test equipment, and interpreting color standards — far beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterous tool-use capabilities.

task: Enhance, retouch, and resize photographs and negatives, using airbrushing and other techniques.
human_time_rationale: One instance = retouching and resizing a single photograph. A skilled retoucher spends ~30–60 min on moderate enhancement work including airbrushing, color correction, and resizing.
robot_success_rationale: This is primarily a skilled digital/cognitive task requiring fine visual judgment, software proficiency, and artistic decision-making. Physical robots have no meaningful pathway to operate photo-editing software or airbrush tools autonomously at acceptable quality.

task: Tend machines that twist together two or more strands of yarn or insert additional twists into single strands of yarn to increase strength, smoothness, or uniformity of yarn.
human_time_rationale: One instance = monitoring and tending a twisting/plying machine through one production cycle (~30 min), including loading yarn bobbins, checking tension, and clearing thread breaks.
robot_success_rationale: Requires delicate dexterous manipulation of fine yarn threads, threading through guides, detecting and repairing breaks, and adjusting tension settings — all highly fine-motor and tactile tasks far beyond current humanoid capability in an industrial textile setting.

task: Remove spindles from machines and bobbins from spindles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing one spindle from a machine and its bobbin from the spindle. A skilled textile worker can do this in ~2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise grasping of narrow cylindrical spindles and small bobbins, often with tight tolerances and varied orientations. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity and reliable force control for consistent success in textile machinery contexts.

task: Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single machine. A competent technician spends ~20 min checking mechanical, visual, and auditory cues to assess repair needs.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can detect obvious visual anomalies but lack nuanced multi-modal diagnosis (sound, vibration, smell, tactile feel). Generalization across diverse machinery types, unexpected faults, and contextual judgment remain major limitations.

task: Evacuate students from the school bus in emergency situations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = evacuating all students from one bus in an emergency. A trained driver/aide guides ~30–50 students off in roughly 5–10 minutes under pressure.
robot_success_rationale: Requires emergency vehicle access, operating bus emergency exits, crowd management of panicked children, physical assistance for mobility-impaired students, outdoor/chaotic terrain, and real-time social communication — all beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Escort young children across roads or highways.
human_time_rationale: One instance = safely walking a group of young children across one road or highway crossing, including waiting for safe gap/signal and shepherding children to the other side.
robot_success_rationale: Requires real-time traffic perception, reliable child behavior management, physical intervention capability, and legal/safety accountability. Current humanoids lack the trust, dynamic crowd control, and guaranteed fail-safe responses needed for child safety in live traffic.

task: Set up, mount, or install photographic equipment and cameras.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a camera with tripod/mount, attaching accessories, and positioning for a shoot. A competent photographer takes ~20–30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous manipulation (thread-mounting cameras onto tripods, attaching lenses, tightening knobs), precise spatial positioning, and judgment about framing. Current humanoids lack reliable fine-thread fastening and tool-specific dexterity for varied camera hardware.

task: Unwind lengths of yarn, thread, or twine from spools and wind onto bobbins.
human_time_rationale: One instance = unwinding and rewinding one spool onto one bobbin. A competent worker threads and winds a typical spool in roughly 5–10 minutes depending on length and material.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise fine-motor threading of thin filament, consistent tension control during winding, and tool/machinery operation (winding machine). Current humanoids lack the dexterity and closed-loop tension sensing needed reliably.

task: Administer general and local anesthetics.
human_time_rationale: One instance = administering anesthesia to one patient for one procedure, including assessment, induction, and initial monitoring. Typical induction and setup takes ~20–40 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sterile IV placement, precise drug titration, continuous patient assessment, airway management (intubation), real-time clinical judgment, and legal/medical licensure. Far beyond any current robot's capabilities — safety-critical and highly unstructured.

task: Prepare hot, cold, and mixed drinks for patrons, and chill bottles of wine.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one drink order (1–3 drinks) for a patron. A skilled bartender/server completes this in ~3–5 minutes including chilling wine if needed.
robot_success_rationale: Bartending requires fine dexterity (pouring, mixing, shaking), navigating unstructured bar environments, interpreting varied orders, operating multiple appliances, and social interaction. Current humanoids lack reliability for this complex, multi-step, fluid-handling task.

task: Clean, oil, and lubricate machines, using air hoses, cleaning solutions, rags, oilcans, and grease guns.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning, oiling, and lubricating a single industrial machine. A competent worker typically takes 20–40 minutes depending on machine size and access points.
robot_success_rationale: Requires coordinated multi-tool use (air hose, oilcan, grease gun, rags) with precise nozzle insertion into tight lubrication ports. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, force control, and tool-switching needed for reliable autonomous completion on varied industrial machines.

task: Mount, frame, laminate, or lacquer finished photographs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = mounting/framing/laminating one photograph. A skilled photo lab worker takes ~15–25 min depending on method (cutting mat, aligning, sealing, framing).
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise alignment of thin, flexible photo paper, fine dexterity for matting/framing, tool use (cutters, squeegees, frame clips), and quality judgment. Far beyond reliable 2026 humanoid capability.

task: Load materials into extruding and forming machines, using hand tools, and adjust feed mechanisms to set feed rates.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading a batch of material into an extruding/forming machine and adjusting feed rate settings; a competent operator typically takes 10–20 minutes per setup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires heavy/bulk material handling, precise placement into industrial machine hoppers/feedways, and fine mechanical adjustment of feed dials/levers. Current humanoids lack the force control, specialized tool-use, and industrial machine generalization needed reliably.

task: Install, level, and align machine components such as gears, chains, guides, dies, cutters, or needles to set up machinery for operation.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a single machine with its components (gears, dies, etc.). A skilled machinist typically takes 30–60 min per setup depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precision leveling/alignment to tight tolerances, tool-switching, force feedback interpretation, and reading machine specs. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, calibrated torque sensing, and domain generalization for multi-component machine setup.

task: Load and unload film.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading or unloading a roll of film into/from a camera. A competent photographer takes ~1–5 min depending on camera type.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extremely fine dexterity: threading thin film leader onto a spool in dim/dark conditions, precisely aligning sprocket holes, handling delicate light-sensitive material. Well beyond current humanoid manipulation capability.

task: Examine objects to be included in exhibits to plan where and how to display them.
human_time_rationale: One instance = examining all objects for one exhibit plan. A curator spends ~30–60 min assessing condition, dimensions, and spatial/lighting needs for the collection.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-level aesthetic judgment, contextual knowledge of exhibit themes, fragile object handling, and complex spatial planning. Current robots lack curatorial reasoning, nuanced perception of artistic/historical significance, and the generalization needed for autonomous exhibit design decisions.

task: Produce computer-readable, digital images from film, using flatbed scanners and photofinishing laboratories.
human_time_rationale: One instance = scanning one roll/batch of film. A competent human loads film onto a flatbed scanner, sets parameters, scans, and saves digital files in ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise film handling (fragile, thin negatives), interacting with scanner software GUIs, and fine dexterous loading into film holders. Current humanoids lack the fine manipulation and software-interaction capability needed for reliable autonomous operation.

task: Treat problems affecting the oral mucosa, such as mouth ulcers and infections.
human_time_rationale: One instance = examining and treating a single patient's oral mucosal condition (assessment, diagnosis, and applying/prescribing treatment). A competent clinician typically takes 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical diagnosis, differential reasoning, fine intraoral dexterity, sterile technique, and patient communication. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in perception, fine motor skill, and autonomous medical decision-making.

task: Scrub and polish counters, steam tables, and other equipment, and clean glasses, dishes, and fountain equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full cleaning cycle of a typical counter/steam table station plus associated dishes and fountain equipment; a competent worker completes this in ~30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can wipe flat surfaces but struggle with varied equipment shapes, steam table inserts, polishing, delicate glassware handling, reaching fountain nozzles, and judging cleanliness quality across diverse, cluttered items.

task: Perform surgery to prepare the mouth for dental implants and to aid in the regeneration of deficient bone and gum tissues.
human_time_rationale: One surgical session for implant site prep and bone/gum tissue regeneration; a competent oral surgeon typically spends 1.5–2.5 hours per case.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity in a confined, fluid-filled oral cavity, real-time tactile/force feedback, sterile technique, and complex clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability; even surgical robots need expert teleoperation.

task: Start metering pumps and observe operation of machines and equipment to ensure continuous flow of filaments extruded through spinnerettes and to detect processing defects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = startup sequence plus initial observation period for one extrusion line; a skilled operator typically takes ~10–20 min to start pumps, confirm flow, and scan for defects.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine manipulation of industrial pump controls, sustained visual inspection of hair-thin filaments through spinnerettes, anomaly detection of subtle defects, and real-time process judgment — all well beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Operate a wheelchair lift to load or unload wheelchairs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading or unloading one wheelchair user via a lift. Includes positioning, securing, operating controls, and confirming safe transit. Typically 3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating vehicle-mounted mechanical lift controls, safely positioning and securing a wheelchair and occupant, and coordinating with a human passenger — far beyond current humanoid dexterity, caregiving, and safety reliability in dynamic vehicle contexts.

task: Clean school bus interiors by picking up waste, wiping down windows, or vacuuming.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one school bus interior (trash pickup, windows, vacuuming). A competent cleaner typically finishes in 20–30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Bus interior is a cramped, cluttered, narrow-aisle environment. Robot must navigate tight rows, handle a vacuum/wiper tool, reach overhead windows, and pick varied debris — far beyond current humanoid dexterity and confined-space mobility.

task: Monitor for trains at railroad crossings and signal the bus driver when it is safe to proceed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one railroad crossing check: stop, visually scan both directions, confirm no train, then signal driver. Typically takes ~1–3 minutes per crossing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reliable long-range perception of oncoming trains, outdoor mobility, and signaling to a driver. Current humanoids lack robust outdoor terrain navigation, reliable far-distance threat detection, and safe vehicle-adjacent operation. Cannot drive or board the bus.

task: Treat infections of the oral cavity, salivary glands, jaws, and neck.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient encounter: examination, diagnosis, and treatment planning/initiation (e.g., prescribing antibiotics, incision & drainage). Typical clinical visit for oral/neck infection.
robot_success_rationale: Requires licensed clinical judgment, sterile intraoral/neck procedures, fine dexterous surgical skill, and diagnostic reasoning. Far beyond any current humanoid robot's autonomous medical capability.

task: Open and close school bus doors for students.
human_time_rationale: One instance = opening + closing the bus door for one stop. A driver pulls a lever or pushes a button; the full cycle takes ~15 seconds.
robot_success_rationale: Task is physically simple but requires the robot to be seated in the driver's seat, operating vehicle-specific door controls, while also monitoring student safety. Humanoid robots cannot operate buses autonomously in traffic; accessing and actuating bus door levers/buttons in situ is extremely difficult without dedicated integration.

task: Operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and to obtain product samples.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one test run: configuring machine, initiating run, monitoring output, and collecting a product sample for review. Typical cycle is 15–30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating diverse industrial machines via controls/interfaces, interpreting run quality, collecting samples, and making judgment calls — far beyond current humanoid generalization, dexterity, and machine-interface capabilities.

task: Perform cleaning duties, such as sweeping, mopping, and washing dishes, to keep equipment and facilities sanitary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full cleaning round (sweeping + mopping a typical room/area + washing a batch of dishes); a competent worker completes this in ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Sweeping/mopping flat surfaces is feasible (~50–70%), but washing dishes requires fine dexterous manipulation under water, tool switching, and generalization across cluttered sinks — dragging the combined success probability down sharply.

task: Create artificial light, using flashes and reflectors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a flash/reflector lighting configuration for a single shoot setup. A competent photographer takes ~15 min to position, test-fire, and adjust units.
robot_success_rationale: Requires aesthetic judgment of light quality, precise angling of reflectors, adjusting flash power by reading visual output, and operating specialized studio equipment controls — well beyond current robot generalization and perceptual feedback capabilities.

task: Remove tumors and other abnormal growths of the oral and facial regions, using surgical instruments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one surgical tumor removal procedure. A typical oral/maxillofacial tumor excision takes 1–3 hours; 120 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, sterile surgical technique, real-time tissue differentiation, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid capabilities; even surgical robots (e.g., Da Vinci) require full human control and are not autonomous.

task: Evaluate the position of the wisdom teeth to determine whether problems exist currently or might occur in the future.
human_time_rationale: One instance = evaluating all four wisdom teeth for one patient, including reviewing X-rays and clinical exam. A competent dentist/oral surgeon typically completes this in ~10 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires interpreting dental radiographs, performing intraoral clinical examination, and applying clinical judgment about impaction/eruption risk. Current robots lack medical imaging interpretation, fine intraoral access, and diagnostic reasoning for this specialized task.

task: Buckle seatbelts or fasten wheelchair tie-down straps to secure passengers for transportation.
human_time_rationale: One instance = securing one passenger (seatbelt or wheelchair tie-downs). A competent worker fastens belts/straps in ~1–3 minutes per passenger.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to locate and click buckles or tension tie-down straps on varied equipment, navigate tight vehicle interiors, and interact safely around a human passenger. Current humanoids lack the precision and situational awareness for reliable autonomous execution in this confined, safety-critical context.

task: Set up, operate, or tend machines that extrude and form filaments from synthetic materials such as rayon, fiberglass, or liquid polymers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and running one production batch/shift cycle on an extrusion machine; a competent operator typically spends ~45 min on setup, calibration, and initial tending before steady-state monitoring begins.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating large industrial extrusion machinery with precise controls, adjusting spinnerets, monitoring viscosity/temperature, and responding to process faults. No current humanoid robot can operate or tend such specialized industrial equipment autonomously at acceptable quality.

task: Serve customers in eating places that specialize in fast service and inexpensive carry-out food.
human_time_rationale: One instance = serving one customer: taking order, handling payment, and handing over food. A fast-food transaction typically takes 2–4 minutes end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires natural language interaction, cash/card handling, tray assembly, and adapting to unpredictable customers. Current humanoids lack robust spoken dialogue, POS integration, and reliable multi-step manipulation in chaotic public environments.

task: Repair or replace worn or defective parts or components, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = diagnosing and replacing/repairing a single worn part (e.g. a bolt, gasket, or switch) using hand tools; median across simple and moderately complex repairs.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diagnosing defects, selecting correct tools, applying varied torque/force with precision, and adapting to unpredictable part geometries. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use dexterity and fault-diagnosis autonomy for unstructured repairs.

task: Fit, test, and evaluate devices on patients, and make adjustments for proper fit, function, and comfort.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fitting a single device (e.g., orthotic, prosthetic, hearing aid) on one patient, including testing and adjustments; typically 20–45 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity, nuanced patient communication, clinical judgment, dynamic adjustment to patient feedback, and safe physical interaction with a human body — well beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Remove impacted, damaged, and non-restorable teeth.
human_time_rationale: One instance = extraction of a single tooth (including prep, extraction, and post-op care). Impacted teeth (e.g. wisdom teeth) average 20–60 min; simple extractions ~15 min; blended median ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Dental extraction demands extreme fine dexterity, force modulation, real-time tactile feedback inside a small oral cavity, and sterile surgical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability; even research surgical robots cannot do this autonomously.

task: Serve food, beverages, or desserts to customers in such settings as take-out counters of restaurants or lunchrooms, business or industrial establishments, hotel rooms, and cars.
human_time_rationale: One instance = serving one customer order (take-out counter, lunchroom, etc.). Competent worker takes ~2–6 min: greeting, retrieving/packaging items, handing off, processing payment.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating varied environments (hotel rooms, cars, counters), handling diverse containers, social interaction, and payment processing. Perception and dexterity for unstructured settings remain weak; car/room delivery adds mobility/generalization challenges.

task: Brew coffee and tea, and fill containers with requested beverages.
human_time_rationale: One instance = brewing a pot/batch of coffee or tea and filling requested containers. Includes setup, brewing (~5–7 min), and pouring. Median ~8 min total.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multiple fine-dexterous steps: handling filters, measuring grounds/leaves, operating a brewer, pouring without spilling into varied containers. Generalization across appliance types and container sizes is very challenging for current humanoids.

task: Wash dishes, glassware, and silverware after meals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = washing dishes/glassware/silverware after a typical meal for ~2–4 people; includes scrubbing, rinsing, and setting aside to dry.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous handling of fragile, slippery, varied-shaped items under water; grasping delicate glassware without breaking, scrubbing effectively, and managing a wet/cluttered sink environment far exceed current humanoid capabilities reliably.

task: Provide emergency treatment of facial injuries including facial lacerations, intra-oral lacerations, and fractured facial bones.
human_time_rationale: One instance = treating a single patient's facial injury (laceration, intra-oral laceration, or fracture stabilization) in an emergency setting; 30–60 min is typical for assessment, irrigation, suturing, and splinting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires surgical-grade fine dexterity, sterile technique, intra-oral access, real-time clinical judgment, and patient interaction. Far beyond current humanoid capabilities; even specialized surgical robots cannot generalize to unstructured emergency facial trauma.

task: Move controls to activate and adjust extruding and forming machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = activating and adjusting controls for a single machine setup or production run adjustment; a trained operator typically takes 2–5 minutes per adjustment cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Industrial control panels involve buttons, dials, levers, and touchscreens requiring precise manipulation and process knowledge. Humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity, domain-specific control-reading, and safe interaction with high-force industrial equipment in unstructured layouts.

task: Open cabinet doors to cut multifilament threadlines away from guides, using scissors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = opening a cabinet, locating threadlines across guides, and cutting them free. A skilled textile worker completes this in ~2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires opening a cabinet door (moderate), then identifying and cutting fine multifilament threadlines from small guides with scissors — extremely fine dexterity, precise perception of thin fibers, and tool-use in a confined space far exceeds 2026 robot capability.

task: Examine, interview, and measure patients to determine their appliance needs and to identify factors that could affect appliance fit.
human_time_rationale: One instance = examining, interviewing, and measuring one patient for appliance fitting. A competent orthotist/prosthetist typically spends 20–40 minutes per patient intake assessment.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced clinical interviewing, physical examination with medical judgment, precise anatomical measurement, and patient interaction. Far beyond current robot capabilities in perception, social cognition, and clinical reasoning.

task: Remove excess, entangled, or completed filaments from machines, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing filaments from one machine. Skilled worker spends ~5–10 min locating, cutting, and removing entangled or completed filaments with hand tools.
robot_success_rationale: Filament removal demands fine dexterity to grasp and cut thin, flexible, tangled threads in cluttered machine geometry. Perception of fine filaments is difficult; confined spaces and varied entanglement patterns make autonomous tool use highly unreliable for current robots.

task: Perform surgery on the mouth and jaws to treat conditions such as cleft lip, cleft palate, and jaw growth problems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one surgical procedure (e.g., cleft palate repair or jaw osteotomy). Typical oral/maxillofacial surgeries range from 2–6 hours; ~4 hours is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme sub-millimeter dexterity, sterile technique, real-time clinical judgment, haptic feedback, and legal/ethical oversight. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability; even specialized surgical robots need human surgeons to operate them.

task: Perform maintenance and minor repairs on firefighting equipment, including vehicles, and write and submit proposals to modify, replace, and repair equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a scheduled maintenance/minor repair session on a piece of firefighting equipment (e.g., SCBA, hose, pump, or vehicle check). A competent technician typically spends ~90 min per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving/operating heavy fire vehicles, fine dexterous repairs on diverse equipment, and cognitive writing/proposal tasks. Current humanoids cannot operate vehicles, lack specialized tools knowledge, and cannot author regulatory proposals autonomously.

task: Adjust machine settings such as speed or tension to produce products that meet specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = adjusting a machine's settings (e.g., speed/tension dials) until output meets spec; includes reading gauges, making tweaks, and verifying output quality.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading analog/digital gauges, interpreting spec sheets, operating varied machine controls (knobs, panels, levers), and closed-loop quality judgment — beyond reliable frontier robot generalization and perception in unstructured industrial settings.

task: Attach fasteners, grommets, buttons, buckles, ornamental trim, and other accessories to covers or frames, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = attaching a set of accessories (fasteners, grommets, buttons, buckles, trim) to a single cover or frame; a competent worker typically completes this in ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle small, varied hardware (grommets, buttons, buckles) and precise force control with hand tools across non-flat surfaces. Current humanoids struggle with multi-tool fine manipulation and accessory variety.

task: Collect and return dirty dishes to the kitchen for washing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting all dirty dishes from a table/room and returning them to the kitchen sink or counter. A competent adult typically takes 3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick-and-place dishes, but challenges include detecting/grasping varied dirty dishes (residue, stacking, irregular shapes), navigating between rooms, and safely carrying fragile items without dropping. Generalization across cluttered, real-world settings remains limited.

task: Show and explain orthopedic and prosthetic appliances to healthcare workers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single demonstration/presentation session covering a set of orthopedic/prosthetic devices to a group of healthcare workers; typically 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert clinical knowledge, nuanced verbal explanation, answering ad-hoc questions, and reading audience understanding. Current robots lack conversational depth, domain expertise, and credible instructional presence for medical education.

task: Restore form and function by moving skin, bone, nerves, and other tissues from other parts of the body to reconstruct the jaws and face.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single reconstructive jaw/facial surgery. Complex free-flap or tissue-transfer procedures typically run 6–12 hours in the OR; 8 hrs is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, microsurgical anastomosis of vessels/nerves, real-time surgical judgment, and sterile technique far beyond any 2026 general-purpose humanoid's capabilities. Research surgical robots exist but are task-specific, not general-purpose.
