robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measuring, cutting, drilling, and fastening of custom structures to tight tolerances. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, tool-use versatility (saws, drills, clamps), and spatial planning needed for bespoke fixture construction.

task: Select appropriate equipment settings and adjust patient positions to obtain the best sites and angles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning one patient and adjusting equipment settings for a single imaging/procedure setup. A competent technologist typically takes 3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, patient communication, nuanced body repositioning, and fine equipment calibration. Current humanoids lack medical domain knowledge, safe patient-contact dexterity, and ability to interpret anatomical cues for optimal angles.

task: Form and assemble slab cores around patterns, and position wire in mold sections to reinforce molds, using hand tools and glue.
human_time_rationale: One instance = forming and assembling one slab core around a pattern with wire reinforcement and glue. Skilled foundry worker completes one mold in roughly 20–30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise manipulation of irregular, fragile sand/slab cores, fine wire positioning, glue application, and pattern alignment — all in an unstructured foundry environment. Far beyond 2026 humanoid dexterity and task generalization limits.

task: Perform food preparation and serving duties, such as carving meat, preparing flambe dishes, or serving wine and liquor.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single food prep/serving event (e.g., carving a roast, preparing one flambé dish, or pouring and presenting wine for a table). A skilled server/chef completes this in ~10–20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (carving, pouring), open-flame handling, social cues for wine service, and high-precision tableside work in dynamic environments. Far beyond reliable autonomous robot capability in 2026.

task: Adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.
human_time_rationale: One instance = reading a measurement, identifying needed adjustment, and making control/setting changes on one machine to bring dimensions back into tolerance.
robot_success_rationale: Requires interpreting precision measurement data, diagnosing out-of-tolerance causes, and operating machine-specific controls (dials, CNC panels, levers). Generalization across varied machines, fine dexterity, and closed-loop judgment are well beyond 2026 humanoid capability.

task: Clean, check, and maintain sonographic equipment, submitting maintenance requests or performing minor repairs as necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning, inspecting, and logging maintenance for one ultrasound machine/transducer set. A competent sonographer or tech takes ~20–40 min per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle delicate transducers, domain-specific inspection knowledge, reading device diagnostics, submitting electronic requests, and performing minor repairs — all far beyond current humanoid generalization and precision.

task: Prepack bulk medicines, fill bottles with prescribed medications, and type and affix labels.
human_time_rationale: One instance = prepackaging one batch/bottle: counting/measuring bulk medicine, filling a bottle, typing and affixing a label. ~10–20 min for a competent pharmacy tech.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (counting pills, handling small bottles), precise dispensing, reading/typing label data, and affixing adhesive labels accurately. Current humanoids lack the fine manipulation and pharmacy-system integration needed.

task: Inspect and test products to verify conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single product/component using precision instruments or circuit testers; a competent QC technician typically spends 10–20 minutes per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise tool handling (calipers, multimeters, probes), fine dexterity to connect/position instruments, interpreting readings, and adapting to varied product geometries. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation and instrument-use generalization needed.

task: Verify that workpiece reference lines are parallel to the axis of table rotation, using dial indicators mounted in spindles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one workpiece alignment check. Skilled machinist mounts dial indicator in spindle, sweeps table rotation, reads runout, and adjusts; typically 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous tool mounting (dial indicator in spindle), precise micro-adjustments based on analog gauge readings, and coordinated machine operation — far beyond current humanoid capability in unstructured machine shop settings.

task: Install broadcast equipment, troubleshoot equipment problems, and perform maintenance or minor repairs, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a single broadcast equipment unit (e.g., transmitter, rack-mount device), including troubleshooting and minor repair. A competent technician typically takes 60–120 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous hand-tool use, cable routing, connector mating, and diagnostic reasoning on specialized broadcast hardware. Current humanoids lack the precision, domain-specific perception, and tool-handling reliability needed for autonomous success.

task: Fit, dispense, and repair assistive devices, such as hearing aids.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single fitting/dispensing or repair appointment. A competent audiologist or hearing instrument specialist typically spends 45–75 minutes per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-millimeter dexterous manipulation of tiny components, audiological assessment, patient communication, calibration software use, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot dexterity and sensory-diagnostic capability.

task: Sprinkle or spray parting agents onto patterns and mold sections to facilitate removal of patterns from molds.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying parting agent to one pattern or mold section. A skilled worker coats the surface evenly by spray or sprinkle in roughly 2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires holding and aiming a spray/shaker tool, achieving even surface coverage on varied mold geometries, and judging application sufficiency. Generalization to complex mold shapes and tool manipulation limits current humanoids.

task: Provide health care, first aid, immunizations, or assistance in convalescence or rehabilitation in locations such as schools, hospitals, or industry.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient care encounter (e.g., first aid treatment, immunization, or rehab session). Median across visit types is ~20–45 min for a competent nurse/aide.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (injections, wound care), clinical judgment, adaptive patient interaction, and navigation across varied environments. Current humanoids lack the precision, safety certification, and generalization for autonomous clinical care.

task: Operate ovens or furnaces to bake cores or to melt, skim, and flux metal.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one batch/heat cycle (loading, monitoring, skimming/fluxing, unloading). A typical foundry melt or core-bake cycle runs 30–60 min of active operator involvement.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating industrial furnace controls, handling ladles of molten metal, skimming slag, and applying flux—tasks involving extreme heat, heavy/specialized tools, precise judgment, and hazardous materials far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Inspect playing fields to locate any items that could injure players.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single playing field (e.g., a soccer pitch or football field). A competent groundskeeper walks the full surface systematically, taking ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can navigate flat terrain and use cameras for object detection, but reliably identifying hazardous items (glass shards, small debris, uneven turf, sprinkler heads) in varied outdoor lighting and complex ground textures remains well beyond current generalization. False negatives are safety-critical.

task: Administer anesthesia to animals, under the direction of a veterinarian, and monitor animals' responses to anesthetics so that dosages can be adjusted.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one surgical anesthetic event per animal, from induction through monitoring to recovery. A competent vet tech typically spends ~30–60 minutes on this per procedure.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise drug calculation, IV/mask administration, continuous physiological monitoring, real-time dosage judgment, and communication with a vet. Far beyond current robot dexterity, medical sensing integration, and autonomous clinical decision-making.

task: Plan, purchase, prepare, or serve meals to patients or other family members, according to prescribed diets.
human_time_rationale: One instance = planning and preparing one prescribed meal per person, including light shopping, cooking ~30 min, and serving.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diet-specific planning, grocery shopping (vehicle/navigation), cooking from a recipe, and caregiving delivery. Each sub-task is hard for current humanoids; combined end-to-end autonomous execution is beyond frontier capability.

task: Monitor patients' progress and provide ongoing observation of hearing or balance status.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient monitoring/observation session, including review of test results, charting, and clinical assessment. Typically 20–40 minutes per visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, interpretation of audiological/vestibular test data, patient communication, and professional licensure. Current robots lack medical diagnostic cognition, specialized audiometric tool use, and the clinical reasoning needed for hearing/balance assessment.

task: Prepare feed for animals according to specific instructions, such as diet lists or schedules.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single feeding batch for a group of animals per a diet schedule; includes gathering, measuring, mixing, and portioning feed items.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading/interpreting diet lists, navigating varied farm/zoo environments, measuring and mixing multiple feed types, and portioning correctly. Current robots lack reliable generalization across unstructured feed prep environments and multi-step recipe following.

task: Inspect and clean food preparation areas, such as equipment, work surfaces, and serving areas, to ensure safe and sanitary food-handling practices.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and cleaning a full prep station (equipment, surfaces, serving area). A competent food-service worker typically completes this in ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can wipe flat surfaces at ~40–70%, but food prep areas require nuanced inspection (detecting residue, grease, contaminants), disassembling/reassembling equipment, and meeting sanitation standards—well beyond current general-purpose robot capability.

task: Start conveyors and open furnace doors to load stock, or signal crane operators to uncover soaking pits and lower ingots into them.
human_time_rationale: One instance = starting conveyors/opening furnace doors OR signaling crane ops to load one batch of ingots into soaking pits; a trained operator completes this in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy industrial control panels, coordinating with crane operators, and managing furnace/soaking pit systems in a hazardous steel mill environment. Current humanoids lack the specialized tool interfaces, safety clearances, and coordination protocols for this industrial setting.

task: Prepare newly installed geothermal heat systems for operation by flushing, purging, or other actions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = commissioning a single geothermal heat system. Flushing/purging loops, checking pressures, and verifying flow typically takes a competent HVAC technician 1.5–2.5 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized flush carts, connecting hoses to manifolds, reading gauges, adjusting valves, and interpreting system-specific results. Highly unstructured, tool-heavy, requires domain expertise and judgment far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Integrate hot water heater systems with geothermal heat exchange systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = integrating a hot water heater with a geothermal heat exchange system in a single installation. A skilled plumber/HVAC technician typically takes 6–8 hours for piping, connections, controls, and commissioning.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex multi-trade work: threading pipes, soldering, pressure testing, electrical wiring, programming controls, and operating in confined mechanical spaces. Far beyond current humanoid robot generalization, dexterity, and tool-use capabilities.

task: Wash and dry bodies, using germicidal soap and towels or hot air dryers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = bathing and drying one patient/resident. A competent aide typically takes 10–20 minutes for a full body wash and dry.
robot_success_rationale: Requires delicate, adaptive touch on a human body with complex geometry, unpredictable movement, and high safety/comfort demands. Current humanoids lack the gentle dexterity, compliance control, and situational judgment needed for safe caregiving contact.

task: Reshape or reconstruct disfigured or maimed bodies when necessary, using dermasurgery techniques and materials such as clay, cotton, plaster of Paris, and wax.
human_time_rationale: One instance = restoring a single disfigured body for viewing. A skilled mortuary reconstructionist typically spends 1–3 hours depending on severity of trauma.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, nuanced artistic judgment, tactile sensitivity with wax/clay/plaster on irregular tissue, and deep domain knowledge. Far beyond current robot capability in unstructured, high-consequence biological contexts.

task: Position stock in furnaces, using tongs, chain hoists, or pry bars.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning one piece of stock into a furnace using tongs, hoist, or pry bar; includes approach, grip, placement, and confirmation. Typical in forge/heat-treat operations.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy industrial tools (chain hoists, pry bars, large tongs) in high-heat environments with heavy, awkward stock. Extreme thermal conditions, heavy loads, and unstructured tool-use far exceed current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Perform special procedures necessary for remains that are to be transported to other states or overseas, or where death was caused by infectious disease.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single set of remains for transport/infectious-disease handling, including embalming, disinfection, sealing, and documentation. Typically 1.5–2.5 hours for a competent mortician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly specialized mortuary dexterity (arterial injection, cavity aspiration, sealing), strict regulatory compliance, infection control protocols, and nuanced judgment. Far beyond any current general-purpose humanoid robot's capabilities.

task: Collect soiled linen or clothing for laundering.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting soiled linen/clothing from a typical room or area (e.g., hotel room, patient room, household). A competent worker gathers, sorts, and bags items in ~10 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can navigate indoor spaces and pick up objects, but soiled linen is deformable, irregularly placed, and often tangled. Identifying, grasping, and collecting varied soft goods from floors, beds, and hampers is challenging for current systems.

task: Rotate sweep boards around spindles to make symmetrical molds for convex impressions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = rotating a sweep board around a spindle to form one symmetrical mold. Skilled moldmaker sets up, sweeps, and checks symmetry; ~20–30 min per mold.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise rotational control of a sweep board around a spindle, tactile feedback to gauge mold symmetry, and specialized tool handling. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, process-specific tool use, and quality-inspection capability needed for this craft task.

task: Ensure food is stored and cooked at correct temperature by regulating temperature of ovens, broilers, grills, and roasters.
human_time_rationale: One instance = checking and adjusting temperature settings on one piece of equipment (oven, broiler, grill, or roaster) during a shift; typically takes a minute or two per check, averaged at ~5 min including logging.
robot_success_rationale: Robot can read digital displays and press buttons on modern equipment, but regulating temperatures requires contextual judgment, reading analog dials, handling varied interfaces, and integrating food safety knowledge. Unstructured kitchen environments and diverse equipment types limit reliability significantly.

task: Insert convex celluloid or cotton between eyeballs and eyelids to prevent slipping and sinking of eyelids.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inserting eye caps/cotton into both eyes of one body. A skilled mortuary technician does this in ~3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Extremely delicate, high-stakes manipulation on soft, deformable tissue in a confined space. Requires precise fine-motor dexterity, tactile sensitivity, and safe handling of fragile biological structures — far beyond current robot capability.

task: Remove parts from furnaces after specified times, and air dry or cool parts in water, oil brine, or other baths.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing a single batch/part from a furnace and completing the quench/cool cycle; typically 5–15 min depending on part size and cooling method.
robot_success_rationale: Extreme heat, heavy/irregular parts, precise timing, and hazardous quench baths challenge current robots. Dexterity for tongs/fixtures and thermal perception are marginal; safety limits and generalization to varied furnace setups further reduce success.

task: Set up and operate machine tools, such as milling machines, lathes, drill presses, and grinders, to machine castings or patterns.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and completing a machining operation on one casting/pattern. Includes fixture setup, tool selection, parameter setting, and running the cut; typical job ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert tool selection, precise fixture setup, reading blueprints, adjusting machine controls, and quality judgment. Humanoids lack the fine dexterity, specialized machine interfaces, and metrology skills needed; no credible 2026 deployment exists.

task: Assist coroners at death scenes or at autopsies, file police reports, and testify at inquests or in court, if employed by a coroner.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting at a single death scene or autopsy, filing associated report, and potential testimony. A scene + autopsy assistance typically takes 2–4 hours; testimony adds variable time averaged in.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced legal judgment, verbal courtroom testimony, sensitive human interaction, fine dexterous autopsy assistance, and unstructured environment navigation — far beyond 2026 robot capabilities in combination.

task: Align, fit, and join parts, using bolts and screws or by welding or gluing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = aligning and joining a typical multi-part subassembly using fasteners, welding, or adhesive, including fit-up and a brief quality check.
robot_success_rationale: Bolting simple standardized parts is feasible (~40–60%), but welding requires specialized end-effectors and precision perception; gluing demands force control and surface prep. Generalization across all three joining methods and varied part geometries drops overall success significantly.

task: Administer hearing or speech and language evaluations, tests, or examinations to patients to collect information on type and degree of impairments, using written or oral tests or special instruments.
human_time_rationale: One full evaluation session per patient, including test administration, observation, and preliminary scoring. Typically 45–90 minutes for a standard hearing or speech/language battery.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, adaptive patient interaction, nuanced verbal/behavioral observation, and specialized audiometric instruments. Current robots lack clinical reasoning, reliable speech understanding in impaired populations, and safe autonomous patient handling.

task: Pour molten metal into molds, manually or with crane ladles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = pouring one ladle/batch into molds. Includes positioning ladle, controlled pour, and moving to next mold; ~10–20 min per cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating crane controls or handling extremely heavy, high-temperature ladles — beyond humanoid payload, heat tolerance, and precision control. No current general-purpose robot can safely manage molten metal pouring in production.

task: Perform physical examinations by taking vital signs, checking neurological reflexes, examining breasts, or performing pelvic examinations.
human_time_rationale: One full physical examination instance covering vital signs, reflex checks, breast, and pelvic exam for a single patient; a competent clinician typically takes 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, calibrated tactile sensing, clinical judgment, sterile technique, patient communication, and ethical/safety safeguards far beyond any current general-purpose humanoid robot's capabilities.

task: Collect samples, or signal workers to sample metal for analysis.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting or signaling for one metal sample. A competent worker locates the source, collects/signals, and labels the sample in ~10 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating industrial environments, identifying correct sample points, operating specialized sampling tools, and coordinating with workers. Poor generalization to hot/molten metal, unstructured plant floors, and precise tool use at source locations.

task: Apply cosmetics to impart lifelike appearance to the deceased.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying restorative cosmetics to a single deceased individual. A skilled mortuary cosmetician typically spends 30–60 minutes per case.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity on irregular, delicate human facial tissue; nuanced color-matching and artistic judgment; working around reconstructed or embalmed features. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capability. High-stakes failure consequences.

task: Work as part of a team of physicians, nurses, or other healthcare professionals to manage patient care by assisting with medical procedures or related duties.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting with a single medical procedure or care episode (e.g., wound dressing, IV placement assist, patient repositioning). Median across varied procedure types ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity for sterile/clinical tasks, real-time adaptive teamwork with humans, nuanced clinical judgment, and safe patient interaction. Current humanoids lack the precision, sterility compliance, and social coordination required.

task: Position patterns inside mold sections, and clamp sections together.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning patterns in one mold and clamping its sections together. A competent worker does this in ~5 minutes with hand tools.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise bimanual manipulation to align delicate patterns inside mold halves and apply even clamping force. Perception of correct pattern seating, varied mold geometries, and force-controlled clamping push well beyond current humanoid dexterity and generalization limits.

task: Set up and operate or tend machines, such as furnaces, baths, flame-hardening machines, and electronic induction machines, that harden, anneal, and heat-treat metal.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and completing one heat-treatment batch/run. Setup plus monitoring a typical cycle (e.g., annealing or hardening a batch of parts) averages ~45 min for a competent operator.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized industrial furnaces/machines with complex controls, precise parameter setting, loading/unloading hot metal parts, and safety-critical monitoring. Current humanoids lack the machine-specific tool-use, high-heat environment tolerance, and process judgment needed.

task: Mount fixtures and industrial coils on machines, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = mounting one fixture or coil set on one machine. A competent technician positions, aligns, and fastens components using hand tools in ~20–30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise alignment of heavy/awkward industrial coils, torquing fasteners to spec with hand tools, and force-feedback judgment. Current humanoids lack reliable torque control, bimanual coordination with heavy loads, and generalization to varied machine interfaces.

task: Remove dishes and glasses from tables or counters, and take them to kitchen for cleaning.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing one table/counter of dishes and glasses and transporting them to the kitchen. A competent busser does this in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick common objects and navigate indoors, but varied dish/glass shapes, stacking, avoiding breakage, navigating crowded dining areas, and full end-to-end reliability remain significant challenges for 2026 humanoids.

task: Repair, replace, and maintain furnace equipment as needed, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single repair/replacement/maintenance job on a furnace unit. A competent HVAC technician typically spends 60–120 minutes diagnosing, disassembling, replacing parts, and reassembling.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert-level diagnosis, fine dexterous tool use in confined/cluttered spaces, varied fasteners, flexible hoses, electrical connections, and contextual judgment about failures. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Roll silverware, set up food stations, or set up dining areas to prepare for the next shift or for large parties.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a dining area/station or rolling silverware for a shift or party section; a competent server/busser typically takes 20–30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (rolling silverware in napkins), navigating varied table layouts, placing items precisely, and generalizing across setups. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation and contextual table-setting knowledge.

task: Offer assistance to mourners as they enter or exit limousines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one mourner in/out of a limousine. A competent human takes ~1–3 minutes per person, including greeting, steadying, and guiding.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor terrain near a vehicle, gentle physical contact with distressed humans, dynamic social cues, and precise hand-steadying. Current humanoids lack safe unstructured caregiving touch, social sensitivity, and reliable outdoor/vehicle-adjacent operation.

task: Stock bar with beer, wine, liquor, and related supplies such as ice, glassware, napkins, or straws.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fully stocking a bar station at shift start. A skilled bartender/barback arranges bottles, ice, glassware, and supplies in ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a cluttered bar environment, carrying heavy/fragile bottles and glassware, stacking ice, and arranging varied supplies. Poor generalization to bar layouts, fragile object handling, and ice management limits current robots severely.

task: Inspect cards and equipment to be used in games to ensure that they are in good condition.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a full set of cards and equipment for one game table before a shift; a competent worker visually and tactilely checks decks and gear in ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine tactile perception to detect marked/bent cards, precise visual inspection for subtle defects, and generalization across varied equipment. Current robots lack the fine-grained haptic sensing and defect-recognition reliability needed for acceptable quality.

task: Conduct individual in-room visits with residents.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single in-room visit with one resident. A competent care worker typically spends 15–25 minutes greeting, conversing, checking on needs, and documenting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social interaction, emotional intelligence, adaptive conversation, and trust-building — far beyond current robot capabilities. Navigation to the room is feasible, but meaningful human-quality engagement is not.

task: Stand behind a gaming table and deal the appropriate number of cards to each player.
human_time_rationale: One instance = dealing one hand to a typical table (4–6 players). A competent dealer shuffles and distributes cards in ~1–3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Card handling demands extreme fine dexterity (thin, slippery, fanned cards), precise orientation, and spatial awareness of multiple player positions. Current humanoids lack reliable card-gripping and controlled single-card dealing capability.

task: Join lips, using needles and thread or wire.
human_time_rationale: One instance = suturing/wiring both lips of a single wound or incision closed. A skilled practitioner (e.g. surgeon or embalmer) takes ~15–25 minutes depending on technique and length.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, precise needle handling in/near delicate tissue, tactile feedback, and spatial accuracy at millimeter scale — well beyond current humanoid robot capability. Risk of harm makes autonomous operation unacceptable.

task: Pack body orifices with cotton saturated with embalming fluid to prevent escape of gases or waste matter.
human_time_rationale: One instance = packing all body orifices on a single deceased individual. A skilled embalmer typically completes this step in 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity to handle delicate tissues, precise force control near fragile orifices, and tactile sensitivity for cotton saturation/placement. Current humanoids lack the surgical-level dexterity and nuanced tissue handling this demands.

task: Mix and add detergents, dyes, bleaches, starches, and other solutions and chemicals to clean, color, dry, or stiffen articles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing and adding a batch of chemical solutions (detergents, dyes, bleaches, etc.) for a single laundry/textile processing cycle; includes measuring, mixing, and pouring.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement of liquids/powders, identifying unlabeled containers, operating valves/dispensers, and safely handling hazardous chemicals. Current robots lack reliable liquid handling, fine dexterity for dispensing, and chemical-safety reasoning.

task: Clean oxides and scales from parts or fittings, using steam sprays or chemical and water baths.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning a single part/fitting. Competent worker submerges/sprays, monitors soak/spray time, inspects, and moves part through process in ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling varied oily/corroded parts, operating chemical baths or steam equipment, and quality-inspecting results. Chemical handling safety, tool operation, and process judgment are beyond current humanoid generalization. Structured pick-and-place elements help slightly.

task: Ensure freshness of food and ingredients by checking for quality, keeping track of old and new items, and rotating stock.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and rotating stock in one storage area (e.g., a cooler or pantry shelf), including date-checking and repositioning items. Typical kitchen worker takes ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-modal quality perception (smell, texture, visual spoilage cues), reading varied date labels, and dexterous rearrangement of diverse packaged/unpackaged foods. Current robots lack reliable freshness sensing and generalized food-handling dexterity.

task: Load parts into containers and place containers on conveyors to be inserted into furnaces, or insert parts into furnaces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading a batch of parts into a container and placing it on a conveyor or directly into a furnace. A competent worker does this in ~5–10 minutes per batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires pick-and-place of heavy/hot industrial parts, precise container loading, and interacting with conveyors/furnace doors in a high-temperature, unstructured environment. Current humanoids lack heat tolerance, sufficient payload capacity, and reliable generalization in such settings.

task: Prepare a variety of foods, such as meats, vegetables, or desserts, according to customers' orders or supervisors' instructions, following approved procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single customer order (e.g., a plated dish or meal component). A competent line cook completes a typical order in ~15–25 minutes end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Cooking from varied recipes in an unstructured kitchen requires multi-step planning, fine dexterity (cutting, stirring, plating), heat management, and generalization across ingredients and tools — well beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Tear down equipment after event completion.
human_time_rationale: One instance = tearing down equipment for a single typical event (e.g., stage, AV, tables/chairs). A competent crew member spends ~90 min on their share of a standard teardown.
robot_success_rationale: Teardown involves highly varied, unstructured tasks: disconnecting cables, disassembling scaffolding, stacking furniture, carrying bulky/heavy items. Current humanoids lack the strength, dexterity, and generalization for such an unscripted, dynamic environment.

task: Perform various financial activities, such as cash handling, deposit preparation, and payroll.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a typical daily financial activity session (cash counting, deposit prep, payroll entry). A competent bookkeeper/teller completes this in ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous cash handling, reading documents, operating computers/software, and complex cognitive financial judgment. Current humanoids lack reliable fine-finger dexterity and financial software interaction for autonomous end-to-end completion.

task: Grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = finishing one part to spec. A competent machinist/metalworker typically spends 10–30 min grinding, filing, and sanding a single part to finished dimensions.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise force-controlled multi-tool dexterity, real-time dimensional feedback, and adaptive technique. Current humanoids lack the fine-force sensing, tool-switching fluency, and in-process metrology needed to reliably hit finished tolerances.

task: Conduct gambling games, such as dice, roulette, cards, or keno, following all applicable rules and regulations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full round/hand of a table game (e.g., a blackjack hand or roulette spin), including shuffling/dealing, collecting bets, resolving outcomes, and paying winners. ~15 min covers a typical multi-player round.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous card handling, chip manipulation, fast social interaction, rule enforcement, fraud detection, and regulatory compliance. Current humanoids lack the fine motor precision, real-time social perception, and contextual judgment needed. Near-zero viability.

task: Test, clean, maintain, and repair broadcast equipment, including testing microphones, to ensure proper working condition.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full maintenance/test cycle on a broadcast equipment set (e.g., one studio's gear). Includes mic testing, diagnostics, cleaning, minor repairs; ~45 min for a competent broadcast tech.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced audio diagnostics, fine connector manipulation, interpreting signal readings, and judgment-based repair decisions. Current robots lack the specialized tool-use, perception of subtle faults, and complex troubleshooting cognition needed.

task: Move controls to light gas burners and to adjust gas and water flow and flame temperature.
human_time_rationale: One instance = adjusting knobs/valves on one burner station to set gas, water flow, and flame temperature; a competent worker completes this in ~1–2 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Turning industrial knobs and valves requires moderate dexterity and force control. Frontier humanoids can manipulate knobs in structured settings but struggle with varied control types, precise positioning, and reading analog gauges for feedback.

task: Escort individuals or groups on cruises, sightseeing tours, or through places of interest, such as industrial establishments, public buildings, or art galleries.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one guided tour/escort session. Typical sightseeing or gallery tour runs 60–120 minutes; 90 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic crowd navigation, rich verbal narration, audience engagement, answering unpredictable questions, and adapting to diverse unstructured environments. Current robots lack social fluency, reliable speech interaction, and robust outdoor/multi-venue mobility.

task: Administer treatments or therapies, such as homeopathy, hydrotherapy, Oriental or Ayurvedic medicine, electrotherapy, and diathermy, using physical agents including air, heat, cold, water, sound, or ultraviolet light to catalyze the body to heal itself.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient treatment session (e.g., hydrotherapy, electrotherapy, diathermy). A competent practitioner typically spends 30–60 minutes per session including setup, administration, and monitoring.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical assessment, nuanced patient communication, precise therapeutic tool application, safety monitoring, and adaptive decision-making. Current robots lack medical judgment, fine dexterity for specialized equipment, and safe unstructured human-contact caregiving at clinical standards.

task: Teach clients to control or strengthen tongue, jaw, face muscles, or breathing mechanisms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single therapy/coaching session with one client. A typical orofacial myology or speech therapy session runs ~45 minutes including instruction, demonstration, and guided practice.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced verbal instruction, real-time observation of subtle muscle movements, tactile guidance, and adaptive feedback tailored to each client. Current robots lack clinical perception, communication fluency, and safe facial/oral interaction needed for this specialized caregiving task.

task: Clean machine filters, and lubricate equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one machine's filters and lubricating its key components; a competent technician typically takes ~20 minutes per machine.
robot_success_rationale: Requires locating/removing filters, applying precise lubrication to specific points, and reassembling — all in varied industrial environments. Current robots lack reliable tool-use, fine manipulation, and generalization for this multi-step maintenance task.

task: Measure noise levels in workplaces and conduct hearing conservation programs in industry, military, schools, and communities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full noise survey of a single workplace/site, including dosimeter placement, measurements across zones, and documenting results. Typically 1–3 hours for a competent industrial hygienist.
robot_success_rationale: While a robot could physically carry a sound level meter, it cannot conduct hearing conservation programs, communicate with workers, interpret regulatory context, make professional judgments, or operate audiometric equipment. Highly cognitive, social, and program-based task.

task: Direct installation or testing of new remote sensing hardware or software.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing or testing one remote sensing device. Includes mounting, wiring, config, and functional test; ~90 min for a competent technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise cable routing, connector mating, software configuration, and diagnostic interpretation. Current humanoids lack fine dexterity for connectors, tool-use for mounting hardware, and software interfacing capability needed end-to-end.

task: Cut, shape, and form metal parts, using lathes, power saws, snips, power brakes and shears, files, and mallets.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fabricating a single metal part from raw stock. A competent metalworker typically spends 30–60 minutes measuring, cutting, and shaping one part depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating multiple specialized heavy tools (lathe, power saw, brake, shear), precise force control with files/mallets, and skilled sequencing. Humanoids lack the dexterity, tool-operation ability, and safety compliance for unstructured metalworking machinery.

task: Examine parts to ensure metal shades and colors conform to specifications, using knowledge of metal heat-treating.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single part for color/shade conformance. A skilled heat-treat inspector visually assesses and judges a part in ~2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Color/shade perception via cameras is feasible, but distinguishing subtle metal heat-treat color variations (e.g., temper colors, oxide layers) with sufficient accuracy and domain-specific judgment is well beyond current robot reliability. Lighting sensitivity and lack of heat-treat expertise further limit success.

task: Place caskets in parlors or chapels prior to wakes or funerals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning a single casket in a parlor/chapel. Includes moving the casket on a trolley, aligning it precisely, and setting up the immediate surroundings. Typically done by 2 workers in ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Caskets are extremely heavy (100–200 kg loaded), requiring coordinated multi-robot or specialized equipment. Humanoids lack the payload capacity, coordinated heavy lifting, and precise spatial placement needed. Tight indoor navigation adds further difficulty.

task: Apply plastic-impregnated fabrics or coats of sealing wax or lacquer to patterns used to produce plastic.
human_time_rationale: One instance = coating one mold pattern with sealant material; a skilled worker brushes/applies and inspects a single pattern in ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise, even application of viscous/flexible materials over complex 3D mold surfaces, material handling of fabric/wax/lacquer, and quality inspection. Current robots lack the dexterity, force control, and generalization for consistent, defect-free coating of irregular patterns.

task: Operate machines that comb, dry and polish furs, clean, sterilize and fluff feathers and blankets, or roll and package towels.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one batch/cycle through a machine (e.g., one fur combed, one feather load cleaned, one set of towels rolled/packaged). Typical cycle is 10–20 minutes including loading, monitoring, and unloading.
robot_success_rationale: Requires loading/unloading soft, deformable, varied materials into specialized industrial machines, adjusting settings, and quality-checking outputs. Robots struggle with floppy textiles, non-standard machine interfaces, and tactile quality assessment.

task: Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments and charts, dials and gauges of furnaces, and color of stock in furnaces to make setting determinations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one adjustment cycle: reading instruments/charts/stock color, interpreting data, and making dial/control changes. A skilled furnace operator completes this roughly every 10–20 minutes during a heat treatment run.
robot_success_rationale: Requires visually interpreting stock color (subtle perceptual judgment), reading analog dials and gauges, cross-referencing charts, and making precise control adjustments — a complex multi-modal perceptual and fine-manipulation task in a hot, industrial environment. Current robots lack reliable color-temperature inference and gauge-reading generalization at this level.

task: Operate cash register, handle money, and give correct change.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one customer transaction: scanning/entering items, accepting payment, and returning correct change. Typical retail transaction takes ~2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle coins/bills, operating a specific POS interface, reliable currency recognition, and correct change calculation. Current humanoids lack reliable fine-finger manipulation of currency and POS integration.

task: Clean and polish footwear, using brushes, sponges, cleaning fluid, polishes, waxes, liquid or sole dressing, and daubers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one pair of shoes. A skilled worker cleans, applies polish/wax, buffs, and finishes sole dressing in roughly 8 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous manipulation of multiple tools (brushes, daubers, sponges) on a curved, flexible surface, precise force control for buffing, and sequenced multi-tool use. Current humanoids lack the dexterity and tactile feedback for acceptable quality.

task: Pick up and deliver items or run errands for guests.
human_time_rationale: One instance = retrieving a requested item and delivering it to a guest (e.g., hotel/resort errand). Includes fetching, travel, and hand-off; typical errand takes ~10–15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating varied indoor environments, handling elevators/doors, locating items, and socially handing off to a guest. Locomotion and pick-and-place are feasible in structured spaces, but unstructured environments, social interaction, and generalization across errand types remain major barriers.

task: Press diaphragm to evacuate air from lungs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single manual diaphragm compression on a patient. A trained person performs this in seconds to ~1 minute including positioning.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise force-controlled contact on a compliant, sensitive human body. Current humanoids lack the fine haptic feedback, calibrated pressure control, and safe physical contact protocols needed for clinical-grade diaphragm compression.

task: Load articles into washers or dry-cleaning machines, or direct other workers to perform loading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading one washer or dry-cleaning machine with a batch of articles; skilled laundry worker completes this in ~5 minutes including sorting.
robot_success_rationale: Grasping and stuffing varied textile items into a drum is challenging due to deformable-object manipulation. Robots can pick clothing but reliably loading a full batch into a specific machine opening autonomously remains ~30% given current dexterity and perception limits.

task: Set up, test, and adjust recording equipment for recording sessions and live performances.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up, testing, and adjusting equipment for a single session/performance. A competent audio engineer typically takes 60–120 minutes for a standard setup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex cable routing, fine-tuning audio mixers/EQ by ear, interpreting acoustic feedback, and coordinating with performers. Current robots lack audio perception, fine dexterous connector-mating, and domain-specific judgment for acceptable sound quality.

task: Select pattern materials such as wood, resin, and fiberglass.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting materials for a single pattern project. A skilled patternmaker spends ~20 min evaluating material properties, availability, and requirements.
robot_success_rationale: Task is primarily cognitive/expert judgment — assessing material properties, tolerances, and suitability. Current robots lack domain expertise and abstract reasoning for expert material selection decisions; no meaningful physical component.

task: Provide assistance with animal euthanasia and the disposal of remains.
human_time_rationale: One instance = euthanizing one animal (preparation, administration, confirmation). A trained veterinary professional typically takes 15–45 minutes including preparation and paperwork.
robot_success_rationale: Requires licensed veterinary judgment, precise IV injection into small/moving vessels, sedation assessment, emotional sensitivity, and legal compliance — far beyond current robot dexterity, perception, and autonomy limits.

task: Close caskets at appropriate point in services.
human_time_rationale: One instance = closing a single casket at the designated moment in a funeral service. A funeral director positions the lid and latches it, typically taking 2–4 minutes including ceremonial care.
robot_success_rationale: Casket lids are large, heavy, and require precise, gentle handling. Latching mechanisms vary widely. Beyond mechanics, the task demands contextual awareness of service timing and social sensitivity — well beyond current robot generalization. High failure risk on latches and timing cues.

task: Assist with duties in other areas of the store, such as monitoring fitting rooms or bagging and carrying out customers' items.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one customer interaction (bagging/carryout or one fitting room monitoring shift unit ~15 min); routine retail support tasks are brief and episodic.
robot_success_rationale: Bagging varied soft/hard items requires adaptable dexterity; carryout requires outdoor/parking lot mobility; fitting room monitoring needs social judgment and communication. Robots struggle with all three in unstructured retail environments.

task: Serve as pallbearers, attend visiting rooms, and provide other assistance to the bereaved.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single funeral service. Pallbearing, visiting-room attendance, and bereaved assistance typically spans ~2 hours for one service.
robot_success_rationale: Requires coordinated multi-person casket carrying, empathetic social interaction, reading emotional cues, and navigating crowded unstructured environments. Current humanoids lack social grace, reliable group coordination, and safe load-bearing with humans.

task: Shape old parts and replacement parts to improve tone or intonation, using hand tools, lathes, or soldering irons.
human_time_rationale: One instance = shaping one part (e.g., a nut, saddle, or bridge pin) for a musical instrument. A skilled luthier/repair tech typically spends 30–60 min filing, lathing, or soldering a single component to spec.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert-level fine dexterity with multiple tools (files, lathes, soldering irons), acoustic judgment for tone/intonation, and adaptive problem-solving. Far beyond current humanoid capability in unstructured, precision craft contexts.

task: Lift upper mold sections from lower sections, and remove molded patterns.
human_time_rationale: One instance = lifting upper mold half off lower half and removing one molded pattern. A skilled worker takes ~5 min including alignment checks and safe part removal.
robot_success_rationale: Mold sections are heavy, irregularly shaped, and may require precise alignment and force. Extracting delicate molded patterns without damage demands fine force control and dexterity. Current humanoids lack reliable heavy-lift + fine extraction capability in unstructured industrial settings.

task: Provide assistance to patrons by performing duties such as opening doors or carrying bags.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patron interaction (e.g., open a door or carry bags to a room/vehicle). A competent bellhop/concierge completes this in ~3 minutes on average.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can open some doors (~40–70%) and carry bags (~30–50%), but combining social cue detection, unstructured patron interaction, navigating lobbies, and handling varied luggage shapes in dynamic spaces drops reliability significantly.

task: Inspect dining and serving areas to ensure cleanliness and proper setup.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one dining/serving area section. A trained worker walks through, checks table settings, cleanliness, and serving station setup in roughly 10 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced visual inspection (stains, misaligned cutlery, smudges), contextual judgment on "proper setup," and navigation around furniture. Current robots lack reliable fine-detail perception and setup-standard generalization.

task: Dress bodies and place them in caskets.
human_time_rationale: One instance = dressing one body and placing it in a casket. A trained mortuary professional typically takes 45–75 minutes total.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme dexterity to dress a limp, fragile human body; highly unstructured, irregular object. Casket placement demands precise coordination and safe handling. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability in real-world deployment.

task: Perform general cleaning activities in kitchen and dining areas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one kitchen and dining area (wiping surfaces, sweeping/mopping floors, tidying). A competent worker typically takes 25–35 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can sweep/mop flat floors (~50–70%) but struggle with cluttered kitchen surfaces, varied objects, grease/residue scrubbing, and multi-step sequencing across an unstructured environment. Generalization and manipulation in tight spaces remain limiting.

task: Receive, store, and inventory pharmaceutical supplies or medications, check for out-of-date medications, and notify pharmacist when inventory levels are low.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a typical pharmacy supply delivery: unboxing, shelving, logging inventory, checking expiry dates, and flagging low stock. Median ~45 min per delivery session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading small expiry-date text on varied pharmaceutical packaging, fine dexterous handling of bottles/blister packs, navigating pharmacy storage systems, and triggering software alerts — well beyond current humanoid generalization and fine perception capabilities.

task: Fabricate lenses to meet prescription specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fabricating a single pair of prescription lenses: blocking, grinding/surfacing, edging, polishing, and inspection. A skilled lab technician takes ~30–60 min end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized optical machinery (lens lathes, edgers, polishers), sub-millimeter dexterity for blocking and mounting, precise prescription verification, and multi-step tool changes — far beyond current general-purpose humanoid capability.

task: Iron or press articles, fabrics, and furs, using hand irons or pressing machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = ironing a single garment (e.g., a dress shirt). A competent human takes ~5–12 minutes depending on garment complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise contact-force control, continuous cloth repositioning on a deformable surface, heat management, and generalization across fabric types and garment shapes — well beyond current humanoid dexterity and compliance.

task: Arrange funeral home equipment and perform general maintenance.
human_time_rationale: One instance = arranging equipment in a preparation room and performing a routine maintenance check; a competent mortuary technician typically takes 30–60 minutes per session.
robot_success_rationale: Funeral home equipment (embalming tables, trolleys, casket lifts, specialty tools) is heavy, varied, and rarely seen in robot training data. Navigation in tight prep rooms, safe tool handling, and maintenance diagnostics far exceed current humanoid generalization.

task: Perform routine laboratory tests or diagnostic tests, such as taking or developing x-rays.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single routine lab/diagnostic test (e.g., one X-ray series). Includes patient prep, image capture or sample processing, and basic documentation — ~15–25 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous instrument handling, sterile technique, patient interaction/positioning, operating specialized medical equipment, and clinical judgment. Current humanoids lack medical-grade dexterity, safety certification, and generalization across lab/imaging workflows.

task: Pre-soak, sterilize, scrub, spot-clean, and dry contaminated or stained articles, using neutralizer solutions and portable machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one batch/article through full cycle (pre-soak, sterilize, scrub, spot-clean, dry). A competent laundry/decontamination worker typically takes ~30–60 min per batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sequencing multiple specialized steps (chemical mixing, portable machine operation, scrubbing, spot inspection, drying), fine dexterity, and judgment for contamination levels. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use chaining and chemical handling in unstructured settings.

task: Manage reserve materials by placing items on reserve for library patrons, checking items in and out of library, and removing out-of-date items.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single reserve item (place on reserve, check in/out, or remove). A competent library worker handles this in ~2–4 minutes including scanning, shelving, and system entry.
robot_success_rationale: Requires integrating barcode scanning, navigating library shelves, retrieving/placing specific items, and interfacing with library software. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation for thin books, shelf navigation, and software interaction without custom integration.

task: Maintain library equipment, such as photocopiers, scanners, and computers, and instruct patrons in proper use of such equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one equipment maintenance/check or patron instruction session. Competent library staff spend ~15 min inspecting, troubleshooting, or demonstrating a device to a patron.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social interaction and verbal instruction to patrons, nuanced troubleshooting of varied electronics, and fine manipulation of equipment controls. Current humanoids lack reliable spoken instruction, patron interaction, and adaptive tech-support reasoning.

task: Wash, peel, and cut various foods, such as fruits and vegetables, to prepare for cooking or serving.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single batch of one food item (e.g., peeling and dicing one onion or slicing one apple). A competent cook takes ~10–15 min per batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires chained fine dexterous steps—grasping irregular produce, controlled peeling, and precise cutting with a knife—on highly variable shapes and textures. Current humanoids lack the fine motor control and force feedback for safe, consistent knife work on deformable objects.

task: Organize and participate in recreational activities and outings, such as games and field trips.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one organized activity or outing (e.g., a game session or field trip). Includes planning, setup, facilitation, and wrap-up; typical duration is ~2 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social leadership, dynamic group coordination, adaptive communication, and judgment in unstructured outdoor/public environments. Current robots lack social agency, group management, and the generalization needed for unscripted recreational contexts.

task: Monitor machine operation to detect problems such as defective stitching, breaks in thread, or machine malfunctions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one monitoring/inspection cycle per machine shift check. A competent operator scans machine output, thread tension, and stitch quality over ~15 minutes per cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Vision-based defect detection is feasible but distinguishing subtle stitch defects, thread breaks, and machine sounds in a dynamic textile environment requires fine perception, generalization, and contextual judgment beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Immerse articles in bleaching baths to strip colors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = immersing a batch of articles into a bleaching bath. Includes handling items, submerging them, and ensuring full coverage — typically a few minutes of active work per batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires grasping varied wet/flexible articles, navigating chemical bath containers, and ensuring full submersion. Liquid handling, flexible material manipulation, and chemical environment tolerance are weak points for current humanoids.

task: Monitor fetal development by listening to fetal heartbeat, taking external uterine measurements, identifying fetal position, or estimating fetal size and weight.
human_time_rationale: One prenatal monitoring session: auscultating fetal heart tones, fundal height measurement, Leopold's maneuvers for position, and size/weight estimation — typically 8–12 minutes per visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise clinical touch (Doppler placement, fundal palpation, Leopold's maneuvers), nuanced force control on a pregnant abdomen, medical interpretation, and patient communication. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and clinical reasoning.

task: Provide for physical safety of groups, performing such activities as providing first aid or directing emergency evacuations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single emergency response event (e.g., one first-aid intervention or one evacuation drill/event), typically lasting 15–45 minutes for a competent safety officer.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex social coordination, dynamic decision-making, verbal communication, situational awareness across crowds, and adaptive first aid. Current humanoids lack reliable autonomous crowd management, triage judgment, and emergency communication capability.

task: Apply insulation materials, such as loose, blanket, board, and foam insulation to attics, crawl spaces, basements, or walls.
human_time_rationale: One instance = insulating one average room/zone (e.g., attic or crawl space). A competent insulator typically spends 1.5–2.5 hours per zone including prep, cutting, fitting, and sealing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating extremely confined, uneven spaces (crawl spaces, attics), cutting and fitting varied flexible/rigid materials, and working overhead or in tight angles. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capability in unstructured, hazardous environments.

task: Inspect restrooms for cleanliness and availability of supplies, and clean restrooms when necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and cleaning one restroom. A competent janitor takes ~10–20 min to inspect, restock supplies, and clean a typical commercial restroom.
robot_success_rationale: Combines inspection, restocking (grasping small/varied supply items), and full cleaning (scrubbing toilets, wiping surfaces, mopping). Each subtask is marginally feasible but chaining all reliably in a cramped, wet, varied environment is beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Arrange floral offerings or lights around caskets.
human_time_rationale: One instance = arranging floral offerings and/or lights around a single casket for a service. A skilled funeral home worker typically takes 15–25 minutes to position and adjust arrangements aesthetically.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced aesthetic judgment, gentle handling of delicate flowers, navigation in a cluttered chapel, and socially sensitive context. Current robots lack reliable aesthetic reasoning, compliant floral manipulation, and generalization in unstructured ceremonial environments.

task: Set up, maintain, calibrate, clean, and test sterility of medical laboratory equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full setup, calibration, cleaning, and sterility test cycle for a single piece of lab equipment; a competent lab tech typically takes 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (calibration adjustments, sterility swabs, reagent handling), domain-specific tool use, aseptic technique, and judgment on pass/fail criteria — far beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Assess nutritional needs of patients, plan special menus, supervise the assembly of regular and special diet trays, and oversee the delivery of food trolleys to hospital patients.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assessing one patient's needs, planning their menu, supervising tray assembly, and overseeing trolley delivery to that patient's ward. ~45 min total cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment for nutritional assessment, complex menu planning, interpersonal supervision of staff, and safe navigation of a dynamic hospital environment. Far beyond current robot generalization and cognitive/social capabilities.

task: Perform medical procedures, such as administering oxygen, inserting and removing airways, taking vital signs, or giving emergency treatment, such as first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR).
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single emergency medical procedure episode (e.g., CPR, O2 administration, airway insertion). A competent EMT/paramedic typically completes one such intervention in ~10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (airway insertion, IV), real-time clinical judgment, adaptive force control on a human body, and dynamic unstructured environments. Far beyond 2026 frontier humanoid capabilities; patient safety stakes make any failure unacceptable.

task: Spray steam, water, or air over spots to flush out chemicals, dry material, raise naps, or brighten colors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = treating a single spot or section of fabric/material with steam, water, or air spray; a skilled worker completes this in roughly 2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise nozzle positioning, variable pressure control, and visual/tactile feedback to judge treatment quality (nap raised, color brightened). Current robots lack reliable fine spray tool use and nuanced fabric-state perception needed for acceptable results.

task: Perform physical examinations, make tentative diagnoses, and treat patients en route to hospitals or at disaster site triage centers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient encounter (exam, tentative diagnosis, initial treatment). A competent paramedic typically spends 10–20 minutes per patient in field triage/treatment.
robot_success_rationale: Requires advanced clinical reasoning, nuanced tactile assessment (palpation, auscultation), dynamic unstructured environments, patient communication, and life-critical decision-making. Far beyond 2026 robot capabilities in generalization, dexterity, and medical judgment.

task: Perform cleaning duties, such as sweeping and mopping floors, vacuuming carpet, tidying up server station, taking out trash, or checking and cleaning bathroom.
human_time_rationale: One full cleaning rotation: sweeping/mopping floors, vacuuming carpet, tidying server station, taking out trash, and checking/cleaning a bathroom — typical shift duty cycle for a restaurant/hospitality worker.
robot_success_rationale: Robots handle flat sweeping/mopping moderately well, but vacuuming carpet, tidying cluttered server stations, handling trash bags, and scrubbing bathrooms demand multi-tool use, unstructured environments, and fine manipulation — far beyond reliable autonomous capability in 2026.

task: Provide care for children with physical, developmental, or mental health disabilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single caregiving shift/session for one child with disabilities. A typical structured care session (feeding, therapy support, hygiene, activity assistance) runs ~3–4 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced emotional attunement, adaptive communication, safe physical handling of vulnerable children, and real-time behavioral judgment. Far beyond current humanoid generalization, dexterity, and safety thresholds for unstructured caregiving of special-needs children.

task: Lend, reserve, and collect books, periodicals, videotapes, and other materials at circulation desks and process materials for inter-library loans.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single patron transaction (check-out, reservation, or return) at a circulation desk, including scanning, stamping, and shelving prep. Typical interaction takes ~3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social interaction, scanning varied media formats, navigating a public desk environment, handling fragile/irregular items (tapes, periodicals), and coordinating ILL paperwork. Current robots lack reliable social-task integration and generalization for this complex, people-facing workflow.

task: Guide garments or garment parts under machine needles and presser feet to sew parts together.
human_time_rationale: One instance = guiding and sewing one seam or joining one pair of garment parts; a competent sewing machine operator typically completes this in 2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Fabric is highly deformable and unpredictable; aligning limp textile parts under a needle requires extreme fine dexterity and tactile feedback far beyond current humanoid capability. No commercial system reliably handles arbitrary garment pieces autonomously.

task: Assemble and check the required supplies and equipment prior to departure.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single pre-departure supply/equipment check. A competent worker gathers, verifies, and organizes all required items before one shift or trip; typically 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires understanding context-specific supply lists, navigating varied storage areas, identifying and verifying diverse items, and making judgment calls on adequacy — well beyond current robot generalization and semantic reasoning in unstructured environments.

task: Sort and count articles removed from dryers, and fold, wrap, or hang them.
human_time_rationale: One dryer load (~10–15 mixed garments/linens): unload, sort by type, count, fold/hang each item. A competent laundry worker takes ~15 min per load.
robot_success_rationale: Laundry folding is improving (~30–60% for single item types) but the full pipeline—unloading a dryer drum, sorting/counting mixed garments, then folding/hanging each—chains many hard dexterous steps, raising failure probability substantially. Dryer access and garment variety add further challenges.

task: Administer emergency first aid, wrap injuries, treat minor chronic disabilities, or refer injured persons to physicians.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient encounter (assessment + treatment/referral). A competent first-aider or occupational health nurse typically takes 10–30 min per case.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical assessment, fine dexterous wound care, adaptive decision-making, and patient communication. Beyond current robot dexterity, perception, and safe human-contact limits. No deployed general-purpose humanoid can safely treat patients autonomously.

task: Perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one PM service on a single vehicle/piece of equipment (oil change, fluid checks, filter swaps, lubrication, inspection). Typical shop PM takes 60–120 min.
robot_success_rationale: PM requires operating lifts, drain plugs, torque wrenches, fluid fills on varied heavy equipment in unstructured bays. Humanoids lack the specialized tool interfaces, under-vehicle mobility, strength, and force-feedback precision needed. Current robots cannot autonomously complete this safely.

task: Troubleshoot equipment, electrical components, hydraulics, or other mechanical systems.
human_time_rationale: One troubleshooting instance = diagnosing and identifying the fault on a single piece of equipment. A skilled technician typically spends 30–60 min per job.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-modal diagnosis (visual, auditory, instrument readings, logical reasoning), use of specialized tools (multimeters, hydraulic gauges), and adaptive problem-solving in unstructured environments—well beyond current robot generalization and tool-use capabilities.

task: Perform removal and rotation of cash, coin, or chip inventories as necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one cash/coin/chip inventory rotation at a single station (till or gaming table). Counting, removing, and restocking typically takes 20–40 min for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise fine-motor handling of bills, coins, and chips; secure chain-of-custody verification; interfacing with cash drawers, safes, and POS systems; and accountability protocols. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and system-integration capabilities.

task: Set up and operate electric news gathering (ENG) microwave vehicles to gather and edit raw footage on location to send to television affiliates for broadcast.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one field deployment: driving to location, setting up microwave mast/dish, gathering/editing footage, and transmitting to affiliate. Typically 3–5 hours end-to-end for a news event.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving a specialized vehicle in traffic, operating a microwave mast, editing footage with editorial judgment, and coordinating live broadcasts. Far beyond any 2026 humanoid or mobile manipulator capability across every sub-task.

task: Perform equipment maintenance tasks such as replacing needles, sanding rough areas of needles, or cleaning and oiling sewing machines.
human_time_rationale: One maintenance session on a sewing machine: replacing a needle (~2 min), sanding rough spots (~5 min), and cleaning/oiling (~8 min). Combined median ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires very fine dexterity (threading tiny needle slots, handling small screws), precise force control for sanding, and knowledge of machine internals. Far beyond current humanoid general-purpose capability; high failure risk on all sub-tasks.

task: Administer hearing tests and examine patients to collect information on type and degree of impairment, using specialized instruments and electronic equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full hearing evaluation for one patient, including case history, otoscopy, and audiometric testing. A competent audiologist typically takes 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical dexterity (otoscope placement in ear canal), patient communication, adaptive test interpretation, and professional judgment. No 2026 humanoid has these clinical interaction, fine-tool-use, or diagnostic reasoning capabilities at acceptable quality.

task: Maintain cage security.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single cage security check (inspect latches, locks, structural integrity, and perimeter). A competent worker completes this in ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires visual/tactile inspection of locks, latches, and structural elements in potentially unstructured environments. Current robots lack reliable fine-grained latch inspection, force-testing dexterity, and generalization across cage types and conditions.

task: Wire and solder electrical and electronic connections and components.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a typical set of wired/soldered connections on a single assembly (e.g., a circuit board or wiring harness). A competent technician takes ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Soldering requires extreme fine dexterity, precise thermal control, and close visual feedback. Current humanoid robots lack the sub-millimeter precision, compliant finger control, and tool-use generalization needed for reliable soldering. Far below frontier capability.

task: Teach proper breathing techniques used during physical exertion.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single instructional session teaching breathing techniques to an individual or small group; ~20 min covers explanation, demonstration, and guided practice.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced verbal instruction, real-time observation of student's breathing form, adaptive coaching, and credible physical demonstration — far beyond current humanoid robot social/pedagogical capability.

task: Match cloth pieces in correct sequences prior to sewing them, and verify that dye lots and patterns match.
human_time_rationale: One instance = matching and verifying a single garment's cloth pieces (dye lots + patterns). A skilled worker handles this in ~5–10 minutes per garment.
robot_success_rationale: Requires subtle color/dye-lot discrimination, precise pattern alignment across multiple fabric pieces, and sequencing knowledge. Vision perception is improving but fine textile inspection, soft-material handling, and nuanced color matching remain very challenging for current humanoids.

task: Position cores into lower sections of molds, and reassemble molds for pouring.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning cores into one mold's lower section and reassembling it. A skilled foundry worker typically takes ~5–10 minutes per mold.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise alignment of heavy, irregular mold cores, tight positional tolerances, and reassembly of mold halves. Current humanoids lack the fine force-feedback, spatial registration accuracy, and heavy-part dexterity needed for consistent foundry mold assembly.

task: Repair books using mending tape, paste, and brushes or prepare books to be sent to a bindery for repair.
human_time_rationale: One instance = repairing one book. A competent library technician takes ~15–25 min to assess damage, apply mending tape/paste, and prepare or package a book for the bindery.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous manipulation of thin tape, paste, and brushes on fragile, irregular paper/binding surfaces; precise pressure control; damage assessment; and packaging decisions — far beyond current humanoid dexterity and perception.

task: Take and prepare to-go orders.
human_time_rationale: One instance = taking one to-go order (noting items, customizations) and preparing/packaging it. Typical fast-casual or diner order takes ~10–15 min end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires natural language order-taking, interpreting customizations, navigating a kitchen, cooking/assembling varied food items, and packaging — far beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capability in unstructured real kitchens.

task: Repair and maintain metal forms and equipment, using hand tools, sledges, and bars.
