robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-micron optical alignment, delicate fiber handling, fragile photonic components, and precise connector mating far beyond current humanoid dexterity. No frontier robot can reliably perform this specialized, tolerance-critical assembly autonomously.

task: Separate products according to weight, grade, size, or composition of materials used to produce them.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sorting a single product/item into the correct category by weight, grade, size, or composition. A competent worker does this in ~1 minute per item.
robot_success_rationale: Vision and weight sensors can classify many products, and pick-and-place is reliable for uniform items. However, varied shapes, compositions, or subtle grade differences challenge perception and generalization on unstructured lines.

task: Monitor finished cartons as they drop from forming machines into rotating hoppers and into gravity feed chutes to prevent jamming.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a continuous 30-minute monitoring shift segment; a competent worker visually watches the flow and intervenes as needed during a typical production block.
robot_success_rationale: Robot can visually detect jams via perception, but physically clearing jams in tight rotating hoppers and chutes requires precise dexterous intervention in cluttered, fast-moving machinery — well beyond reliable 2026 humanoid capability. Safety interlocks further complicate autonomous action.

task: Make preliminary impressions for study casts and occlusal registrations for mounting study casts.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient appointment: seating trays, taking upper/lower alginate impressions, and recording bite registration with wax or polyvinyl siloxane. ~20–35 min for a competent dental assistant/dentist.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise intraoral manipulation, patient cooperation/communication, custom tray fitting, and material timing — all in a confined, sensitive biological environment. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and safety limits.

task: Mix acids, chemicals, or dry cement as required for a specific job.
human_time_rationale: One instance = mixing one batch of material for a specific job; includes measuring, combining, and mixing to correct consistency/ratio, typically 15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading labels, precise measurement of hazardous/variable materials, pouring liquids accurately, and judging mixture consistency. Current humanoids lack reliable liquid handling, chemical safety awareness, and quality-assessment perception needed here.

task: Open valves to return compressed gas to bottoms of specified wells to repressurize them and force oil to surface.
human_time_rationale: One instance = opening valves for one well. Operator locates correct valves, opens them in sequence, confirms pressure readings. Typical field task takes ~10–20 min per well.
robot_success_rationale: Oilfield valve operation requires navigating rugged outdoor terrain, identifying correct valves among many, applying precise torque to large manual valves, and reading pressure gauges — all in unstructured, hazardous environments. Current humanoids lack the outdoor mobility, tool-use reliability, and domain-specific perception needed. Highly specialized controls and safety interlocks further limit autonomy.

task: Apply modular soil- and plant-containing grids over existing roof membranes to create green roofs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a modular green roof grid section (~100 sq ft) on a rooftop. Competent crew members take ~2–3 hrs per section including layout, module placement, soil/plant integration, and edge securing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires rooftop access (stairs/ladders), carrying heavy soil-filled modules, precise placement on uneven membranes, outdoor unstructured environment, wind/weather exposure, and multi-step coordination. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capability.

task: Take and record medical and dental histories and vital signs of patients.
human_time_rationale: One instance = intake for one patient; includes structured interview for medical/dental history plus measuring and recording vitals (BP, HR, temp, O2, weight). Typically 15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires natural conversation to elicit nuanced history, clinical judgment, trusted patient interaction, and precise use of medical instruments (BP cuff, thermometer, scale). Current humanoids lack reliable dexterous medical tool use, voice-based clinical interviewing, and regulatory acceptance for autonomous patient care.

task: Perform general nursery duties, such as propagating standard varieties of plant materials, collecting and germinating seeds, maintaining cuttings of plants, or controlling environmental conditions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one nursery duty cycle (e.g., propagating a batch of cuttings or germinating a seed tray). A competent worker takes ~30–60 min per batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires delicate biological handling (cuttings, seeds), environmental sensing and adjustment, and highly unstructured fine-motor tasks across varied plant types. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, agronomic judgment, and sensor integration needed for reliable autonomous nursery work.

task: Drive machines to work sites.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one trip driving a machine (e.g. excavator, bulldozer) from a staging area to a work site; median trip estimated at ~45 minutes including road/transit time.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot operate heavy machinery or drive vehicles in traffic. No general-purpose robot can legally or physically pilot a bulldozer/excavator on public roads autonomously in 2026.

task: Operate farm machinery, including tractors, plows, mowers, combines, balers, sprayers, earthmoving equipment, or trucks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one operational session (e.g., plowing, harvesting, or spraying a typical field). A competent operator spends ~3–5 hours per session including setup, operation, and shutdown.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot operate tractors, combines, or trucks in real field conditions. These require seated cab operation, specialized controls, outdoor unstructured terrain, and real-time judgment. L4 ag-autonomy systems are narrow/task-specific, not general-purpose humanoids.

task: Inspect locomotives after runs to detect damaged or defective equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full post-run inspection of one locomotive. A competent rail inspector systematically checks undercarriage, brakes, wheels, couplings, engine, and cab systems; typically 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating uneven rail yards, crawling under large equipment, recognizing subtle defects across dozens of component types, and operating in outdoor/industrial environments. Current humanoids lack the specialized locomotion, perception robustness, and defect-recognition expertise needed for autonomous pass/fail judgments.

task: Prepare, care for, and dispose of tissue specimens taken for laboratory analysis.
human_time_rationale: One instance = handling a single tissue specimen: receipt, preparation (labeling, fixation/sectioning), storage or disposal. A trained histotech takes ~10–20 min per specimen.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (sectioning delicate tissue, pipetting reagents), sterile technique, precise chemical handling, label verification, and regulatory-compliant disposal — all far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Install skylights on roofs to increase natural light inside structures or to reduce energy costs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing one skylight unit. A competent roofer typically takes 2–4 hours including cutting the opening, framing, fitting, sealing, and finishing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working on sloped, outdoor rooftop terrain, heavy lifting, precise cutting of roofing materials, complex multi-step sealing/flashing, and structural framing — far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured outdoor environments.

task: Examine products to verify conformance to quality standards.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single product unit on a line. A competent QC worker typically spends 2–8 minutes per unit checking dimensions, surface defects, and conformance criteria.
robot_success_rationale: Vision-based defect detection is maturing, but autonomous physical handling, multi-angle inspection, tactile checks, and pass/fail judgment on varied products in unstructured settings remain challenging for current humanoid platforms.

task: Wash and sterilize equipment, using germicides and sterilizers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = washing and sterilizing a batch of equipment; includes scrubbing, applying germicide, loading sterilizer, and confirming cycle completion. ~20 min for a typical batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling varied, often sharp or irregular instruments, applying liquid chemicals precisely, loading sterilizers with correct orientation, and verifying cycle results. Fine dexterity, chemical handling, and multi-step sequencing remain challenging for current humanoids.

task: Mark or tag identification on parts.
human_time_rationale: One instance = marking/tagging one part (e.g. stamping, labeling, or writing an ID). A competent worker takes ~1–3 minutes per part.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick and place labels or use stamping tools on flat surfaces reasonably well, but varied part geometries, precise placement, and legibility verification challenge current dexterity and perception. Success is context-dependent.

task: Remove and replace tank car dome caps, or direct other workers in their removal and replacement.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing and replacing one tank car dome cap. A competent worker uses tools to unscrew/unseal, inspect, and resecure the cap, typically taking ~10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing atop a tank car (outdoor, curved, slippery surface), applying high torque to large industrial fittings, and coordinating safety-critical steps. Current humanoids lack the mobility, strength, and specialized tool-use for this environment.

task: Train and lead forest and conservation workers in seasonal activities, such as planting tree seedlings, putting out forest fires, and maintaining recreational facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one training/leadership session with a crew for a seasonal activity (e.g., a half-day seedling planting shift). Competent crew leader spends ~4 hours directing, demonstrating, and supervising.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires verbal instruction, adaptive mentorship, crew coordination, and leadership judgment across unstructured outdoor terrain. Current humanoids lack autonomous speech-based training, crew management, and rugged outdoor generalization needed.

task: Inspect trees and collect samples of plants, seeds, foliage, bark, and roots to locate insect and disease damage.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single tree and collecting relevant samples. A competent forester spends ~30–60 min per tree examining canopy, bark, roots, and collecting specimens.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rough terrain navigation, expert visual diagnosis of subtle disease/insect damage, targeted fine-manipulation sample collection (bark, roots, foliage) across varied heights and textures — far beyond 2026 robot generalization and dexterity capabilities.

task: Cook and package batches of food, such as hamburgers or fried chicken, prepared to order or kept warm until sold.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cooking and packaging a single batch (e.g., a burger or portion of fried chicken) to order; includes prep, cooking, and wrapping, ~5–10 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-step cooking (grill/fryer operation, temperature monitoring, flipping), dexterous packaging, and dynamic order management. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation, heat-safe tool use, and kitchen generalization for full autonomous execution.

task: Apply protective coating of fluoride to teeth.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying fluoride varnish or gel to one patient's teeth; includes drying teeth, applying coating, and post-care instructions. Typical dental hygienist time is ~5–15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise intraoral access, fine dexterous brush/tray manipulation on wet, sensitive tissue, patient cooperation management, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and safety standards for dental procedures.

task: Clean and disinfect surgical equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning and disinfecting a full set of surgical instruments from one procedure: rinsing, scrubbing, inspecting, loading into autoclave/chemical disinfectant, and confirming sterility. ~30–60 min is standard.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise fine dexterity to handle delicate instruments, inspection for contamination, exact chemical protocols, autoclave loading, and sterility verification. Current humanoids lack fine manipulation, domain perception, and regulatory-grade reliability for this safety-critical task.

task: Operate large-volume cooking equipment, such as grills, deep-fat fryers, or griddles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single batch/order on large cooking equipment (e.g., grilling 10 burgers or frying a basket of fries), including setup, monitoring, and plating. Typical batch cycle ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Large-volume commercial equipment requires dexterous tool use (tongs, spatulas, fryer baskets), precise thermal judgment, dynamic food positioning, and safety-critical heat/oil management. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation and real-time sensory cooking feedback at this scale.

task: Keep work stations clean and sanitize tools, such as scissors and combs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning and sanitizing a single workstation and its tools (scissors, combs, etc.) between clients; a competent stylist completes this in ~10 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires locating and grasping small, varied tools (scissors, combs), applying sanitizer precisely, and wiping irregular surfaces. Dexterity and fine manipulation of sharp/small objects in an unstructured salon environment are beyond reliable 2026 robot capability.

task: Copy drawings on rough clay or plaster models.
human_time_rationale: One instance = transferring/copying a single drawing onto one rough clay or plaster model surface; a skilled artisan typically takes 30–60 min depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous marking/scribing on irregular, fragile 3D surfaces with high spatial precision. Current robots lack the compliant touch control, surface-adaptive tool pressure, and artistic judgment needed; irregular model geometry compounds perception challenges.

task: Collect data related to ecological or human health risks at brownfield sites.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting environmental/health risk data at a single brownfield site visit, including soil/water sampling, observations, and documentation. Typically 2–4 hours for a competent field technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating hazardous, unstructured outdoor terrain; operating sampling equipment (soil corers, water samplers); following complex protocols; and making contextual judgments. Current humanoids lack the terrain mobility, tool specialization, and autonomous decision-making needed for uncontrolled contaminated sites.

task: Repair, maintain, and adjust equipment, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single repair/maintenance job on a piece of equipment. A competent technician typically spends 30–60 min diagnosing, adjusting, and verifying a repair with hand tools.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diagnosing faults, selecting correct tools, applying calibrated torque, and adapting to highly varied equipment geometries. Current humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity, fault diagnosis, and tool-switching autonomy for unstructured repair tasks.

task: Decontaminate or test field equipment used to clean or test pollutants from soil, air, or water.
human_time_rationale: One instance = decontaminating or testing one set of field equipment (e.g., sampling probes, meters, tubing). A competent technician typically spends 30–60 minutes per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise multi-step chemical rinsing protocols, handling delicate/irregular instruments, reading labels, and verifying cleanliness. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, chemical-handling safety integration, and protocol-following autonomy needed.

task: Operate cash registers to receive payments from patrons.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patron transaction: scanning items, entering amounts, accepting payment, and making change. Typically 1–3 minutes at a typical retail/food counter.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (handling cash, coins, cards), social interaction, reading a POS screen, and adapting to varied payment types. Current humanoids lack reliable cash-handling dexterity and POS system integration for autonomous checkout.

task: Set up equipment and prepare medical treatment rooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one treatment room: laying exam table paper, arranging instruments, stocking supplies, and verifying equipment. A competent medical assistant takes ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise placement of sterile/clinical instruments, restocking varied small items, handling delicate medical equipment, and contextual judgment about room-specific setups. Generalization across diverse tools and hygiene standards far exceeds current robot capability.

task: Conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify or quantify problems, such as asbestos, poor air quality, water contamination, or other environmental hazards.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full inspection of a single site/building. A competent inspector typically spends 1–3 hours surveying, sampling, and documenting hazards such as asbestos, air quality, or water contamination.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-modal sensing (air sampling, material testing, visual survey), expert judgment, navigating complex/hazardous unstructured environments, and generating compliant regulatory reports. Current humanoids lack specialist sensor suites, chemical sampling capability, and inspection-grade decision-making.

task: Shampoo, rinse, condition, and dry hair and scalp or hairpieces with water, liquid soap, or other solutions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = shampooing, rinsing, conditioning, and drying one client's hair/scalp. A competent cosmetologist or caregiver completes this in ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires delicate, adaptive touch on a person's head, precise water temperature/pressure control, managing flexible hairpieces, and safe close-contact caregiving — well beyond 2026 robot dexterity and compliance capabilities.

task: Bleach, dye, or tint hair, using applicator or brush.
human_time_rationale: One full hair coloring/bleaching service on a single client: application (~20 min) plus processing/rinse time (~40 min), typical in a salon setting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise chemical application across an uneven, moving human head; fine dexterity with brush/applicator; safety-critical skin/eye avoidance; working on a live person. Far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Install attachments to machines for gluing, folding, printing, or cutting.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a single attachment (e.g., a gluing or cutting head) onto a machine. A skilled technician typically takes 15–25 min including alignment and securing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise alignment, torque-controlled fastening, and tool-use on varied industrial machinery. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, force feedback, and machine-specific knowledge for reliable autonomous attachment installation.

task: Help production workers by performing duties of lesser skill, such as supplying or holding materials or tools, or cleaning work areas and equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single assist event (supplying/holding materials, tools, or cleaning a work area). Competent helper completes a typical errand/cleanup in ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Simple fetch-and-carry and surface wiping are within frontier robot range, but unstructured factory floors, varied tool shapes, "holding" tasks requiring force control, and reactive coordination with a human worker reduce reliability significantly.

task: Observe hand signals, grade stakes, or other markings when operating machines so that work can be performed to specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single observation/interpretation of a signal, stake, or marking during machine operation; a skilled operator reads and reacts within ~1–2 minutes per cue.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy construction machinery (graders, excavators, dozers) — humanoids cannot drive or control such equipment in the field. Even if perception of signals/stakes were feasible, vehicle operation disqualifies current robots.

task: Select and obtain materials and supplies, such as textbooks and performance pieces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting and retrieving a set of materials (e.g., textbooks, sheet music) for a class or session. Includes locating, evaluating, and physically gathering items.
robot_success_rationale: Requires semantic understanding to identify correct books/pieces by title or content, navigating varied storage environments, and making selection judgments — well beyond current robot generalization and semantic perception capabilities.

task: Shape, bend, or join sections of glass, using paddles, pressing and flattening hand tools, or cork.
human_time_rationale: One instance = shaping/bending/joining one glass section. A skilled glassworker takes ~10–20 min per piece depending on complexity and glass type.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme heat tolerance, precise tactile feedback on molten/hot glass, nuanced force control with specialized tools (paddles, cork), and high dexterity. Current robots lack heat resistance, fine haptic sensing, and the fluid reactive control needed for glasswork.

task: Apply water or setting, straightening or waving solutions to hair, and use curlers, rollers, hot combs and curling irons to press and curl hair.
human_time_rationale: One instance = styling a single client's hair with solutions and heat tools. A competent stylist typically spends 20–30 minutes on this combined chemical-and-heat process.
robot_success_rationale: Requires delicate dexterity on a moving human head, precise heat-tool control near skin, chemical application judgment, and tactile sensitivity. No current humanoid robot handles live-person hair styling safely or reliably.

task: Clean teeth, using dental instruments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full dental cleaning appointment for one patient, including scaling, polishing, and flossing. A competent dental hygienist typically takes 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a small, wet, moving oral cavity; precise force control near sensitive tissue; patient cooperation management; and specialized dental tool use — all far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Attach solar panels to existing roofs, according to specifications and without damaging roofing materials or the structural integrity of buildings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a standard residential solar array (e.g., 8–12 panels) on one roof. A crew of 2 competent installers typically completes this in ~4 hours including racking, mounting, and wiring tie-in.
robot_success_rationale: Requires steep/uneven roof locomotion (severe fall risk), precise torque-controlled fastening into rafters without damaging materials, heavy panel handling, outdoor unstructured environment, and blueprint interpretation — all far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Perform tests to identify any potential hazards related to recycled products used at green building sites.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and testing one batch/type of recycled product at a green building site; a competent inspector typically takes ~60 minutes per material type.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-modal hazard testing (chemical, structural, labeling), contextual judgment, use of specialized instruments, and nuanced reporting. Current robots lack reliable tool-use versatility and domain reasoning for unstructured site inspection of novel materials.

task: Test performance of vehicles that use alternative fuels, such as alcohol blends, natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, biodiesel, nano diesel, or alternative power methods, such as solar energy or hydrogen fuel cells.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full performance test session for one alternative-fuel vehicle, including setup, instrumented test runs, data logging, and post-run checks. Typically 1–3 hours; ~120 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving on test tracks or public roads, operating specialized fuel/power systems, and interpreting live instrumentation — all beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. No frontier humanoid can operate a vehicle in traffic or execute structured test protocols autonomously.

task: Set up equipment for employee use, performing or ensuring proper installation of cables, operating systems, or appropriate software.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up one workstation (PC, monitor, cables, OS/software install). A competent IT technician typically takes 30–60 minutes end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine cable routing, recognizing diverse port types, and installing/configuring software—combining dexterous manipulation with complex cognitive IT tasks far beyond current humanoid capabilities. No viable 2026 system handles this autonomously.

task: Clean and polish removable appliances.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning and polishing one removable appliance (e.g., toaster, blender base). Involves disassembly if needed, wiping, polishing, and reassembly.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying, safely handling, and polishing varied appliance surfaces with correct pressure and product. Fine dexterity for small parts, disassembly/reassembly, and generalization across appliance types are challenging for current humanoids.

task: Display students' work in schools, galleries, and exhibitions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a single display of student work (e.g., hanging artwork, arranging 3D pieces, placing labels) in one venue. A competent adult takes ~90 min for a typical classroom/gallery section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise wall-mounting, drilling, leveling, reading labels, navigating varied/unstructured spaces, and aesthetic judgment. Current humanoids lack fine tool-use for hanging hardware, planning, and curatorial decision-making.

task: Lower gauge rods into tanks or read meters to verify contents, temperatures, and volumes of liquid loads.
human_time_rationale: One instance = gauging one tank. Worker lowers rod or reads meter, notes temperature/volume, and records result. Typically 3–7 minutes per tank.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating outdoor/industrial terrain to tank hatches, manipulating gauge rods or meter valves with precision, reading analog/digital gauges, and safely handling hazardous liquids. Unstructured environments, fine tool-use, and safety constraints make this very difficult for current humanoids.

task: Remove products, machine attachments, or waste material from machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing one product/attachment/waste batch from a machine. A competent worker takes ~2–5 min depending on machine type and item size.
robot_success_rationale: Simple pick-and-place from a known machine fixture is feasible (~70%), but variability in machine types, attachments, confined spaces, heavy/awkward items, and safety interlocks reduces confidence significantly for general-purpose humanoids.

task: Perform general warehouse activities, such as opening containers and crates, filling warehouse orders, assisting in taking inventory, and weighing and checking materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a single warehouse activity cycle (e.g., fulfilling one order or processing one incoming shipment), including opening, picking, weighing, and checking. ~20–30 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Mix of sub-tasks varies widely in difficulty. Pick-and-place is manageable (~70%), but opening containers/crates, operating weighing equipment, interpreting order sheets, and navigating cluttered warehouse aisles compound failure rates significantly. Generalization across varied item types and packaging is a key limitation.

task: Connect ground cables to carry off static electricity when unloading tanker cars.
human_time_rationale: One instance = connecting grounding cables to one tanker car before unloading. A worker retrieves the cable, clamps it to the car's grounding lug, and verifies connection — typically 3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating uneven rail-yard terrain, locating a specific grounding lug on a large industrial vehicle, and applying a spring-loaded or screw clamp with precise force. Outdoor unstructured environment, heavy cable handling, and safety-critical verification push success low for current humanoids.

task: Operate locomotives to transport freight or passengers between stations or to assemble or disassemble trains within rail yards.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one typical freight or passenger run between stations, or one yard assembly/disassembly session. Median intercity/freight run ~2–4 hours; yard work ~1–2 hours. ~180 min is a reasonable midpoint.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot operate locomotive controls in a moving train on live rail. Requires highly specialized cab operation, situational awareness, regulatory compliance, and emergency response. No humanoid platform is capable of this task autonomously.

task: Inspect, adjust, or control radio equipment or airport lights.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single inspection/adjustment cycle of radio equipment or airport lighting system; a competent technician/controller typically takes ~15–25 minutes per check.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized aviation control panels, reading analog/digital gauges, and making precise adjustments. Current humanoids lack aviation-specific tool-use, interface familiarity, and regulatory-grade reliability for safety-critical avionics tasks.

task: Operate industrial trucks, tractors, loaders, and other equipment to transport materials to and from transportation vehicles and loading docks, and to store and retrieve materials in warehouses.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one material transport or storage/retrieval cycle (e.g., forklift load from dock to rack). A competent operator completes a typical cycle in ~20–40 min.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot operate forklifts, pallet jacks, or industrial trucks autonomously in live warehouse environments. Requires vehicle operation, spatial navigation under load, and safety compliance — far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Move materials over short distances, such as around a construction site, factory, or warehouse.
human_time_rationale: One instance = moving a load from point A to point B within a site (e.g., 50–200 m). A competent worker with a hand truck or forklift takes ~10 min including loading/unloading.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoids can carry objects and navigate flat indoor terrain reasonably well, but unstructured construction/warehouse environments, varied payloads, obstacle avoidance, and loading/unloading steps significantly reduce reliable autonomous success.

task: Disassemble axles to repair, adjust, and replace defective parts, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = disassembling, repairing/adjusting/replacing parts, and reassembling one axle. A competent mechanic typically takes 60–120 minutes depending on axle type and defect.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-torque tool use (impact wrenches, pry bars), precise force application on corroded/seized parts, diagnostic judgment on defects, and complex dexterous manipulation in tight spaces — well beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capability.

task: Mix food, liquid formulas, medications, or food supplements according to instructions, prescriptions, and knowledge of animal species.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one batch/serving mixed per prescription or instructions. Measuring, combining, and checking quality takes a competent worker ~10–15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement of varied ingredients, reading labels/prescriptions, species-specific knowledge, and fine manipulation of liquids/powders. Current robots lack reliable liquid handling, label reading in context, and domain knowledge integration for safe pharmaceutical/nutritional mixing.

task: Mix ingredients according to specified procedures or formulas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one mixing batch per a specified formula (e.g., a dough, batter, or chemical blend). Gathering, measuring, and combining ingredients typically takes 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots struggle with measuring precise quantities, pouring liquids without spillage, reading/interpreting varied formulas, and adapting mixing technique (folding vs. whisking). Generalization across recipes is very limited in 2026 systems.

task: Unload cars containing liquids by connecting hoses to outlet plugs and pumping compressed air into cars to force liquids into storage tanks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = unloading one rail tank car. A competent worker connects hoses, pressurizes the car, monitors transfer, and disconnects — typically 30–60 minutes depending on volume.
robot_success_rationale: Requires heavy industrial hose handling, precise coupling of large pressurized fittings, operating compressed-air systems, and safety-critical valve management outdoors. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, force capability, and generalization in unstructured industrial environments.

task: Study historical climate change indicators found in locations, such as ice sheets or rock formations to develop climate change models.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full field study session at a single location (e.g., ice core site or rock outcrop), including travel, sampling, documentation, and preliminary analysis. Typically a full workday (~8 hrs).
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme-environment mobility (glaciers, remote rock formations), precise scientific sampling (ice cores, sediment), complex instrument use, and high-level geological/climate expertise. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured, hazardous outdoor terrain.

task: Demonstrate activities to children.
human_time_rationale: One instance = demonstrating a single activity (e.g., craft, game, exercise) to a group of children; typically takes ~10 minutes including setup and explanation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires adaptive social interaction, reading children's engagement, dynamic communication, and responsive improvisation. Current robots lack robust child-safe social intelligence, expressive communication, and real-time pedagogical adjustment needed for acceptable quality.

task: Design or perform experiments with equipment, such as lasers, accelerators, or mass spectrometers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and running a single experiment session (e.g., aligning a laser, running samples through a mass spectrometer). A typical session takes 1–3 hours for a competent researcher.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert-level cognitive planning, precise optical/instrument alignment (sub-mm), interpreting live data, adjusting parameters, and operating highly specialized controls. Far beyond current general-purpose humanoid capabilities.

task: Check conditions and weights of vessels to ensure cleanliness and compliance with loading procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single vessel (tank, container, or hold) for cleanliness and load compliance; a competent inspector typically takes 15–25 minutes per vessel.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating confined, uneven maritime spaces, visual/olfactory cleanliness assessment, reading load markings, and judgment calls on compliance — all poorly handled by current humanoids. Perception and generalization limits dominate.

task: Remove or limit contamination following emergencies involving hazardous substances.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single hazmat decontamination response. Trained responders typically spend 2–5 hours containing/removing contamination at an emergency scene; 4 hrs is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires specialized PPE/SCBA that humanoids cannot don, operation of pumps/vacuums/decon equipment in chaotic outdoor environments, chemical hazard sensing, regulatory decision-making, and coordination with emergency teams — far beyond 2026 frontier capability.

task: Thin and space trees and control weeds and undergrowth, using manual tools and chemicals, or supervise workers performing these tasks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a half-day work session covering a typical forest plot or stand. Thinning, spacing, and weed control with manual tools and chemicals is physically intensive and takes several hours per unit area.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rough-terrain mobility, operating chainsaws/brush cutters, identifying target vs. non-target vegetation, and applying chemicals safely — all far beyond 2026 humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured forest environments.

task: Conduct insect or plant disease surveys.
human_time_rationale: One instance = surveying a single field or plot. A competent agronomist/entomologist typically spends 1–3 hours walking transects, sampling, and recording observations per field visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor locomotion on uneven terrain, fine visual identification of subtle pest/disease symptoms on diverse crops, nuanced sampling decisions, and tool use. Current robots lack reliable agricultural perception, generalization, and rough-terrain mobility for this complex survey task.

task: Receive starting signals from conductors and use controls such as throttles or air brakes to drive electric, diesel-electric, steam, or gas turbine-electric locomotives.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one typical train run/trip. A standard freight or passenger rail run averages ~2–4 hours; 180 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Locomotive cabs are not designed for humanoid operation; throttle/brake controls require seated human interface. Rail automation exists but uses dedicated ATO systems, not general-purpose humanoid robots. Perception, safety, and regulatory barriers are extreme.

task: Attach pumps and hoses to wellheads.
human_time_rationale: One instance = connecting a pump and hose assembly to a single wellhead. Requires fitting alignment, torqueing connections, and checking seals; a skilled worker takes ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Wellhead environments are outdoor/unstructured with heavy, dirty, non-standardized hardware requiring high torque, precise thread engagement, and tool use. Current humanoids lack the force, specialized tools, and robustness for oilfield assembly.

task: Perform reforestation or forest renewal, including nursery and silviculture operations, site preparation, seeding and tree planting programs, cone collection, and tree improvement.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full reforestation work shift (~8 hrs) covering site prep, planting, seeding, or nursery ops. A manager measures daily crew output across these activities.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rough-terrain locomotion, digging, precise seedling handling, cone collection, and multi-step silviculture judgment across highly unstructured forest environments. Far beyond 2026 frontier robot capabilities in combination.

task: Start pumps and adjust valves or cables to regulate the flow of products to vessels, using knowledge of loading procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and managing a single vessel loading operation: starting pumps, adjusting valves/cables, and confirming stable flow. Typical duration for a competent operator is ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized industrial pumps, valves, and cable controls in a maritime/industrial environment, interpreting flow gauges, and applying domain-specific loading knowledge. Current humanoids lack the specialized tool-use, fluid-system reasoning, and hazardous-environment certification needed.

task: Inspect locomotives to verify adequate fuel, sand, water, or other supplies before each run or to check for mechanical problems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = pre-run inspection of a single locomotive; includes walking around, checking fuel/sand/water levels and mechanical components — typically 20–40 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a large, complex industrial vehicle with many specialized gauges, valves, and access panels; reading analog/digital displays; climbing ladders; operating in outdoor/rail-yard terrain. Current humanoids lack the mobility, specialized perception, and domain-specific tool-use for this environment.

task: Apply reflective roof coatings, such as special paints or single-ply roofing sheets, to existing roofs to reduce solar heat absorption.
human_time_rationale: One instance = coating one average residential roof (~150 m²). A 2-person crew takes ~4 hours including prep, rolling/spraying paint or laying single-ply sheets, and cleanup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor climbing/traversal on uneven, sloped, slippery rooftop surfaces, handling large rolls of sheet material or spray equipment, consistent coverage quality, and edge-awareness for safety — all far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Capture or segregate waste material, such as aluminum swarf, machine cutting fluid, or solvents, for recycling or environmentally responsible disposal.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting and segregating waste materials (swarf, cutting fluid, solvents) from a single machine or workstation into labeled containers; a routine end-of-shift task.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling loose, sharp metallic swarf, liquid fluids/solvents in varied containers, correct stream segregation, and label recognition. Perception of wet/oily materials, fine debris, and unstructured container types challenges current robots significantly.

task: Administer therapeutic medication and advise patron to seek medical treatment for chronic or contagious scalp conditions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying a therapeutic scalp treatment to one patron and verbally advising them on their condition. A skilled cosmetologist/barber completes this in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous scalp manipulation, visual/tactile diagnosis of skin conditions, natural language advisory communication, and clinical judgment — all well beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. Safe medication handling on a human scalp adds safety constraints.

task: Trim, file, grind, deburr, buff, or smooth surfaces, seams, or joints of assembled parts, using hand tools or portable power tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = finishing one assembled part/joint. A competent worker spends ~15–25 min selecting the right tool, inspecting seams, and iteratively grinding/buffing to acceptable quality.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine force-controlled dexterity with hand/power tools, adaptive pressure sensing, and visual inspection of subtle surface defects. Current humanoids lack the precise compliance control and tool-switching judgment needed for acceptable finish quality.

task: Open and close doors, windows, or drawers to test their operation, trimming edges to fit, as necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one door/window/drawer: open/close several times, assess fit, trim edges if needed. Trimming adds time; simple check is ~2 min, trimming ~10–15 min; median ~8 min.
robot_success_rationale: Opening handles is feasible (~40–70%), but diagnosing fit issues, deciding whether trimming is needed, and then precisely operating a hand plane or saw on edges requires high-level judgment, fine dexterity, and tool use well beyond 2026 frontier capability.

task: Serve meals and snacks in accordance with nutritional guidelines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = serving a single meal or snack to one person/group, including plating, portioning, and delivering to the table or tray. ~20 min covers prep-to-serve.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nutritional knowledge integration, fine dexterous plating, liquid handling, portioning judgment, and navigating to recipients. Current humanoids lack reliable food-safe manipulation, dietary compliance reasoning, and unstructured kitchen generalization.

task: Apply bioremediation techniques to hazardous wastes to allow naturally occurring bacteria to break down toxic substances.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and applying bioremediation treatment to a single contaminated site/area, including sampling, nutrient/amendment addition, and monitoring setup. Typically 1–3 hours of active fieldwork.
robot_success_rationale: Requires unstructured outdoor terrain navigation, precise liquid/amendment injection, soil sampling, and complex procedural judgment. Current humanoids lack the specialized tools, chemical handling capability, and environmental adaptability for autonomous bioremediation fieldwork.

task: Arrange indoor and outdoor space to facilitate creative play, motor-skill activities, and safety.
human_time_rationale: One instance = arranging one indoor or outdoor space (e.g., a classroom or playground area). A competent worker moves furniture/equipment, sets up activity zones, and checks hazards.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-level spatial planning, semantic understanding of child safety and play zones, moving diverse heavy/bulky furniture, and judgment calls. Current robots lack the planning, generalization, and heavy manipulation needed.

task: Observe positions of cars passing loading spouts, and swing spouts into the correct positions at the appropriate times.
human_time_rationale: One instance = positioning the spout for one vehicle. Worker observes car arrival, judges alignment, and swings spout into place; roughly 2–4 minutes per car.
robot_success_rationale: Requires robust outdoor/industrial perception to track moving vehicles, precise timing, and forceful manipulation of a large swinging spout. Current humanoids lack the strength, reach, and reliability for heavy industrial spout handling in dynamic environments.

task: Design or build laboratory equipment needed for special research projects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = designing/building a single custom lab apparatus for a research project. Ranges from hours to days; ~8 hrs is a reasonable median for a skilled technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative engineering design, interpretation of abstract research requirements, fine dexterous fabrication, tool use across diverse materials, and high-level reasoning. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications, using instruments such as micrometers, calipers, magnifiers, or rulers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single part/product: pick up, visually inspect, measure with 2–3 instruments (caliper, micrometer, ruler), record result. ~5–10 min for a competent inspector.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can perceive and move objects but precisely handling and reading precision instruments (micrometers, calipers) requires fine dexterity and tactile feedback beyond current frontier humanoids. Tool manipulation and measurement recording remain unreliable.

task: Organize and label materials and display students' work in a manner appropriate for their ages and perceptual skills.
human_time_rationale: One instance = organizing and labeling materials plus mounting/displaying a class set of student work in a classroom. Covers sorting, labeling, and arranging ~25–30 items.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading developmental context, making age-appropriate aesthetic/pedagogical judgments, fine motor labeling, pinning/hanging varied items at varied heights, and adapting to unstructured classroom layouts — far beyond 2026 robot generalization and cognitive planning.

task: Seal open sides of modular units to prepare them for shipment, using polyethylene sheets, nails, and hammers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sealing one modular unit's open sides. A competent worker measures, cuts, and nails polyethylene sheeting across multiple open faces; ~20–30 min per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling large, flexible, lightweight polyethylene sheets (difficult for robots), accurate nailing into edges, and coordinated two-handed manipulation over wide surface areas. Current humanoids lack reliable flexible-material handling and precise nailing at scale.

task: Clean interiors of tank cars or tank trucks, using mechanical spray nozzles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one tank car/truck interior. Setup, spray cycle, inspection, and breakdown typically takes ~45–90 minutes for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires entering a confined, curved, hazardous vessel; positioning and operating industrial spray nozzles; tolerating chemical exposure. Current humanoids lack confined-space mobility, chemical resistance, and the specialized tool-use required for autonomous tank cleaning.

task: Inspect facilities or installations to determine their environmental impact.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single facility inspection visit. A competent environmental inspector typically spends 2–5 hours on-site assessing equipment, sampling, reviewing logs, and documenting findings.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert judgment, regulatory knowledge, multi-modal sampling (air, water, soil), reading gauges/documents, and traversing varied industrial terrain. No current humanoid robot can autonomously integrate these complex cognitive and physical demands at acceptable quality.

task: Assemble prototypes or integrated-technology demonstrators of new or emerging environmental technologies for aircraft.
human_time_rationale: One prototype/demonstrator assembly for a novel aircraft environmental tech system; a skilled aerospace technician typically spends a full 8-hour shift per unit, accounting for complex integration, wiring, and testing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly specialized, non-standardized components, fine dexterous wiring and connector work, domain expertise in aerospace engineering, iterative problem-solving, and use of specialized tools — far beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Distribute programs to patrons.
human_time_rationale: One instance = handing a single program booklet to one patron. A competent human picks it up and hands it over in ~30 seconds.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a crowd, detecting an approaching patron, grasping a thin paper booklet, and handing it over smoothly. Thin-object grasping, human interaction, and dynamic crowd navigation remain challenging for 2026 humanoids.

task: Demonstrate activities to children.
human_time_rationale: One instance = demonstrating a single activity (e.g., a craft, game, or exercise) to a group of children; typically 5–15 minutes including setup and engagement.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic social interaction, reading children's reactions, adaptive communication, and engaging behavior. Current robots lack natural child-directed expression, spontaneous feedback, and safe, trusted interaction in unstructured child environments.

task: Drive and control rail-guided public transportation, such as subways, elevated trains, and electric-powered streetcars, trams, or trolleys, to transport passengers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one operator shift/route run (~4-hour average revenue service block for a subway/tram operator, a standard measurable unit for transit agencies).
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot physically operate train/tram controls in live public service. Requires seated cab operation, complex safety protocols, emergency response, and passenger interaction. Narrow ATO systems exist but are not general-purpose humanoid robots.

task: Insert processed negatives and prints into envelopes for delivery to customers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inserting one order's negatives and prints into an envelope. A competent worker handles this in ~1–3 minutes including organizing and sealing.
robot_success_rationale: Handling thin, flexible film negatives and photo prints requires precise, delicate dexterity. Inserting limp flat materials into paper envelopes without bending or misaligning is challenging for current humanoids; perception of thin transparent film adds difficulty.

task: Operate conveyors and equipment to transfer grain or other materials from transportation vehicles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = unloading one transportation vehicle (e.g., grain truck). A competent operator sets up, runs, and shuts down conveyor equipment; typical truck unload takes ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating large industrial conveyor controls, connecting augers/spouts to vehicles, monitoring flow, and adjusting settings. Humanoids cannot safely operate this specialized heavy equipment autonomously in unstructured outdoor/industrial grain facility environments.

task: Demonstrate and sell hair care products and cosmetics.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single product demonstration/sales pitch to a customer, covering product application, explanation, and closing; ~30 min is typical for a beauty consultant.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social persuasion, reading customer reactions, fine dexterous cosmetic application on a person, and live sales improvisation — all far beyond current humanoid capabilities. Safety and consent issues compound difficulty.

task: Comb, brush, and spray hair or wigs to set style.
human_time_rationale: One styling session on a single client/wig — combing, brushing, and spraying to set a finished style; typical for a salon or cosmetology setting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly dexterous, force-sensitive manipulation of hair near a human head, wielding multiple tools (comb, brush, spray can), and aesthetic judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability.

task: Observe patients' vital signs to assess physical condition.
human_time_rationale: One instance = observing and recording a full set of vital signs (BP, pulse, temp, SpO2, respiration) for one patient; a competent nurse typically completes this in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots lack reliable physical interfaces for BP cuffs, thermometers, and stethoscopes on uncooperative/varied patients. Sensor placement requires dexterous contact, patient communication, and clinical judgment—well beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capability.

task: Place glass into dies or molds of presses and control presses to form products, such as glassware components or optical blanks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = loading glass into a die/mold and operating the press through one press cycle. A skilled operator typically completes this in 2–4 minutes per piece.
robot_success_rationale: Handling hot, irregular, or molten glass gobs requires high heat tolerance and precise placement in tight molds. Press controls vary widely. Generalization to unstructured glassware environments is poor for current humanoids; gripper and thermal limits are significant barriers.

task: Provide a variety of materials and resources for children to explore, manipulate, and use, both in learning activities and in imaginative play.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a variety of materials/resources for one session. A competent educator spends ~20 min selecting, arranging, and preparing materials across activity and play areas.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced pedagogical judgment about age-appropriateness, child interests, and safety, plus navigating varied storage areas and arranging diverse materials creatively. Far beyond current robot generalization and contextual reasoning.

task: Assemble boilers at installation sites, using tools such as levels, plumb bobs, hammers, torches, or other hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assembling a single boiler unit on-site. A competent boilermaker/HVAC tech typically takes a full shift (6–8 hrs) accounting for alignment, fitting, and sealing steps.
robot_success_rationale: Requires heavy-load manipulation, precise leveling with multiple hand tools, torch work, and adaptability to unstructured installation sites. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, strength, tool-switching, and safety compliance capabilities.

task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One full cycle (select, store, order, issue, inventory) for a classroom's supplies — a periodic task typically measured per session or restocking cycle, ~45 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-step cognitive planning (ordering, cataloging), system access (procurement software), fine manipulation of varied items, and contextual judgment about need. Far beyond current autonomous generalist robot capability.

task: Assimilate arriving children to the school environment by greeting them, helping them remove outerwear, and selecting activities of interest to them.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one child's arrival routine: greeting, removing coat/backpack, and guiding them to an activity. Typically takes 5–10 minutes per child.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social-emotional warmth, child-specific rapport, fine dexterous help with zippers/buttons, and personalized activity selection. Current robots lack child-safe interaction norms, reliable dexterity on small fasteners, and nuanced preference inference.

task: Direct measurement of the intensity or types of radiation in work areas, equipment, or materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = surveying a single work area or piece of equipment with a radiation detector, logging readings. A competent health physicist takes ~15–25 minutes including setup and documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Robot must navigate radiation zones, correctly handle/position specialized detectors (Geiger counters, dosimeters), interpret readings, and document results. Sensor integration and unstructured environment navigation remain major barriers; no commercial deployments exist for this task.

task: Install attic ventilation systems, such as turbine vents, gable or ridge vents, or conventional or solar-powered exhaust fans.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a single attic vent unit (e.g., one ridge vent, turbine, or exhaust fan). A competent roofer/installer typically takes 1–2 hours per unit including cutting, fitting, sealing, and securing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires working at height on sloped roof surfaces, cutting roofing material, precise fitting and weatherproof sealing, and handling varied vent types. Current humanoids lack safe roof locomotion, roofing tool use, and outdoor unstructured environment generalization.

task: Install and perform minor repairs to hardware, software, or peripheral equipment, following design or installation specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single hardware/software install or minor repair (e.g., swapping a RAM stick, replacing a peripheral, running a driver update). A competent tech typically takes 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (small screws, connectors, cables), logical troubleshooting, reading system diagnostics, and software interaction. Current humanoids lack reliable fine manipulation and cognitive IT reasoning for unstructured repair tasks.

task: Sort specialized hazardous waste at landfills or disposal centers, following proper disposal procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sorting one batch/load of hazardous waste items at a disposal center; a competent worker processes a typical incoming batch in ~30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying unknown, unlabeled, or poorly labeled hazardous materials by sight/smell/chemical markings, applying regulatory knowledge, and handling dangerous substances safely. Beyond current robot perception, generalization, and safety compliance capabilities.

task: Operate controls to open and close transit vehicle doors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one door open/close cycle at a stop. A trained operator presses a button or flips a lever; takes ~20–30 seconds per stop.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating transit vehicle controls (levers, buttons, panels) inside a moving/stopped vehicle. Humanoids lack vehicle integration, positional stability in a cab, and reliable fine interaction with transit-specific control interfaces at service quality.

task: Assimilate arriving children to the school environment by greeting them, helping them remove outerwear, and selecting activities of interest to them.
human_time_rationale: One instance = greeting one child at arrival: warm verbal welcome, helping remove coat/backpack, and guiding them to a suitable activity. Typically 5–10 minutes per child.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social/emotional perception, child-safe physical interaction (unzipping, removing coat), preference elicitation, and adaptive communication with young children — far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities in unstructured, dynamic caregiving settings.

task: Massage and treat scalp for hygienic and remedial purposes, using hands, fingers, or vibrating equipment.
human_time_rationale: One scalp massage/treatment session per client, typically 10–20 minutes in a cosmetology or trichology setting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly sensitive, compliant touch on a human head — precise force control, real-time tactile feedback, and safety-critical dexterity far beyond current humanoid capability. Risk of injury raises the bar even further.

task: Perform building commissioning activities by completing mechanical inspections of a building's water, lighting, or heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full mechanical inspection of one building system (water, lighting, or HVAC) during commissioning. A competent commissioning agent typically takes 2–4 hours per system per visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating complex multi-floor mechanical spaces, reading gauges/panels, operating valves and controls, interpreting anomalies, and documenting findings to professional standards. Current robots lack the generalization, judgment, and tool-use breadth for autonomous commissioning.

task: Draw patterns for articles designed, cut patterns, and cut material according to patterns, using measuring instruments and scissors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = drafting, cutting pattern, and cutting fabric for a single garment/article. A skilled tailor/seamstress typically takes 30–60 min for this combined workflow.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise drafting with measuring tools, freehand curved-line drawing, and controlled scissor use on flexible fabric — all demanding fine dexterity and spatial reasoning well beyond current humanoid capability. Generalization to varied patterns is also extremely difficult.

task: Position, attach, or blow insulating materials to prevent energy losses from buildings, pipes, or other structures or objects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = insulating one typical section (e.g., a wall cavity, pipe run, or attic bay). A competent insulation worker takes ~45–90 min per section depending on material and access.
robot_success_rationale: Requires spray/blow equipment operation, navigating tight/overhead spaces, precise material placement, and judgment about coverage quality. Current humanoids lack the tool-use dexterity, equipment interface, and unstructured-environment generalization needed.

task: Fabricate ducts for high efficiency heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems to maximize efficiency of systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fabricating one duct section; cutting, forming, and seaming sheet metal typically takes a skilled sheet metal worker 60–120 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise sheet metal cutting, bending, seaming, and measuring to tight tolerances using specialized tools (shears, brakes, roll formers). Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, tool-use, and generalization capabilities.

task: Operate machines to process materials in compliance with applicable safety, energy, or environmental regulations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one processing run/shift segment (e.g., loading, running, and monitoring a machine through one cycle). Competent operators typically manage a cycle in ~60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized industrial machinery, reading gauges, adjusting controls, and ensuring regulatory compliance. Humanoid robots cannot operate most heavy processing equipment autonomously, lack safety-compliance judgment, and cannot interface with machine controls reliably.

task: Inspect equipment and read order sheets to prepare for delivery to users.
human_time_rationale: One instance = reviewing one delivery batch: checking equipment condition and reading associated order sheets before dispatching. Typically 10–20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading/interpreting printed or digital order sheets (OCR + comprehension), visually inspecting varied equipment for defects, and cross-referencing — challenging perception, language understanding, and generalization for current humanoids.

task: Set up or inspect equipment prior to operation.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up or inspecting one piece of equipment before operation. Competent worker typically spends 15–25 min on checks, adjustments, and readiness confirmation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying diverse equipment types, reading gauges/displays, manipulating varied controls, and making judgment calls about readiness. Current robots lack reliable generalization across equipment types, fine perception of gauges, and contextual fault reasoning.

task: Operate auxiliary machines or equipment used in coating or painting processes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up/operating auxiliary coating/painting equipment (e.g., mixers, spray guns, conveyors) for a single production run or batch; ~20 min median per cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating diverse, specialized industrial machinery (valves, mixers, control panels, spray systems). Humanoids lack reliable tool-use generalization, fine motor control for calibration, and domain-specific perception needed for quality assurance in coating processes.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single interaction where a staff member provides an assistive device, sets up tech, or helps a student access a facility (e.g., restroom assistance). Median ~20 min including setup and support.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex social judgment, personalized communication, safe physical contact with a vulnerable person, fine device setup, and facility navigation. Current robots lack reliable safe caregiving, nuanced interaction, and adaptive physical assistance for diverse disabilities.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one class session. Includes gathering/arranging materials, setting up furniture, and distributing supplies; typically 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a varied classroom, rearranging furniture, identifying and placing diverse materials, and interpreting activity-specific instructions. Current humanoids lack reliable generalization for multi-step, context-dependent classroom setup tasks.

task: Measure vehicle emissions to determine whether they are within acceptable limits.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one vehicle emissions test. A technician connects a probe, runs the engine through test cycles, and reads results — typically 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires locating and inserting a tailpipe probe, operating diagnostic equipment, interfacing with vehicle controls, and interpreting results. Current humanoids lack the precise tool-use, vehicle-interface capability, and procedural generalization needed across varied vehicles.

task: Maintain equipment used in a particular sport.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full maintenance session for a sport's equipment set (e.g., sharpening skates, stringing a racket, inflating/inspecting balls). A competent equipment manager takes ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Highly varied tasks per sport (sharpening, stringing, inflating, patching, taping) requiring fine dexterity, tool-use, and domain-specific knowledge. Current humanoids lack the precision tooling and generalization needed across equipment types.

task: Transplant trees, vegetables, or horticultural plants.
human_time_rationale: One instance = transplanting a single plant (digging hole, placing, backfilling, watering in). A competent worker averages ~10–20 min depending on plant size.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor uneven terrain navigation, forceful digging with tools, gentle bimanual handling of fragile root balls, and depth/placement judgement. Current humanoids lack reliable outdoor mobility, tool-force control, and plant-damage-aware grasping.

task: Prepare materials, classrooms, and other indoor and outdoor spaces to facilitate creative play, learning and motor-skill activities, and safety.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single classroom/space for a session. A competent educator/aide typically spends ~20–40 min arranging materials, furniture, and safety checks.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diverse sub-tasks: carrying/arranging furniture, setting out varied craft/play materials, safety-checking outdoor equipment, and contextual judgment about age-appropriateness. Current robots lack reliable generalization across all these unstructured, multi-step, context-dependent actions.

task: Operate, control, or monitor integrated gasification combined cycle (IGCC) or related equipment, such as air separation units, to generate electricity from coal.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one 8-hour shift of continuous monitoring and control of an IGCC plant. Operators work in shifts to maintain uninterrupted plant operation.
robot_success_rationale: IGCC operation requires interpreting complex multi-system control panels, making nuanced process adjustments, responding to alarms, and coordinating with plant personnel. No humanoid robot has the specialized perception, cognition, or certified control interfaces for industrial power plant operation. Highly regulated, safety-critical environment.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single assistance interaction (e.g., setting up a device, guiding to restroom, adjusting technology). Median across varied interactions is ~20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social understanding, dynamic trust-building, fine dexterity for varied assistive devices, safe physical guidance of humans with disabilities, and adaptive communication — all far beyond current humanoid generalization and safety standards.

task: Monitor gauges or meters that measure speed, amperage, battery charge, or air pressure in brake lines or in main reservoirs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single monitoring check/scan of the gauge panel (speed, amperage, brake pressure, battery). A competent operator glances and reads all meters in ~1–2 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Reading analog/digital gauges via vision is feasible (~50–70%), but this task is embedded in operating a vehicle or large machinery (train, truck, industrial equipment) that a humanoid cannot drive or control, severely limiting autonomous success.

task: Select and assemble books, materials, supplies, and equipment for training, courses, or projects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = gathering and assembling all materials for a single training course or project. A competent worker spends ~30–60 min selecting, locating, and staging varied items.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading/interpreting a course list, navigating varied storage areas, identifying correct books and supplies by title/label, and making judgment calls. Current robots lack reliable semantic search, text comprehension, and multi-room retrieval at this complexity.

task: Operate machinery to perform activities such as backfilling excavations, vibrating or breaking rock or concrete, or making winter roads.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a single discrete job (e.g., backfilling one excavation or breaking a concrete section). A competent operator typically takes 1–2 hours per unit job.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized heavy equipment (excavators, compactors, breakers) in unstructured outdoor terrain. Humanoid robots cannot drive or control such machinery autonomously; far beyond 2026 frontier capabilities.

task: Develop or execute tests to detect diseases, genetic disorders, or other abnormalities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = running a single diagnostic test panel (e.g., PCR, blood panel, genetic assay) from sample prep through result interpretation; ~30–60 min is typical for a lab technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine pipetting, sterile technique, calibrated instrument operation, and clinical judgment for result interpretation. Current humanoid robots lack the precision, lab-instrument interfacing, and cognitive reasoning needed for autonomous diagnostic testing.

task: Inspect facilities, supplies, vehicles, and equipment to ensure conformance to standards.
human_time_rationale: One inspection round covering a typical facility, its supplies, vehicles, and equipment — e.g., a shift safety walkthrough. A competent inspector averages ~45 min per round.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating varied indoor/outdoor terrain, reading gauges/labels, checking vehicle interiors, and applying compliance standards. Current robots lack reliable multi-environment mobility, generalized visual inspection judgment, and tool/gauge reading accuracy needed for autonomous sign-off.

task: Disinfect or sterilize equipment or supplies, using germicides or sterilizing equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = disinfecting/sterilizing a batch of equipment. A competent worker spends ~5 min prepping/loading, ~10 min cycle time, ~5 min unloading and confirming sterility.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying varied equipment, applying germicide correctly or loading an autoclave, and verifying sterility indicators. Unstructured tool-use, chemical handling, and quality verification are well beyond current humanoid generalization; high error risk.

task: Sample and test air to identify gasses, such as bromine, ozone, or sulfur dioxide, or particulates, such as mold, dust, or allergens.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting air samples at a site and running initial tests. Includes setup, sampling at multiple points, and logging results; ~30–60 min typical for an environmental technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires carrying and operating specialized sampling instruments (pumps, impingers, sensors), precise procedural steps, and interpretation of results. Humanoids lack integrated lab-instrument control, calibration routines, and domain-specific tool-use for this workflow.

task: Cut products to specified dimensions, using hand or power cutters.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cutting one piece to spec. Worker measures, marks, aligns, and cuts; ~3–7 min median depending on material and tool.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement, material alignment, and controlled tool use (e.g. circular saw, shears). Robots lack reliable fine dexterity for measuring/marking, safe power-tool handling, and generalization across varied materials and cutter types.

task: Make special pickups of recyclable materials, such as food scraps, used oil, discarded computers, or other electronic items.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one special pickup stop/visit: travel to location, collect varied recyclable materials (e-waste, oil, food scraps), load into vehicle, depart. ~45 min median per stop.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving a collection vehicle on public roads (humanoids cannot), handling highly varied, irregular, and hazardous items (oil containers, e-waste), coordinating logistics, and navigating unpredictable outdoor environments — all far beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Climb trees, using climbing hooks and belts, or climb ladders to gain access to work areas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = ascending to a work area in a single tree or on a ladder. A competent arborist/lineman takes ~5–10 min to gear up hooks/belt and climb to working height.
robot_success_rationale: Climbing trees with hooks/belts requires extreme dexterous tool-use, dynamic balance on irregular surfaces, and full-body coordination far beyond current humanoid capability. Ladder climbing is partially demonstrated in labs but unreliable outdoors at height.

task: Attach slings, ropes, or cables to objects such as pipes, hoses, or bundles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = attaching a single sling/rope/cable to one object (e.g., a pipe bundle). A competent rigger completes this in ~3–7 minutes including inspection.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise rope/cable handling, knot-tying or hook-latching around irregular objects (pipes, hoses, bundles). High dexterity and force-controlled manipulation needed; current humanoids struggle with flexible materials and varied geometries.

task: Identify or separate waste products or materials for recycling or reuse.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sorting a typical batch/bin of mixed waste materials at a workstation; a competent worker sorts, identifies, and separates into correct categories in ~15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick and place common recyclables but struggle with perception of contaminated, crumpled, or novel materials, grasp variability of irregular waste items, and reliable categorization of ambiguous objects without human judgment.
