task: Clean, sharpen, and lubricate tools and equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning, sharpening, and lubricating a single tool (e.g., a hand tool or blade). A competent worker takes ~15–25 min per tool.
robot_success_rationale: Sharpening requires precise force control, angle consistency, and fine dexterity beyond current humanoid capability. Lubrication application and multi-step sequencing on varied tools further reduce success. Cleaning alone is feasible (~40%), but the full task is not.

task: Patrol park or forest areas to protect resources and prevent damage.
human_time_rationale: One patrol instance = a single shift patrol of a park/forest section. Competent rangers typically complete a patrol route in 3–5 hours; 4 hours (240 min) is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Outdoor rough terrain (trails, roots, slopes, weather) severely limits humanoid mobility (~30–60%). Add multi-hour endurance, autonomous judgment on damage/threats, radio reporting, and intervention — current robots cannot sustain this mission reliably.

task: Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting and depositing one batch of scrap/waste to the correct disposal point (bin, skip, or waste station) per shift or work cycle; ~15 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying varied waste types, sorting by regulation, navigating to correct disposal points (possibly outdoors/rough terrain), and operating bin lids or chutes. Perception, classification, and compliance generalization remain weak in unstructured industrial settings.

task: Maintain supply of fluids, such as plasma, saline, blood, or glucose, for use during operations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = checking, retrieving, and restocking surgical fluid supplies (plasma, saline, blood, glucose) for a single operation; a scrub/circulating nurse typically spends ~10 min per case.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sterile technique, reading labels, managing blood-product protocols, navigating refrigerators/supply rooms, and coordinating with surgical staff in a dynamic OR. Perception, sterile handling, and contextual judgment far exceed 2026 robot capabilities.

task: Prune, cut down, fertilize, and spray trees as directed by tree surgeons.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing directed work on one tree (pruning, cutting, fertilizing, or spraying). A competent arborist assistant spends ~45 min per tree on average.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured terrain, climbing or reaching tall canopies, operating chainsaws/pruning tools and spray equipment, and interpreting surgeon directions. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities in dexterity, reach, tool-use, and safety.

task: Serve meals or feed patients needing assistance or persuasion.
human_time_rationale: One instance = serving and/or hand-feeding one patient one meal, including setup, encouragement, and cleanup. Typically 15–25 minutes for a care worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous feeding motions, adaptive social/emotional persuasion, reading patient cues, and safe interaction with fragile individuals. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and caregiving capability.

task: Estimate land production possibilities, surveying property and studying factors such as crop rotation history, soil fertility, or irrigation facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = surveying a single parcel/field. A competent agronomist/appraiser walks the land, reviews crop history, tests soil, and assesses irrigation in ~2 hours per parcel.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor terrain navigation, soil sampling, interpreting historical crop records, assessing irrigation infrastructure, and synthesizing agronomic knowledge — far beyond current robot generalization, outdoor mobility, and reasoning capabilities.

task: Operate nuclear power reactors in accordance with policies and procedures to protect workers from radiation and to ensure environmental safety.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full operator shift (typically 8 hours) monitoring and operating a nuclear reactor in accordance with safety procedures.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating complex, highly regulated control systems with real-time judgment, emergency decision-making, regulatory compliance, and radiation safety protocols far beyond any current humanoid robot's cognitive and dexterous capabilities. Safety criticality demands near-zero risk tolerance.

task: Exercise or practice under the direction of athletic trainers or professional coaches to develop skills, improve physical condition, or prepare for competitions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one structured training session (practice or workout). Typical athletic training sessions run 60–120 minutes; 90 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires interpreting complex coached instructions, sport-specific motor skills, dynamic athleticism, and adaptive performance feedback. Current humanoids lack the agility, sport-specific movement repertoire, and social responsiveness needed for coached athletic training.

task: Install layers of vegetation-based green roofs, including protective membranes, drainage, aeration, water retention and filter layers, soil substrates, irrigation materials, and plants.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a complete green roof system on a typical residential/small commercial roof (~100 m²). A skilled crew takes ~1 full day (6–8 hrs) to lay all layers, substrate, irrigation, and plants.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rooftop locomotion on uneven/sloped surfaces, heavy material handling (substrate bags, rolls), multi-step layered assembly, plant placement, and irrigation connections — far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities in unstructured outdoor construction environments.

task: Obtain information about or inspect performance facilities, equipment, and accommodations to ensure that they meet specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single performance facility (stage, equipment, accommodations). A competent inspector typically spends ~45–90 minutes on a thorough walkthrough and checklist review.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating large, complex, varied venues; reading spec sheets; assessing subjective quality of acoustics, seating, lighting rigs, and dressing rooms; cross-referencing contract requirements. Current robots lack robust semantic inspection, contextual judgment, and multi-room generalization needed for autonomous pass/fail decisions a supervisor would trust.

task: Operate shredding and chipping equipment, and feed limbs and brush into the machines.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing and chipping one load of brush/limbs (a typical job site batch). A competent worker spends ~30–60 min feeding, monitoring, and managing debris flow.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy outdoor machinery, handling bulky/irregular limbs in unstructured terrain, and safe equipment control. Humanoids lack the strength, reach, and machine-operation capability for this hazardous task.

task: Demonstrate patient care in clinical units of hospitals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient care demonstration (e.g., showing a procedure or technique to staff/students at a bedside). Typical demos last 20–40 minutes including setup and Q&A.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, nuanced patient interaction, communication, adaptive caregiving, and safe handling of medical equipment in unstructured hospital environments. Far beyond current humanoid robot generalization and caregiving capability.

task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full cycle of selecting, storing, ordering, issuing, and inventorying supplies for a classroom. A competent clerk handles this in ~45 min per session.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires cognitive decision-making (selecting/ordering items), software/system interaction (ordering, inventory databases), and diverse manipulation across an unstructured environment. Current robots lack the judgment, system integration, and generalization needed for the full workflow.

task: Perform routine maintenance on boilers, such as replacing burners or hoses, installing replacement parts, or reinforcing structural weaknesses to ensure optimal boiler efficiency.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one routine boiler maintenance session (replacing burners/hoses, fitting parts, reinforcing structure). A competent technician typically needs 1–2 hours per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-force torque on pipe fittings, fine dexterity with varied fasteners, navigation in tight mechanical spaces, and safety-critical judgment. Well beyond current humanoid robots' reliable autonomous capability.

task: Prepare or examine food trays for conformance to prescribed diet.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing or checking one food tray. A dietary aide typically takes 2–4 minutes to assemble and verify a single patient/resident tray against a diet card.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading diet orders, recognizing food items and portions, cross-referencing dietary restrictions, and arranging items correctly. Current robots lack reliable food-item recognition, label reading, and diet-compliance reasoning in unstructured kitchen/tray-line environments.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single assistive interaction (e.g., setting up a device, guiding a student to a restroom). Median across varied interactions is ~15 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social communication, adapting to diverse disability needs, fine dexterous device setup, and safe physical assistance in unpredictable spaces. Current humanoids lack the generalization, safety assurance, and interpersonal judgment required.

task: Attend to children's basic needs by feeding them, dressing them, and changing their diapers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single caregiving cycle (feeding + dressing + diaper change) for one child. A competent caregiver typically completes this in ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Children are unpredictable, soft, and fragile — requiring extreme dexterity, gentle force control, and real-time behavioral adaptation. Feeding, fastening clothing snaps, and safely changing diapers far exceed current humanoid robotic capabilities. Safety risk makes autonomous deployment unacceptable.

task: Collect debris and refuse from tree trimming and removal operations into piles, using shovels, rakes, or other tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing debris from a typical tree trimming job site. A competent worker with rake/shovel takes ~20–30 min to gather branches, leaves, and wood chips into piles.
robot_success_rationale: Outdoor unstructured terrain, highly variable debris (branches, logs, leaves), requires raking/shoveling tools, and navigating rough ground. Current humanoids lack reliable outdoor locomotion, tool-use generalization, and perception for chaotic organic debris.

task: Cable, brace, tie, bolt, stake, and guy trees and branches to provide support.
human_time_rationale: One instance = supporting one tree or branch. A skilled arborist takes ~30–60 min to assess, position materials, climb if needed, and secure hardware.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing or working at height, driving stakes into ground, tensioning cables, and using specialized tools in highly unstructured outdoor terrain. Far beyond current humanoid locomotion, dexterity, and generalization limits.

task: Administer drug and alcohol tests, including random drug screens of offenders, to verify compliance with substance abuse treatment programs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = administering a single drug/alcohol test to one offender, including ID verification, specimen collection, documentation, and chain-of-custody handling. ~15–25 minutes typical.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identity verification, legal chain-of-custody documentation, handling biohazardous specimens with fine dexterity, reading test strips, and interpersonal compliance management — all far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities. Regulatory and safety requirements also preclude autonomous administration.

task: Maintain optimum physical fitness levels by training regularly, following nutrition plans, or consulting with health professionals.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single training session or health consultation. A typical workout or dietitian/physician visit runs ~45–75 minutes for a professional athlete or fitness-focused worker.
robot_success_rationale: This task requires self-directed physical training, interpreting personalized nutrition plans, and engaging in nuanced health consultations — all demanding autonomy, social cognition, and goal-oriented self-regulation far beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Organize and label materials and display students' work.
human_time_rationale: One instance = organizing/labeling materials and mounting one classroom set of student work displays. A competent teacher or aide takes ~30 min per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading handwritten/printed labels, fine manipulation of paper, adhesives, pins, and hooks, aesthetic layout judgment, and navigating cluttered classroom furniture. Current humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity, semantic understanding of student work, and display-quality placement.

task: Scrub arms and hands and assist the surgical team to scrub and put on gloves, masks, and surgical clothing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single pre-surgical scrub-and-gown session for one person; standard surgical scrub protocol (hands, arms) plus assisted gowning/gloving takes ~8–12 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires ultra-precise dexterity for sterile gloving/gowning, real-time adaptation to human body and sterile-field rules, fine manipulation of soft garments and tiny glove cuffs, and strict aseptic compliance — far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Copy and attach load specifications to loaded tanks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = copying and attaching a load spec document/tag to one loaded tank. A competent worker retrieves the spec, makes a copy, and physically affixes it to the tank in ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires printing or retrieving paper documents, handling and attaching labels/tags to curved tank surfaces, and navigating an industrial environment. Fine manipulation of paper/tags and reliable attachment (clips, ties, adhesive) remain challenging for current humanoids.

task: Transplant and remove trees and shrubs, and prepare trees for moving.
human_time_rationale: One instance = transplanting or removing a single average tree/shrub, including digging, root ball preparation, moving, and replanting. Competent landscaper takes ~1–1.5 hrs.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-force digging, large tool use (shovels, spades, root cutters), heavy lifting of root balls, outdoor uneven terrain, and nuanced judgment about root systems. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Accompany patients to and from wards for medical or dental treatments, shopping trips, or religious or recreational events.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient escort trip (e.g., ward to treatment room or off-site outing and back). Median round-trip including wait time is ~45 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating complex, dynamic hospital/outdoor environments, managing patient safety, social interaction, and situational judgment. Current robots lack robust outdoor mobility, reliable patient monitoring, and adaptive caregiving needed for autonomous escort.

task: Analyze patrons' hair and other physical features to determine and recommend beauty treatment or suggest hair styles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = consulting with one patron; a skilled stylist typically spends 5–15 minutes examining hair texture, face shape, scalp condition, and discussing preferences before recommending treatments/styles.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social interaction, aesthetic judgment, face-shape analysis, hair texture/condition assessment, and personalized communication. Current robots lack the social fluency, domain expertise integration, and tactile perception needed for acceptable quality.

task: Transfer finished products, raw materials, tools, or equipment between storage and work areas of plants and warehouses, by hand or using hand trucks or powered lift trucks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one transfer trip (load, move, unload). Typical warehouse/plant transfer between two points takes ~10–20 min including loading and unloading.
robot_success_rationale: AMRs handle structured warehouse routes well, but a general-purpose humanoid must load varied items onto hand trucks, navigate cluttered plant floors, operate powered lift trucks, and unload — each step is error-prone. Unstructured environments and vehicle operation drop success significantly.

task: Operate or maintain distributed power generation equipment, including fuel cells or microturbines, to produce energy on-site for manufacturing or other commercial purposes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one daily/shift operational check and routine maintenance cycle on distributed generation equipment (fuel cell or microturbine unit); a competent technician typically takes 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading gauges/HMIs, adjusting valves, replacing filters, interpreting alarms, and using specialized tools on complex industrial equipment. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use, fault-diagnosis cognition, and safe operation around high-voltage/high-pressure systems.

task: Hoist tools and equipment to tree trimmers, and lower branches with ropes or block and tackle.
human_time_rationale: One instance = hoisting a single load of tools up or lowering one branch down. Attaching rope/rigging, hoisting, and securing takes ~5–10 minutes per exchange.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor uneven terrain, rope/rigging manipulation, dynamic load control, and coordinating with elevated workers. Current humanoids lack reliable rope handling, knot-tying, and block-and-tackle operation in unstructured outdoor environments.

task: Inspect or test raw materials, parts, or products to determine compliance with environmental standards.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting/testing a single batch or sample of material/part. Includes sampling, running a test protocol, and recording compliance results; ~20 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized lab instruments (spectrometers, titrators, pH meters), interpreting multi-step test protocols, and making compliance judgments. Current humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity with lab equipment and domain-specific reasoning for regulatory standards.

task: Provide patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities with routine physical, emotional, psychological, or rehabilitation care under the direction of nursing or medical staff.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single care session (e.g., assisting one patient with hygiene, exercises, or emotional support). Typical sessions run 30–60 minutes depending on patient needs.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced emotional attunement, adaptive communication, and safe physical handling of vulnerable individuals with unpredictable behaviors. Current robots lack social intelligence, trauma-aware interaction, and the dexterity/judgment for safe unstructured caregiving.

task: Inspect manufactured or processed products to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single product/batch unit. A competent inspector typically spends ~15 min checking dimensions, surface quality, markings, and documentation against spec sheets.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can handle visual surface checks and simple measurements, but cross-referencing contract specs, interpreting varied defect types, handling diverse product geometries, using inspection tools (calipers, gauges), and making judgment calls remain well beyond current autonomy.

task: Perform animal grooming duties, such as washing, brushing, clipping, and trimming coats, cutting nails, and cleaning ears.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full grooming session for one medium-sized dog (wash, dry, brush, clip/trim coat, cut nails, clean ears). Typical professional grooming session takes 45–90 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high dexterity for nail trimming, ear cleaning, and coat clipping on a live, moving animal. Animal behavior is unpredictable; safe restraint, tool switching, and fine motor precision near sensitive areas far exceed current robot capabilities.

task: Maintain and repair laboratory equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = diagnosing and repairing one piece of lab equipment (e.g., centrifuge, microscope, pipette calibrator). Median repair/maintenance session for a competent technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert diagnosis, fine dexterous manipulation of varied precision instruments, use of specialized tools, reading error codes, and judgment-based troubleshooting — far beyond 2026 humanoid robot generalization and dexterity limits.

task: Clean mold-contaminated sites by removing damaged porous materials or thoroughly cleaning all contaminated nonporous materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = remediating a single mold-contaminated area (e.g., a bathroom wall or small room section). Includes bagging/removing porous materials and scrubbing/treating nonporous surfaces; ~2 hrs median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires visually identifying mold boundaries, tearing out porous materials (drywall, insulation), bagging debris, and applying biocidal cleaners with adequate force/coverage. Far beyond current humanoid generalization, dexterity, and demolition-tool-use capabilities.

task: Prepare test stations, instrumentation, or data acquisition systems for use in specific tests of fuel cell components or systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single test station/instrumentation setup for a fuel cell test. A competent technician typically spends 45–90 minutes checking connections, calibrating sensors, configuring DAQ software, and verifying safety interlocks.
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly specialized knowledge (fuel cell systems, DAQ software, calibration procedures), fine electrical/pneumatic connections, software configuration, and safety judgment. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capability in unstructured lab environments.

task: Operate boom trucks, loaders, stump chippers, brush chippers, tractors, power saws, trucks, sprayers, and other equipment and tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full work shift operating a mix of arborist/grounds equipment. A typical job cycle (e.g., tree removal with boom truck + chipper + chainsaw) runs ~3–5 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving vehicles on public/outdoor terrain, operating large specialized machinery (boom trucks, tractors, chippers), and wielding power saws — all beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. No frontier system can drive or operate these vehicles autonomously.

task: Provide security by patrolling interior and exterior of courthouse and escorting judges and other court employees.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full security shift (8 hours). Patrolling, escorting, and monitoring are continuous duties measured per shift by a supervisor.
robot_success_rationale: Requires mixed indoor/outdoor navigation, threat recognition, social judgment, legal authority, dynamic escort of people, and real-time response to unpredictable events. Current humanoids lack reliable autonomy, situational awareness, and authority for security roles.

task: Examine or test crops or products to estimate their value, determine their grade, or locate any evidence of disease or insect damage.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single crop sample or product lot. A competent grader takes ~15–25 min to visually assess, handle, and grade one batch for quality, disease, and pest damage.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced multi-modal perception (visual, tactile, olfactory) across highly variable crop types, diseases, and damage patterns. Current robots lack reliable fine-grained agricultural inspection generalization, grading judgment, and outdoor/field mobility for unstructured crop environments.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as hall and cafeteria monitoring and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single monitoring shift (e.g., one lunch period or one bus loading/unloading event). Typical school duty period runs 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic crowd supervision, social judgment, verbal intervention, and outdoor/vehicle-adjacent mobility. Current humanoids lack reliable social interaction, situational authority, and safe operation near children and moving buses.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one activity session; includes arranging furniture, distributing materials, and setting up equipment. Typical prep takes ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a varied classroom, rearranging furniture (heavy, awkward), distributing diverse materials to specific locations, and interpreting activity-specific instructions. Current humanoids lack the contextual understanding, heavy manipulation, and reliable generalization needed.

task: Sketch rough and detailed drawings of apparel or accessories, and write specifications such as color schemes, construction, material types, and accessory requirements.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one complete garment/accessory design: rough sketch, detailed drawing, and written specs. A skilled fashion designer typically takes 45–75 minutes per design.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative ideation, fine artistic dexterity (sketching on paper/tablet), and domain-specific fashion knowledge. Current humanoid robots lack drawing capability, creative generalization, and spec-writing cognition entirely.

task: Perform minor repairs to machines, such as replacing damaged or worn parts.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one minor repair (e.g., replacing a worn belt, gasket, or small part). A competent technician diagnoses, sources the part, and completes the swap in ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diagnosing faults, locating correct replacement parts, using varied tools (wrenches, screwdrivers), and fine dexterous manipulation in unstructured machine interiors — well beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity limits.

task: Provide patients with assistance in bathing, dressing, or grooming, demonstrating these skills as necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one patient through a full bathing, dressing, or grooming session. A competent aide typically spends 20–30 minutes per patient.
robot_success_rationale: Requires adaptive dexterity on an unpredictable human body, dynamic safety judgment, and interpersonal communication. Current humanoids lack the gentle compliance, situational awareness, and trust required for safe unassisted patient caregiving.

task: Provide sample garments to agents and sales representatives, and arrange for showings of sample garments at sales meetings or fashion shows.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing and distributing sample garments plus arranging/staging a showing or sales meeting. Includes organizing samples, coordinating with agents, and setting up the display event.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires high-level coordination, communication with human agents, scheduling logistics, and social judgment. Robots lack the language negotiation, organizational planning, and interpersonal skills needed. Physical garment handling alone is insufficient.

task: Conduct visual inspections of emission control equipment and smoke emitted from gasoline or diesel vehicles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one vehicle's emission control equipment and exhaust smoke. A trained inspector typically spends ~3–7 min per vehicle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating under/around vehicles, identifying varied emission components, and visually assessing smoke color/opacity. Current robots lack reliable under-vehicle mobility, expert visual smoke interpretation, and generalization across vehicle types.

task: Trim jagged stumps, using saws or pruning shears.
human_time_rationale: One instance = trimming one jagged stump. A competent worker inspects, positions tool, and makes clean cuts in roughly 5–10 minutes depending on stump size.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor uneven terrain navigation, forceful two-handed tool use (saw/shears), precise cut placement on irregular organic material, and high torque grip. Current humanoids lack reliable outdoor stability and forceful cutting dexterity.

task: Operate, control, or monitor equipment, such as acid or gas carbon dioxide removal units, carbon dioxide compressors, or pipelines, to capture, store, or transport carbon dioxide exhaust.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one monitoring/control shift cycle (~1 hour) checking gauges, adjusting parameters, and logging status for a CO₂ capture/compression/pipeline system.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading specialized industrial gauges, operating control panels, and making safety-critical adjustments on complex chemical/mechanical plant equipment. Current humanoids lack domain-specific tool use, hazardous-environment certification, and reliable fine manipulation of industrial valves and controls.

task: Fit patients for orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices, such as crutches.
human_time_rationale: One fitting session for a single patient — includes assessment, sizing, adjusting the device, and verifying fit/comfort. Typical clinical visit component.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, tactile sensitivity to patient anatomy, interpersonal communication, and fine adjustment of hardware to a human body. Far beyond current robot dexterity, perception, and caregiving generalization. No deployed system approaches this.

task: Clear sites, streets, and grounds of woody and herbaceous materials, such as tree stumps and fallen trees and limbs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing a typical residential lot or road segment of stumps, fallen limbs, and brush; competent crew takes ~90 min using chainsaws, stump grinders, and loaders.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating chainsaws, stump grinders, chippers, and heavy loaders on rough, uneven outdoor terrain. Current humanoid robots lack the mobility, tool-use capability, and force needed for unstructured outdoor clearing work.

task: Remove jammed materials from machines and readjust components as necessary to resume normal operations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = clearing one jam and readjusting affected components. A skilled worker diagnoses, clears material, and resets the machine in roughly 5–15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fault diagnosis, locating jammed material in occluded/confined machine spaces, applying variable force, and readjusting components — demanding fine dexterity, perception in cluttered environments, and task-specific generalization well beyond 2026 frontier capability.

task: Check and maintain equipment to ensure that it is in working order.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and servicing a single piece of equipment (e.g., a machine or tool). A competent technician typically takes ~30 min for a standard check-and-maintain cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can visually inspect surfaces and simple indicators, but maintenance requires diverse tool use, diagnosing faults, replacing parts, and applying judgment across unpredictable equipment states — well beyond current autonomous capability.

task: Inspect trees to determine if they have diseases or pest problems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one tree. A competent arborist visually examines bark, foliage, branches, and root zone for disease/pest signs, taking ~10–20 min per tree.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert visual recognition of subtle biological symptoms (discoloration, lesions, frass, galls) across varied outdoor terrain and canopy heights. Current robots lack specialized horticultural perception, reliable outdoor locomotion on uneven ground, and domain-specific diagnostic models at acceptable accuracy.

task: Instruct, motivate, safeguard, and assist patients as they practice exercises or functional activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient exercise/therapy session. A typical physical therapy session guiding a patient through exercises lasts ~20–45 minutes; 30 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic verbal motivation, real-time safety judgment, adaptive physical assistance, and empathetic communication with vulnerable patients. Current robots lack reliable conversational coaching, nuanced physical guarding, and adaptive caregiving in unstructured clinical environments.

task: Attach wigs or hairpieces to model heads and dress wigs and hairpieces according to instructions, samples, sketches or photographs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = attaching and dressing one wig/hairpiece on one model head. A skilled wig dresser takes ~20–30 min per piece including securing, styling, and matching reference.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extremely fine dexterity (handling delicate hair strands, pins, combs), nuanced aesthetic judgment matching sketches/photos, and complex bimanual manipulation. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability.

task: Test fabrics or oversee testing so that garment care labels can be created.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one fabric/garment sample through standard care protocols (wash, dry, iron) and recording results for label creation. Includes setup, monitoring, and documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating laundry/ironing equipment, measuring color fastness and shrinkage with instruments, interpreting nuanced results, and making labeling decisions — complex multi-step tool use, judgment, and documentation far beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Perform grounds maintenance tasks, such as removing snow and mowing the lawn.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one grounds maintenance session (e.g., mowing a typical residential lawn or clearing snow from a driveway/walkway), estimated at ~60 minutes for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating powered outdoor equipment (mowers, snowblowers) on uneven terrain in variable weather. Humanoid robots cannot reliably drive/operate such machinery autonomously; outdoor unstructured environments and tool operation push success very low.

task: Test vessels for leaks, damage, and defects, and repair or replace defective parts as necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and repairing one vessel. Includes setup, pressure/leak testing, damage assessment, and replacing/repairing one defective component. ~45 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires specialized testing equipment (pressure gauges, dye, ultrasound), interpreting subtle defect signals, and precise repair/replacement work. Current humanoids lack the tool-use fluency, sensory integration, and judgment needed for vessel diagnostics and repair.

task: Clean facilities such as floors or locker rooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning a single facility space (e.g., one locker room or floor area). A competent janitor sweeps, mops, wipes surfaces, and tidies in ~45 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots handle flat-floor sweeping/mopping reasonably well, but locker rooms add clutter, obstacles, benches, varied surfaces, and confined spaces. Generalization across unstructured environments and fine wiping tasks remains limited for 2026 humanoids.

task: Give facials to patrons, using special compounds, such as lotions and creams.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full facial treatment for a single patron, including cleansing, exfoliation, mask, and moisturizer application; typically 45–60 min in a spa setting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires delicate touch on a human face, precise product application, real-time skin-feel feedback, patron comfort management, and nuanced social interaction. Current robots lack the fine dexterity, compliant force control, and tactile sensitivity needed safely on skin.

task: Pour, trim, and polish study casts.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full set of study casts (upper + lower). Pouring gypsum, trimming on a model trimmer, and polishing takes a skilled dental lab tech ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise mixing of dental stone, vibrating impressions, operating a wet model trimmer with exact angulation, and fine polishing — demanding dexterity, tactile feedback, and specialized tool use far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Maintain or repair vehicles with alternative fuel systems, including biodiesel, hybrid, or compressed natural gas vehicles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single maintenance/repair job on an alternative-fuel vehicle (e.g., fluid service, component swap, diagnostic check). Typical shop job takes ~2 hours for a competent technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating lifts, specialized diagnostic tools, handling high-voltage/high-pressure systems, fine dexterous component work, and safety-critical judgment. Current humanoids lack vehicle-service tool use, HV safety protocols, and generalization for this domain.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one activity session; includes gathering/arranging materials, setting up furniture, and distributing supplies — typically 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a varied classroom, interpreting activity-specific instructions, arranging furniture, and distributing diverse materials. Poor generalization, limited semantic understanding of "prepared," and unreliable manipulation of varied objects (books, scissors, chairs) keep success very low.

task: Direct, and participate in, forest fire suppression.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one operational shift (≈8 hours) directing/participating in active wildfire suppression, the standard unit a fire incident commander would measure.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy equipment, aerial coordination, rough outdoor terrain navigation, real-time tactical decision-making, crew communication, and extreme heat/smoke conditions. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in dexterity, mobility, generalization, and safety.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one duty period (e.g., a cafeteria/hall monitoring shift or bus loading/unloading event), typically 30–60 minutes for a school aide.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic social navigation, crowd monitoring, child safety judgment, communication, and bus-area outdoor mobility. Current humanoids lack reliable social reasoning, authority enforcement, and safe operation around unpredictable children at scale.

task: Clean up before and after installation, including vacuuming carpet and discarding remnant pieces.
human_time_rationale: One instance = pre/post cleanup of a typical installation work area: vacuuming carpet and discarding remnants. A competent worker completes this in ~20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Vacuuming with a handheld/upright tool requires grasping and maneuvering an unfamiliar appliance; identifying, picking up, and discarding irregular remnant pieces adds unstructured manipulation. Generalization and tool-use limits reduce reliability significantly.

task: Provide assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one person with one access need (e.g., restroom trip, handing a device). Includes setup, guidance, and safe return; ~15 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced physical support, safe body contact, dynamic adaptation to individual disabilities, and social/emotional judgment. Current humanoids lack reliable safe caregiving dexterity, fall-prevention capability, and person-specific adaptation needed here.

task: Examine sample garments on and off models, modifying designs to achieve desired effects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = examining and modifying one sample garment on/off a model. Includes fitting assessment, pinning, and design adjustments; ~20–30 min per garment sample.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced aesthetic judgment, drape/fit perception on a live model, fine dexterous manipulation (pinning, tucking, altering fabric), and creative design decision-making — all far beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Seal outlet valves on tank cars, barges, and trucks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sealing one outlet valve on one vehicle/vessel. A worker locates the valve, applies/fastens the seal, and verifies closure — typically 5–15 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating unstructured industrial outdoor environments, identifying varied valve types across tank cars/barges/trucks, applying seals with precise force. Poor generalization, heavy-equipment access, and dexterous sealing in awkward positions exceed 2026 robot capabilities.

task: Maintain quality of pool water by testing chemical levels.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full chemical test of a pool: collect water sample, apply test kit/strips, read and record pH, chlorine, alkalinity, etc. Typically 10–20 minutes for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor/poolside locomotion, precise sample collection, operating test kits or digital meters, reading color charts or displays, and interpreting chemical results — well beyond reliable autonomous capability for 2026 humanoids.

task: Apply green technologies or techniques, such as the use of coiled tubing, slim-hole drilling, horizontal drilling, hydraulic fracturing, or gas lift systems.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one green technology application session (e.g., setting up and executing a coiled tubing or hydraulic fracturing operation on a single well), typically spanning one full shift (~8 hours).
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy, specialized oilfield machinery (coiled tubing units, frac pumps, gas lift valves) in hazardous outdoor environments. Current humanoids cannot drive or control such equipment, manage high-pressure systems, or handle the complex decision-making involved.

task: Prepare materials and classroom for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing a single classroom before one class session; includes arranging furniture, setting out supplies, and staging materials.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a cluttered classroom, rearranging furniture (heavy chairs/desks), distributing varied materials, and understanding lesson context. Poor generalization, heavy object manipulation, and semantic task understanding limit success significantly.

task: Trim, top, and reshape trees to achieve attractive shapes or to remove low-hanging branches.
human_time_rationale: One instance = trimming/topping/reshaping one medium tree. A competent arborist/landscaper typically spends 30–60 min per tree depending on size and complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor locomotion on uneven ground, climbing or reaching high branches, operating pruning tools with precision, and making aesthetic judgments about shape — all far beyond current general-purpose humanoid capability.

task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single inventory/supply cycle (select, store, order, issue, and count stock for a classroom). A competent staff member typically spends ~45 min per cycle.
robot_success_rationale: This task blends cognitive decisions (selecting/ordering supplies), system access (procurement software), physical handling of varied items, and social interaction (issuing to staff). Current humanoids lack procurement reasoning, UI navigation, and unstructured multi-step generalization needed to complete all sub-tasks autonomously.

task: Inspect and adjust rental items to meet needs of customer.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting and adjusting one rental item (e.g., bicycle, furniture, equipment) for a single customer. Includes visual check, functional test, and minor adjustments.
robot_success_rationale: Requires interpreting customer needs (language/context), inspecting varied item types, and making fine adjustments. Generalization across diverse rental items and customer interaction is beyond reliable 2026 robot capability.

task: Organize and label materials and display students' work.
human_time_rationale: One instance = organizing/labeling classroom materials and mounting a set of student work (e.g., one class's assignment). A competent teacher or aide takes ~30 min per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading handwritten labels, fine dexterity (pinning/taping paper, using markers), social/aesthetic judgment on display layout, and navigating varied classroom furniture. Far beyond reliable 2026 robot capability.

task: Shape eyebrows and remove facial hair, using depilatory cream, tweezers, electrolysis or wax.
human_time_rationale: One instance = shaping both eyebrows and removing facial hair on one client. A competent esthetician typically completes this in 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity on a moving human face, precise force control for tweezing/waxing near eyes, safe chemical application, and high-stakes skin/eye safety. Far beyond 2026 robot capability.

task: Collect, identify, evaluate, or preserve case evidence.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a single crime/case scene, including collecting, tagging, photographing, and bagging physical evidence items. Competent forensic tech typically spends 30–60 min per scene.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced perception to identify legally significant evidence, fine dexterity for careful collection without contamination, chain-of-custody documentation, and unstructured judgment. Current robots lack forensic domain reasoning and contamination-safe handling protocols.

task: Conduct fuel cell testing projects, using fuel cell test stations, analytical instruments, or electrochemical diagnostics, such as cyclic voltammetry or impedance spectroscopy.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one fuel cell test run including setup, data acquisition (e.g., CV or EIS sweep), and initial logging. Typically 3–5 hours for a competent electrochemist.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert-level scientific judgment, precise instrument configuration, interpretation of electrochemical data, and operation of highly specialized lab equipment. Far beyond current general-purpose robot autonomy.

task: Maintain patients' restrictions to assigned areas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one monitoring shift segment (~30 min) of actively enforcing a single patient's area restriction, including redirecting, reminding, or physically guiding a patient back to their assigned zone.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social interaction, patient-specific judgment, de-escalation, and safe physical redirection of potentially distressed individuals. Current robots lack reliable human interaction, empathy, and safe caregiving dexterity for unpredictable patients.

task: Shovel, sweep, or otherwise clean work areas.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning a single work area (e.g., a workshop floor or bay). A competent worker shoveling debris and sweeping takes ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can sweep flat surfaces reasonably well, but shoveling irregular debris, navigating cluttered industrial work areas, and tool-switching (shovel vs. broom) remain challenging for general-purpose humanoids. Unstructured environments and varied debris types lower reliability.

task: Clean and disinfect rooms and furnishings to maintain a safe and orderly environment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning and disinfecting a single room with its furnishings; a competent housekeeper or custodian typically takes 30–60 min per room.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can sweep/mop flat floors (~40–70%), but disinfecting furnishings requires navigating clutter, applying sprays precisely, wiping varied surfaces, moving objects, and validating coverage — well beyond current reliable autonomy.

task: Operate ship loading and unloading equipment, conveyors, hoists, and other specialized material handling equipment such as railroad tank car unloading equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one ship/tank car loading or unloading cycle. Competent operators typically complete a single hold/car operation in 45–90 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating large specialized maritime/industrial machinery (cranes, conveyors, tank car systems) with complex controls, dynamic dockside environments, safety-critical decisions, and vehicle/equipment operation far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Administer traction to relieve neck or back pain, using intermittent or static traction equipment.
human_time_rationale: One traction session: setup, fitting harness/halter, programming/adjusting equipment, monitoring patient for ~15–20 min, then removal and documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise patient assessment, fitting a harness to a human body, real-time pain/safety monitoring, and clinical judgment. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, haptic sensitivity, and medical decision-making for safe traction therapy.

task: Participate in athletic events or competitive sports, according to established rules and regulations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one competitive sporting event or match. Typical duration ranges from 30–90 minutes; 60 minutes is a reasonable median across sports.
robot_success_rationale: Competitive sports demand real-time dynamic perception, agility, strategy, rule-following, and interaction with unpredictable opponents/environments. Current humanoids lack the speed, reflexes, coordination, and sport-specific motor skills to compete autonomously.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one class session; gathering materials, arranging desks/chairs, setting up displays. Typical prep takes 15–30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires understanding of diverse, context-dependent layouts; moving furniture, distributing varied materials, operating projectors/boards. High generalization demand, unstructured environment, and multi-step planning exceed current humanoid capabilities reliably.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one student with one need (e.g., setting up a device, escorting to restroom). Typical interaction spans 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social communication, adaptive dexterity for varied assistive devices, safe physical assistance with mobility/restroom access, and real-time judgment about a vulnerable person's needs — all far beyond 2026 robot capability.

task: Record environmental data from field samples of soil, air, water, or pests to monitor the effectiveness of integrated pest management (IPM) practices.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting and recording environmental data from a single field site visit. Includes sampling soil/water/air/pests, labeling, and logging data — typically 30–60 min per site.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured terrain navigation, multi-modal sampling (soil probes, water collection, pest trapping), precise tool use, and structured data logging. Current robots lack reliable outdoor generalization and multi-tool dexterity for IPM-specific equipment.

task: Perform postural drainage, percussions, or vibrations or teach deep breathing exercises to treat respiratory conditions.
human_time_rationale: One session of postural drainage, percussion/vibration, or deep breathing instruction for a single patient typically takes 20–30 minutes including positioning and patient coaching.
robot_success_rationale: Requires clinical judgment, adaptive patient interaction, precise therapeutic touch, verbal coaching, and continuous feedback from a non-compliant or ill patient. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, perception, and caregiving generalization. Safety risk is critical.

task: Fabricate models or samples in paper, wood, glass, fabric, plastic, metal, or other materials, using hand or power tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fabricating one model/sample. Skilled craftworker typically spends 60–120 min depending on material and complexity; 90 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires selecting correct materials, switching diverse hand/power tools, precise shaping across wildly varied materials and geometries. Current humanoids lack the tool-switching generalization, fine dexterity, and task planning needed for open-ended fabrication.

task: Select, store, order, issue, inventory, and maintain classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full cycle (select, store, order, issue, inventory, maintain) for a typical classroom. A competent aide/clerk might spend ~45 min per session managing supplies.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires abstract procurement decisions, ordering via systems, record-keeping, and maintenance judgment — far beyond physical pick-and-place. Robots lack autonomy for inventory management, ordering, and condition assessment of varied classroom items.

task: Maintain production equipment and machinery.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one routine maintenance session on a single machine (lubrication, inspection, minor adjustments, part replacement). A competent technician averages ~1 hour per machine per session.
robot_success_rationale: Maintenance demands diverse dexterous tool use (wrenches, grease guns, multimeters), diagnosis of faults, navigating tight machinery spaces, and adaptive problem-solving. Current humanoids lack the fine manipulation, specialized tool use, and generalization required for safe, unsupervised success.

task: Acquire, maintain, and repair laboratory equipment and tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single equipment/tool servicing event (acquisition check-in, routine maintenance, or minor repair). Median across these sub-tasks for a lab technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires ordering systems, fine tool use, reading instrument displays, diagnosing faults, sourcing parts, and context-aware repair decisions — far beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity limits.

task: Restrain or aid patients as necessary to prevent injury.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single episode of physically restraining or supporting one patient (e.g., during a procedure or fall risk). Typically 5–15 minutes including assessment and safe release.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced force control, real-time reading of patient distress, safe physical contact with a fragile/unpredictable human body, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, safety, and social-perception capabilities.

task: Apply hand or foot brakes and move levers to lock hoists or winches.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying brakes and moving levers to lock a single hoist or winch. A competent operator does this in under a minute as a routine safety step.
robot_success_rationale: Requires locating and forcefully actuating industrial levers, pedals, and brake handles — varied form factors, high force requirements, and safety-critical precision. Current humanoids lack reliable force-controlled lever manipulation in unstructured industrial settings.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one activity session; includes gathering/arranging materials, setting up desks/chairs, and laying out supplies. ~15–25 min typical.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a varied environment, identifying and distributing many heterogeneous objects (papers, scissors, glue, chairs), rearranging furniture, and understanding contextual goals. Too complex and unstructured for current humanoids reliably.

task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full cycle (select, store, order, issue, inventory) for a classroom's supplies in a single session; a competent staff member typically completes this in ~45 min.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires high-level decision-making (selecting/ordering), navigating varied storage environments, reading/updating inventory systems, and social issuing to users. Current robots lack the cognitive integration, tool-use (computers/forms), and generalization needed end-to-end.

task: Replace missing teeth and associated oral structures with permanent fixtures, such as implant-supported prostheses, crowns and bridges, or removable fixtures, such as dentures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one prosthetic tooth-replacement procedure (e.g., placing a crown/bridge or fitting dentures). Multi-appointment work averaged; a single clinical session runs ~2–4 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a small, wet, dynamic oral cavity; sterile surgical judgment; implant drilling into bone; tissue manipulation; bite registration — far beyond any current general-purpose humanoid robot's capability.

task: Separate scrap waste and related materials for reuse, recycling, or disposal.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sorting a typical batch/bin of scrap materials; a competent worker identifies, separates, and routes materials to appropriate containers in ~20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires visual classification of varied, irregular scrap materials (metal, plastic, fabric, mixed waste), fine grasping of awkward shapes, and decision-making on disposal routing. Current robots struggle with unstructured material variation and classification reliability.

task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full cycle (select, store, order, issue, inventory) for a typical classroom's supplies in a single session; ~30–60 min for a competent school aide.
robot_success_rationale: Requires cognitive planning (ordering, selecting vendors), fine manipulation of varied items, navigation through storage rooms, and system-level decision-making. Current humanoids lack the autonomous cognitive workflow and tool-use needed for end-to-end execution.

task: Prepare pharmaceutical compounds for commercial distribution.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one batch of a pharmaceutical compound through weighing, mixing, packaging, and labeling for commercial distribution. A competent technician typically takes ~45 minutes per batch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme precision in weighing/mixing, sterile handling, regulatory compliance, and fine dexterity with specialized equipment (balances, filling machines, labelers). Current humanoids lack the contamination control, sub-milligram precision, and specialized tool integration required.

task: Adjust or repair computer controlled exhaust emissions devices.
human_time_rationale: One instance = diagnosing, adjusting, or repairing one vehicle's computer-controlled emissions device (e.g., EGR valve, catalytic converter, O2 sensor). Includes scan tool hookup, diagnosis, and repair.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating OBD diagnostic tools, navigating under/around a vehicle engine bay, removing/replacing precision components, and interpreting complex fault codes. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity, tool-use, and automotive reasoning capabilities.

task: Adjust, maintain, and repair tools, equipment, and machines, and assist more skilled workers with similar tasks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single adjustment, maintenance, or repair job on one tool/machine. A competent helper spends ~45 min on a typical task (lubrication, part swap, calibration, or assisting a skilled mechanic).
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly varied dexterity (wrenches, fasteners, fine adjustments), tool-use generalization, fault diagnosis, and adaptive collaboration with a skilled worker — all well beyond reliable 2026 robot capability in unstructured environments.

task: Receive, examine, and tag articles to be altered, cleaned, stored, or repaired.
human_time_rationale: One instance = receiving and processing a single article (garment/item): visual examination, identifying required service, and attaching a tag. Typical in dry-cleaning or alterations intake.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterous tagging, nuanced visual inspection for stains/damage, reading customer instructions, and contextual judgment on service type. Current robots lack reliable fine manipulation and semantic garment understanding.

task: Operate idle reduction systems or auxiliary power systems to generate power from alternative sources, such as fuel cells, to reduce idling time, to heat or cool truck cabins, or to provide power for other equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = activating and monitoring an idle reduction/APU system for one rest stop. A driver spends ~10–20 min connecting shore power, adjusting controls, and confirming heating/cooling/power output.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating truck-cab-specific controls, connecting external power/fuel lines, and navigating a semi-truck environment. Current humanoid robots lack the specialized tool-use, situational awareness, and vehicle-interface ability for this task.

task: Administer anesthetics to limit the amount of pain experienced by patients during procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = administering anesthesia for a single procedure, including pre-op assessment, induction, maintenance, and handoff to recovery; typical case ~30–60 min total.
robot_success_rationale: Requires medical licensure, clinical judgment, fine IV/airway manipulation, real-time patient monitoring, and adaptive decision-making under life-threatening stakes. Far beyond any 2026 general-purpose robot's capabilities.

task: Evaluate the functioning of the neuromuscularskeletal system and the spine using systems of chiropractic diagnosis.
human_time_rationale: One patient evaluation session: history intake, orthopedic/neurological tests, postural analysis, spinal palpation, and chiropractic diagnosis. Typical clinical encounter.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert tactile palpation, nuanced clinical reasoning, patient communication, interpreting subtle neurological signs, and licensed chiropractic judgment — far beyond current robot dexterity, sensory resolution, and clinical AI integration.

task: Attach onboard diagnostics (OBD) scanner cables to vehicles to conduct emissions inspections.
human_time_rationale: One instance = attaching OBD cable to one vehicle. A technician locates the OBD-II port (under dash), plugs in the connector, and confirms connection — typically ~2–4 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: OBD-II port is in a confined, dark under-dash space with variable locations across vehicle models. Requires precise cable alignment and insertion force. Current humanoids lack reliable confined-space dexterity and vehicle-interior navigation for consistent autonomous success.

task: Clean work area and check and store equipment after treatment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = post-treatment cleanup of one workstation: wiping surfaces, inspecting and storing equipment. Competent worker typically takes 10–20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires recognizing diverse specialized equipment, checking for damage/readiness, stowing items in correct locations, and wiping surfaces — all in a variable, semi-structured clinical/treatment environment. Perception and task generalization are major bottlenecks.

task: Bleach, clean, or polish teeth to restore natural color.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient teeth-cleaning/polishing/bleaching session. A dental hygienist typically spends 30–60 minutes per patient for cleaning and polishing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a small, wet oral cavity on a moving patient, precise force control to avoid tissue damage, specialized dental tools, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Test samples for specific gravity, using hydrometers, or send samples to laboratories for testing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one sample with a hydrometer or preparing/labeling it for lab submission; a competent technician takes ~10 minutes including reading and recording results.
robot_success_rationale: Requires pouring liquid into a graduated cylinder, carefully placing a fragile hydrometer, reading a meniscus scale, and recording results — demanding fine dexterity, precise fluid handling, and optical measurement interpretation that current humanoids handle poorly.

task: Provide nursing, psychiatric, or personal care to patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single care session (e.g., bathing, feeding, medication, or emotional support) for one patient. Typical direct-care interactions average ~30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires adaptive social/emotional communication, nuanced safety judgment, fine dexterity for personal care, and trust-building with cognitively vulnerable individuals — all well beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities. High failure and safety risk.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one student interaction (providing a device, tech setup, or restroom assistance). Median across varied needs: ~20 min including assessment, retrieval, and physical assistance.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced human assessment of disability needs, configuring specialized assistive tech, physical caregiving (restroom help), social/emotional sensitivity, and generalization across highly variable scenarios — all beyond current robot capability.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one activity session; gathering, arranging, and distributing materials typically takes a competent teacher/aide ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a varied classroom, identifying and sorting many heterogeneous materials, arranging furniture, and interpreting activity-specific instructions. Far beyond reliable autonomous generalization for current humanoids; poor dexterity with diverse objects and high task ambiguity.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one student with one access need (e.g., delivering a device, helping reach a restroom). Typical interaction takes ~10–20 minutes end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced human interaction, understanding individual disability needs, configuring assistive tech, and safely guiding/supporting a person physically. Current robots lack reliable caregiving dexterity, social judgment, and safe human-contact mobility for this context.

task: Direct collection, preparation, and handling of evidence and personal property of prisoners.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing one prisoner's property/evidence: inventorying, bagging, labeling, and securing items. A competent officer typically takes 20–40 minutes per prisoner.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (bagging, labeling, sorting varied items), legal/procedural compliance, chain-of-custody documentation, and judgment about item classification. Far beyond current autonomous robot capability in unstructured, high-stakes settings.

task: Examine merchandise to ensure that it is correctly priced and displayed and that it functions as advertised.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single merchandise item for correct price tag, display placement, and functional check. A competent retail worker takes ~2–4 minutes per item.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading price labels (OCR feasible), verifying display placement (vision feasible), AND functional testing of the product (highly variable, unpredictable interactions). Generalization across product types is very poor; manipulation and product-specific testing push success low.

task: Assemble, typeset, scan, and produce digital camera-ready art or film negatives and printer's proofs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = producing a single camera-ready print job (e.g., a flyer or brochure page): typesetting, assembly, scanning, and proofing typically takes 1–3 hours for a skilled prepress technician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized prepress software, scanners, film negative equipment, and proofing machinery. Humanoid robots lack the software UI interaction, fine dexterous tool-use, and domain knowledge to perform this workflow autonomously end-to-end.

task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single inventory/supply cycle (select, store, order, issue, and reconcile stock for a classroom). A competent worker typically spends ~45 min per cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Task spans cognitive planning (selecting/ordering items), computer/system interaction, locomotion through storage areas, and social issuing to humans. Current robots lack the software integration, generalization, and end-to-end autonomy required across all five sub-tasks.

task: Prepare and administer medications as directed by a physician.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing and administering a single medication dose to one patient: verifying order, drawing up/counting medication, and delivering it (oral, injection, or IV). ~15 min including documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (drawing syringes, handling pills, IV lines), clinical judgment, patient ID verification, safety-critical precision, and interaction with fragile patients. Far beyond current humanoid capabilities in unstructured clinical settings.

task: Secure patients into or onto therapy equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = securing one patient onto one piece of therapy equipment (e.g., traction table, therapy harness, adaptive chair). A competent therapist/aide typically takes 3–7 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires highly compliant, patient-specific dexterous manipulation (straps, buckles, padding), safe force modulation around a fragile human body, and real-time social/pain cues — far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Instruct and monitor students in the use of equipment and materials to prevent injuries and damage.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single instructional/monitoring session for a group of students on equipment/material safety; typically 15–25 minutes for a lab or shop class setup briefing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires verbal instruction, real-time social perception, contextual hazard judgment, and dynamic intervention with students—far beyond current humanoid generalization in unstructured social/educational environments.

task: Scrape or wash conveyors, using belt scrapers or belt washers, to minimize dust production.
human_time_rationale: One instance = scraping/washing one conveyor belt section. A competent worker typically takes ~15–25 minutes to set up, scrape/wash, and inspect one conveyor.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized industrial tools (belt scrapers/washers) on large moving machinery in dusty, cluttered environments. Poor generalization to conveyor layouts, tool attachment, force control, and safety around live equipment severely limits current humanoids.

task: Provide a variety of materials and resources for children to explore, manipulate, and use, both in learning activities and in imaginative play.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up/restocking a play/learning area with varied materials for a group of children; a competent childcare worker takes ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires selecting age-appropriate, pedagogically varied materials, organizing them accessibly, and anticipating imaginative use — demanding high-level contextual judgment, social awareness, and fine manipulation across dozens of object types. Far beyond current robot generalization.

task: Help physicians examine and treat patients, handing them instruments or materials or performing such tasks as giving injections or removing sutures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting during a single patient exam/procedure (e.g., handing instruments, giving an injection, or removing sutures). Typical clinical encounter assistance lasts ~10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (injections, suture removal), precise coordination with a physician, sterile technique, patient interaction, and real-time adaptive response. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured clinical settings.

task: Prepare sketches of ideas, detailed drawings, illustrations, artwork, or blueprints, using drafting instruments, paints and brushes, or computer-aided design equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one complete drawing/sketch/blueprint. A competent drafter or illustrator typically spends 30–90 min on a single professional-quality piece; 60 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative intent, fine motor control for drafting instruments or brushes, and complex cognitive planning. Current humanoids lack the artistic reasoning, precise stylus/brush dexterity, and CAD software interaction needed to produce supervisor-acceptable drawings autonomously.

task: Examine shipment contents and compare with records, such as manifests, invoices, or orders, to verify accuracy.
human_time_rationale: One instance = verifying one incoming shipment. A competent worker opens boxes, counts/identifies items, and cross-checks against a manifest or invoice, typically taking 15–30 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading documents, recognizing diverse items by label/SKU, counting accurately across varied packaging, and cross-referencing records — far beyond current robot generalization, perception, and cognitive integration capabilities in unstructured shipment environments.

task: Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being and report unusual behavior or physical ailments to medical staff.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a routine monitoring round for one patient, including observation, brief interaction, vital checks, and documentation. Typically 20–40 minutes per patient per shift cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced emotional perception, natural language interaction, clinical judgment, and anomaly detection across behavioral/physical cues. Current robots lack reliable affect recognition, empathetic communication, and autonomous medical reporting workflows.

task: Perform nursing duties, such as administering medications, measuring vital signs, collecting specimens, or drawing blood samples.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single nursing duty (e.g., administering one medication or drawing one blood sample). A competent nurse typically completes each sub-task in ~10–20 min including documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (venipuncture, syringe handling), sterile technique, real-time patient feedback, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid capability; safety and regulatory barriers are prohibitive.

task: Diagnose machine tool malfunctions to determine need for adjustments or repairs.
human_time_rationale: One instance = diagnosing a single machine tool malfunction. A skilled technician typically spends 20–40 min gathering symptoms, inspecting, testing, and reaching a diagnosis.
robot_success_rationale: Requires integrating multi-modal sensory data (vibration, sound, visual inspection), domain knowledge, causal reasoning, and reading instrument panels. Current humanoids lack the diagnostic cognition, specialized tool use, and reliable interpretation of subtle machine-state cues needed for autonomous diagnosis.

task: Check courtroom for security and cleanliness and assure availability of sundry supplies, such as notepads, for use by judge, jurors, and attorneys.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single pre-session courtroom check covering security scan, cleanliness inspection, and supply restocking at judge/jury/attorney stations; typically 15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-part inspection (security threats, cleanliness assessment), nuanced judgment, and precise placement of varied supplies across multiple stations. Current robots lack reliable contextual judgment, fine manipulation of small items at varied locations, and autonomous threat recognition.

task: Walk flow lines to locate leaks, using electronic detectors and by making visual inspections, and repair the leaks.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting one flow line segment, using detector and visual checks, then repairing leaks found. A skilled technician typically takes 45–90 min per segment.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extended outdoor/rough terrain locomotion, operating handheld electronic detectors, interpreting sensor readings, and performing plumbing-style repairs. Current humanoids lack outdoor navigation range, tool-use generalization, and repair dexterity for this full pipeline.

task: Eliminate irritating margins of fillings and correct occlusions, using dental instruments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = adjusting margins and occlusion for a single filling. A skilled dentist typically spends 10–30 minutes checking, grinding, and polishing per restoration.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a patient's mouth, real-time tactile/visual feedback, patient cooperation management, and precise use of specialized dental instruments. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities; safety-critical context adds further constraint.
