task: Screen persons entering courthouse using magnetometers, x-ray machines, and other devices to collect and retain unauthorized firearms and other contraband.
human_time_rationale: One instance = screening one person through the checkpoint: directing them, reviewing magnetometer/X-ray results, and handling any flagged items. Typically 1–3 minutes per person.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized fixed security equipment, interpreting X-ray imagery, social interaction/commands with the public, physical pat-downs, and legal authority to detain/confiscate. Far beyond current humanoid capability in unstructured, socially complex, high-stakes environments.

task: Perform oral or periodontal surgery on the jaw or mouth.
human_time_rationale: One surgical procedure (e.g., tooth extraction, periodontal flap surgery, or implant placement); median OR time including prep and closure is ~60–120 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, sterile technique, real-time tactile feedback, and clinical judgment inside a small oral cavity. No general-purpose humanoid robot is remotely capable; even surgical robots require human surgeons to operate them.

task: Administer oral medications or hypodermic injections, following physician's prescriptions and hospital procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = administering a single medication dose (oral or injection) to one patient: verify order, prepare dose, identify patient, administer, and document. Typical nurse time ~5–10 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (drawing up syringes, handling small pills), patient identification, safe injection technique, and real-time patient response monitoring. Current humanoids lack medical precision, sterile technique, and clinical judgment needed for safe autonomous administration.

task: Disassemble defective parts or components, such as inaccurate or worn gauges or measuring instruments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = disassembling one defective gauge/measuring instrument. A skilled technician spends ~15–35 min depending on complexity; 25 min is a reasonable median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle tiny screws, delicate precision components, and varied instrument designs. Current humanoids lack the precision grip, tool-switching, and fault-diagnosis needed for unstructured disassembly of small precision instruments.

task: Maintain machine tools in proper operational condition.
human_time_rationale: One instance = routine maintenance check on a single machine tool (lubrication, cleaning, inspection, minor adjustments). A skilled machinist/technician typically spends ~30–60 min per machine.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-step inspection, lubrication with precision tool access, fault diagnosis, and fine adjustments on complex industrial machinery. Current humanoids lack the specialized tool use, force feedback, and domain perception needed for acceptable quality.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one duty shift (e.g., one cafeteria/hall monitoring period or one bus loading/unloading event), typically 30–60 minutes for a school paraprofessional.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic social perception, crowd management, verbal communication, contextual judgment, and interaction with unpredictable children. Current humanoids lack robust social reasoning, speech interaction, and authority presence needed for safe supervision.

task: Perform minor facial rejuvenation procedures, including the use of Botox and laser technology.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient session combining Botox injection and laser treatment. A trained aesthetic practitioner typically takes 30–60 minutes including prep, consultation, and procedure.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme sub-millimeter dexterity for needle injection into precise facial anatomy, calibrated laser energy delivery, real-time patient response monitoring, and medical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability; safety and regulatory barriers are absolute.

task: Fill pulp chamber and canal with endodontic materials.
human_time_rationale: One instance = filling the pulp chamber and root canal(s) of one tooth. A competent endodontist typically takes 30–60 minutes for obturation, including sealer placement and compaction.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-millimeter dexterity inside a tooth canal, specialized dental instruments, real-time tactile and radiographic feedback, and intraoral access. Far beyond current humanoid robot dexterity and perception capabilities.

task: Choose and prepare sites for new trees, using controlled burning, bulldozers, or herbicides to clear weeds, brush, and logging debris.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one planting site (e.g., ~1 acre). A skilled forestry worker using machinery or herbicides typically takes 3–5 hours per acre, median ~4 hrs.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating bulldozers, conducting controlled burns, and applying herbicides over rough outdoor terrain — all beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. Heavy equipment operation, fire management, and unstructured outdoor navigation are unsolved at autonomy level required.

task: Inspect electronic equipment, instruments, products, or systems to ensure conformance to specifications, safety standards, or applicable codes or regulations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single electronic unit/assembly. A competent inspector spends ~45 min checking specs, safety standards, codes, and documenting findings.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-modal perception (visual, electrical probing, spec cross-referencing), judgment on compliance edge cases, and fine dexterous tool use. Current robots lack reliable electrical test integration, code knowledge, and pass/fail reasoning for complex non-standardized electronics.

task: Remove the deceased from place of death and transport to funeral home.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing one deceased individual from their place of death, loading onto a transport cot, and driving to the funeral home. Typically 30–60 minutes total for a trained removal team.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving a vehicle on public roads (humanoids cannot), navigating highly varied/unstructured environments (homes, hospitals), carefully handling a full human body with a cot, plus legal/procedural steps. Far beyond current robot capabilities.

task: Stamp products with information such as dates, using hand stamps or automatic stamping devices.
human_time_rationale: One instance = stamping a single product. A competent worker inks and presses a stamp onto one item in roughly 30–60 seconds.
robot_success_rationale: Pick-and-place and pressing motions are within robot capability, but aligning a stamp precisely, managing ink consistency, and handling varied product surfaces/orientations challenge current dexterity and perception. Automatic stamping devices would be easier to trigger than hand stamps.

task: Modify and refine designs, using working models, to conform with customer specifications, production limitations, or changes in design trends.
human_time_rationale: One instance = refining a single design iteration using a working model; involves hands-on modification, evaluation, and adjustment cycles. Typically 1–3 hours for a competent designer/engineer.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-level cognitive design reasoning, aesthetic judgment, interpretation of customer specs, and creative problem-solving. Current humanoid robots lack the abstract reasoning, domain expertise, and fine tool-use needed for design iteration work.

task: Screen, control, and handle evidence and exhibits during court proceedings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = managing evidence/exhibits throughout a single court proceeding/hearing; a competent court clerk or bailiff spends ~45 min per proceeding on average.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal knowledge, chain-of-custody judgment, coordination with attorneys/judge, navigation of unpredictable courtroom protocol, and secure handling of sensitive materials. Far beyond current robot generalization and social intelligence.

task: Change dressings on wounds.
human_time_rationale: One instance = changing the dressing on a single wound. Includes removing old dressing, cleaning the wound, and applying new dressing. Typical skilled nurse time: 10–20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise, delicate dexterity on irregular, sensitive tissue; sterile technique; perceptual judgment of wound condition; gentle force control near pain thresholds. Far beyond current humanoid manipulation capability.

task: Provide a variety of materials and resources for children to explore, manipulate, and use, both in learning activities and in imaginative play.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a variety of materials/resources for one session. A competent educator spends ~20 min selecting, gathering, and arranging items for a learning/play activity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced judgment about developmental appropriateness, child safety, and creative variety. Current robots lack the pedagogical reasoning, object-diversity handling, and contextual awareness needed to curate and present materials meaningfully.

task: Provide transportation or escort for expeditions, such as shopping trips or visits to doctors or dentists.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one escort trip (e.g., drive to doctor, wait, return). Typical round-trip with wait time ~60–90 min for a competent caregiver/driver.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving on public roads or navigating varied outdoor/indoor environments while escorting a person. Humanoid robots cannot operate vehicles in traffic; L4 AVs are narrow-domain. Physical escort/navigation with a human in tow is also far beyond current capability.

task: Walk, ride bicycles, drive vehicles, or use public conveyances to reach destinations to deliver messages or materials.
human_time_rationale: One delivery trip (pick up item, travel to destination, hand off). Typical urban courier trip including transit/walking time averages ~20–30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires driving vehicles or riding bicycles in public traffic — beyond any 2026 humanoid's capability. Walking-only sub-tasks are feasible but the full task demands vehicle/bike operation on public roads, which humanoids cannot do.

task: Count money and audit money drawers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = counting and auditing a single cash drawer; a competent cashier typically takes 10–20 minutes to sort, count, record, and reconcile all bills and coins.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle thin, floppy bills and small coins, reliable currency recognition, precise counting logic, and reconciliation with records. Current humanoids lack the fine manipulation and reliable currency-handling needed for acceptable accuracy.

task: Set up, adjust, or operate basic or specialized machine tools used to perform precision machining operations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up and running a single machining job (e.g., one part run on a lathe/mill). A competent machinist typically spends 30–60 min on setup, tooling, and initial operation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise tool selection, fine adjustments, reading gauges, interpreting blueprints, and operating complex machine controls. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, domain-specific tool-use, and judgment for precision machining autonomously.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a typical duty shift (e.g., one cafeteria period, one bus loading event, or one library assistance session), averaging ~60 minutes for a school aide.
robot_success_rationale: Requires dynamic social interaction, crowd management, verbal communication with children, situational judgment, and operating across multiple unpredictable environments. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and social-AI integration in real deployments.

task: Pack, seal, label, or affix postage to prepare materials for shipping, using hand tools, power tools, or postage meter.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one shipment package: folding/packing contents, sealing box with tape, applying label or postage. Typical competent worker completes in ~5–10 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Multi-step task requiring varied dexterity: folding boxes, handling tape/dispenser, precise label placement, and postage meter use. Each sub-step is partially feasible but chaining all reliably without error remains beyond current general-purpose humanoids.

task: Organize and label materials and display students' work.
human_time_rationale: One instance = organizing/labeling a classroom set of materials and mounting a batch of student work displays; a competent teacher/aide typically spends ~30 min per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading handwritten labels, fine manipulation of paper/tape/pins, aesthetic judgment for display layout, navigating cluttered classroom furniture, and interpreting context-specific organizational schemes — all well beyond 2026 robot generalization.

task: Shave, trim, and shape beards and moustaches.
human_time_rationale: One instance = shaving/trimming/shaping a single client's beard and moustache. A skilled barber typically completes this in 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity with sharp tools on irregular, moving human facial geometry. Safety-critical proximity to eyes, nose, and arteries. Current robots lack the tactile precision, compliant force control, and safety guarantees needed.

task: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one classroom for one class session; gathering/arranging materials, setting up furniture, displays, and handouts typically takes 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex multi-step planning, navigating cluttered environments, handling diverse materials (paper, scissors, chairs, projectors), and context-specific judgment. Current humanoids lack reliable generalization for this unstructured, multi-object, multi-room workflow.

task: Measure patients' range-of-joint motion, body parts, or vital signs to determine effects of treatments or for patient evaluations.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient evaluation session measuring ROM, body parts, and/or vital signs for one patient; a competent clinician typically takes 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires gentle, precise contact with a human patient, clinical judgment to position limbs, goniometer/cuff use, and real-time pain/feedback interpretation. Current humanoids lack the fine dexterity, safe force control, and clinical generalization needed.

task: Direct and participate in on- and off-campus recreational activities for residents of institutions, boarding schools, fraternities or sororities, children's homes, or similar establishments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one organized recreational activity session (e.g., a game, outing, or activity period). Typical sessions run 60–120 minutes including setup, participation, and wrap-up.
robot_success_rationale: Requires leadership, social-emotional intelligence, dynamic crowd management, off-campus navigation, and participant engagement — far beyond current robot generalization. Directing human groups in unstructured social settings is essentially unsolved.

task: Fabricate temporary restorations or custom impressions from preliminary impressions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fabricating one temporary restoration or custom tray from a preliminary impression. A skilled dental lab technician or assistant typically takes 20–40 minutes per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity (mixing, trimming, fitting dental materials at millimeter scale), specialized dental tools, sterile technique, and tactile judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability in an unstructured dental setting.

task: Aid patients in performing tasks, such as bathing or keeping beds, clothing, or living areas clean.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one patient with a single care task (e.g., a bed bath or linen change). A competent aide typically spends 15–35 minutes per task.
robot_success_rationale: Unstructured caregiving requires safe contact with a human body, adaptive dexterity, empathetic communication, and real-time judgment. Current humanoids lack the compliance, fine-touch sensing, and social safety needed for patient care.

task: Inspect work progress, equipment, or construction sites to verify safety or to ensure that specifications are met.
human_time_rationale: One inspection instance = a single site/equipment walk-through. A competent inspector typically spends 30–60 min covering a work zone, checking specs, safety hazards, and documenting findings.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can navigate and capture visual data, but reliably interpreting construction specs, identifying subtle safety violations, traversing cluttered/outdoor uneven terrain, and producing an accepted inspection report exceeds current generalization and reasoning limits.

task: Assist patients to dress, undress, or put on and remove supportive devices, such as braces, splints, or slings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = helping one patient dress/undress or apply/remove one device. A competent aide typically takes 5–15 minutes depending on patient mobility and complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Requires gentle, adaptive dexterity around fragile human bodies, interpreting patient cues, handling soft/irregular garments and custom orthotics, and dynamic safety compliance — all well beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Clean, maintain, calibrate, or repair measuring instruments or test equipment, such as dial indicators, fixed gauges, or height gauges.
human_time_rationale: One instance = servicing a single instrument (e.g., a dial indicator). A competent metrology tech spends ~30–60 min cleaning, calibrating, and documenting one gauge.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to handle delicate precision instruments, specialized calibration knowledge, use of reference standards, and subtle tactile/visual feedback. Far beyond current humanoid robot capability in unstructured settings.

task: Maintain computer equipment used in instruction.
human_time_rationale: One instance = servicing a single instructional computer (cleaning, checking cables, updating software, minor hardware fixes). ~45 min for a competent IT tech.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (cable routing, peripheral connections), navigating cluttered workspaces, running software diagnostics, and contextual judgment about faults. Current humanoids lack reliable tool-use and software interaction for this composite task.

task: Offer health promotion or prevention activities, such as training people to use blood pressure devices or diabetes monitors.
human_time_rationale: One instance = training a single person or small group to use a device (e.g., blood pressure cuff or glucometer), including demonstration, practice, and Q&A.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fluid verbal instruction, adaptive communication, empathy, real-time comprehension assessment, and trust-building — far beyond current humanoid social/cognitive capabilities. Physical demo component is secondary to teaching quality.

task: Fight forest fires or perform prescribed burning tasks under the direction of fire suppression officers or forestry technicians.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one operational shift (approx. 8 hours) of active firefighting or prescribed burn work; standard unit measured by fire management teams.
robot_success_rationale: Requires rugged outdoor locomotion over steep/uneven burning terrain, tool use (hand tools, hoses, drip torches), real-time situational awareness in smoke/heat, and coordinated team communication — far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities. Extreme safety risk disqualifies autonomous deployment.

task: Transport patients to the hospital or within the hospital.
human_time_rationale: One instance = transporting one patient within or to a hospital (e.g., wheelchair/gurney from room to imaging). Typical trip takes 15–25 minutes including prep and handoff.
robot_success_rationale: Requires safely maneuvering a patient on a wheelchair/gurney through crowded, dynamic hospital corridors, communicating with staff/patient, operating elevators, and managing safety — far beyond current humanoid generalization and caregiving capability.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one student interaction (providing a device, tech setup, or facility access assist). Median across device handoff (~5 min) and restroom/facility assistance (~20–30 min) averages ~20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social interaction, adaptive caregiving, fine dexterity for varied assistive devices, safe physical guidance of a person, and real-time judgment about individual needs — far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Open and close cash registers, performing tasks such as counting money, separating charge slips, coupons, and vouchers, balancing cash drawers, and making deposits.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full open/close cycle for one register: counting cash, sorting slips/coupons/vouchers, balancing drawer, preparing deposit. Competent cashier typically takes 15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity to count/sort thin paper bills, slips, and coupons; reading and reconciling numerical totals; interacting with a POS system; and handling secure deposit procedures. Far beyond reliable 2026 robot capability.

task: Thin or space trees, using power thinning saws.
human_time_rationale: One instance = thinning trees in one typical stand section (~0.25 acre). A skilled arborist/logger selects and fells trees with a power saw; ~45 min accounting for selection, cutting, and clearing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor uneven terrain navigation, complex arboricultural judgment for tree selection, safe operation of a high-powered chainsaw, and managing falling trees — all well beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. Extreme safety risks further preclude autonomous use.

task: Administer medications to patients and monitor patients for reactions or side effects.
human_time_rationale: One instance = administering a single medication dose to one patient and observing for initial reactions; includes preparation, administration, and short post-dose monitoring.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (drawing doses, IV handling), clinical judgment for reaction monitoring, patient interaction/safety, and regulatory/liability constraints. Current humanoids lack the perception, dexterity, and medical reasoning needed.

task: Erect signs or fences, using posthole diggers, shovels, or other hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing one post (digging hole, setting post, backfilling/tamping). A competent worker takes ~30–60 min depending on soil and depth.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-force tool use (posthole digger, shovel) in unstructured outdoor terrain, precise vertical alignment, and sustained torque application. Current humanoids lack the strength, stability, and outdoor generalization for this task.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one monitoring/assistance shift covering a single duty period (e.g., one cafeteria lunch period, one bus loading event, or one library assistance session), averaging ~45 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social judgment, dynamic crowd management, verbal interaction with children, and situational authority. Current humanoids lack reliable language-grounded social reasoning, crowd navigation, and autonomous decision-making in unstructured school environments.

task: Examine teeth, gums, and related tissues, using dental instruments, x-rays, or other diagnostic equipment, to evaluate dental health, diagnose diseases or abnormalities, and plan appropriate treatments.
human_time_rationale: One full dental examination per patient: visual inspection, probing, X-rays, and charting. Typical comprehensive exam runs 30–60 minutes for a competent dentist.
robot_success_rationale: Requires licensed clinical judgment, fine intraoral dexterity with specialized instruments, safe X-ray operation, disease diagnosis, and treatment planning — far beyond current humanoid robot capability in perception, dexterity, and medical reasoning.

task: Prune or shear tree tops or limbs to control growth, increase density, or improve shape.
human_time_rationale: One instance = pruning/shearing one medium tree. A competent arborist typically spends 30–60 minutes assessing, positioning, and cutting a single tree.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing or reaching tall canopies, operating pruning shears/saws on irregular branches outdoors, making aesthetic/horticultural judgments, and navigating uneven terrain. Far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Issue medications from dispensary and maintain records in accordance with specified procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = dispensing a single patient's medication order: pulling correct drugs, verifying dosage, labeling, logging, and handing off. Typically 10–20 minutes in a pharmacy/dispensary setting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading prescriptions, navigating complex pharmacy shelving, fine dexterous handling of varied pill bottles/blister packs, accurate labeling, regulatory record-keeping, and safety verification — far beyond 2026 general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Observe and evaluate children's performance, behavior, social development, and physical health.
human_time_rationale: One instance = observing and evaluating one child across a typical structured session (e.g., a classroom period or play session). A competent teacher/childcare worker spends ~20–40 min per child per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social-emotional perception, developmental knowledge, and holistic judgment. Current robots lack reliable affect recognition, behavioral context understanding, and clinical/educational evaluation capabilities needed for acceptable quality.

task: Transport patients to and from treatment areas, lifting and transferring them according to positioning requirements.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient transport (room to treatment area and back), including lift/transfer and positioning. Typically 10–15 minutes for a competent aide.
robot_success_rationale: Requires safe physical handling of a fragile, uncooperative, or unconscious human body, navigating busy clinical corridors, and precise positioning. Current humanoids lack the strength, compliance, safety certification, and generalization for unstructured caregiving transfers.

task: Sew together sections of material to form mockups or samples of garments or articles, using sewing equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sewing one mockup/sample garment (e.g. a shirt or jacket shell). A competent sample machinist typically spends 30–60 min assembling and stitching a basic garment mockup.
robot_success_rationale: Fabric manipulation is extremely challenging for robots: limp, deformable material requires precise bimanual dexterity, alignment, and feeding under a sewing machine. No current general-purpose humanoid can reliably handle, guide, and sew fabric sections to production quality.

task: Remove broken limbs from wires, using hooked extension poles.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing one broken limb from wires. A worker positions, hooks, and clears a single snagged limb using an extension pole; ~10–20 min including repositioning.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor uneven terrain, precise manipulation of long extension poles overhead, variable limb sizes/positions on live or high wires, and reaching 20–40 ft up. Far beyond current humanoid mobility, reach, and tool-use generalization.

task: Interview patients to obtain medical information and measure their vital signs, weight, and height.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one patient intake session including brief interview, vitals (BP, pulse, temp, O2), weight, and height. Typical clinical workflow takes ~10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires natural language medical interviewing, nuanced patient interaction, precise instrument use (BP cuff, thermometer, scale, stadiometer), and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and dexterity in unstructured care settings.

task: Apply fluoride or sealants to teeth.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying fluoride or sealant to one patient's teeth. A dental hygienist/dentist typically takes 10–20 minutes including prep, application, and drying/curing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise intraoral dexterity in a confined, wet, sensitive environment, patient compliance management, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid robot fine-motor and caregiving capabilities. High safety stakes.

task: Diagnose health problems by reviewing patients' health and medical histories, questioning, observing, and examining patients and interpreting x-rays.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full diagnostic consultation per patient: reviewing history, interview, physical exam, and imaging review. Typical outpatient visit runs 30–60 minutes for a competent physician.
robot_success_rationale: Requires medical licensing, nuanced verbal interviewing, physical palpation/auscultation with clinical judgment, and radiological interpretation. Current humanoids lack medical sensors, clinical reasoning AI integration, regulatory clearance, and safe patient-interaction capability.

task: Restrain animals during exams or procedures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = restraining one animal for a single exam or procedure. A competent vet tech typically spends ~5–15 minutes holding/restraining the animal safely.
robot_success_rationale: Requires adaptive force control on a living, unpredictable, squirming animal without causing injury. Current robots lack the compliant, context-sensitive grip, animal-reading instincts, and safe force limits needed. Extremely high failure/harm risk.

task: Deliver or route materials to departments using handtruck, conveyor, or sorting bins.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one delivery run to a department: loading items, transporting via handtruck/conveyor/bins, and unloading. Typical intra-facility run takes ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can navigate flat indoor corridors and do basic pick-and-place, but reliably operating a handtruck, sorting bins, and routing across dynamic, multi-department environments with varied loads remains beyond consistent autonomous capability in 2026.

task: Diagnose and treat diseases, injuries, or malformations of teeth, gums, or related oral structures and provide preventive or corrective services.
human_time_rationale: One patient appointment (diagnosis + treatment). A typical dental visit runs 45–75 minutes; 60 min is a reasonable median for a competent dentist.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a small oral cavity, complex clinical reasoning, diagnostic judgment, and patient interaction. Far beyond 2026 robot capability; even specialized dental robots are remote-controlled, not autonomous.

task: Escort prisoners to and from courthouse and maintain custody of prisoners during court proceedings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = escorting a prisoner from holding facility to courthouse, attending proceedings, and returning. A typical court appearance with transit takes ~3 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires legal authority, complex social/situational judgment, restraint application, navigation of crowded public spaces, unpredictable prisoner behavior, and coordination with court officials. Far beyond current robot autonomy and legal frameworks.

task: Check equipment to ensure that it is operating properly.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single piece of equipment (e.g., a machine or device). A competent worker typically takes ~15 min to visually inspect, test, and verify basic operation.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can perform visual inspection and basic sensor checks, but verifying proper operation often requires interpreting gauges, sounds, vibrations, and anomalies in unstructured settings — beyond reliable frontier-robot capability. Generalization and judgment remain weak.

task: Diagnose electrical problems and install and rewire electrical components.
human_time_rationale: One instance = diagnosing a single electrical fault (e.g., tracing a circuit issue, testing components). A competent electrician typically spends 30–90 min per fault diagnosis.
robot_success_rationale: Requires multi-meter use, interpreting schematics, probing live circuits, and abstract fault reasoning. Current robots lack reliable tool-use with test equipment and diagnostic cognition for unstructured electrical systems.

task: Prepare treatment areas and electrotherapy equipment for use by physiotherapists.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one treatment area and its electrotherapy equipment (e.g., TENS/ultrasound unit) ready for the next patient; includes wiping surfaces, laying paper, checking/setting up device.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine manipulation of medical cables, electrodes, and device controls; navigating a cluttered clinical space; hygiene compliance; and interpreting varied equipment interfaces — all well beyond reliable 2026 humanoid capability in unstructured medical settings.

task: Unload and sort items collected along delivery routes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = unloading and sorting a typical delivery vehicle's collected items (e.g. parcels, returns). A competent worker takes ~25–35 min for a moderately loaded route collection.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing in/out of a vehicle, handling varied package shapes/weights, reading labels, and sorting to correct destinations. Perception and locomotion in confined vehicle space, plus generalization across item types, remain major bottlenecks for 2026 humanoids.

task: Monitor production operations or equipment to ensure conformance to specifications, making necessary process or assembly adjustments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one monitoring cycle/round on a production line, checking equipment and making adjustments. A competent operator typically takes ~30 min per inspection round.
robot_success_rationale: Requires integrated perception (reading gauges, detecting anomalies), contextual judgment, and physical adjustments to varied controls/valves. Current humanoids lack reliable multi-modal process monitoring and corrective decision-making in unstructured factory settings.

task: Collect, seal, and stamp outgoing mail, using postage meters and envelope sealers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = processing a batch of outgoing mail (collecting, sealing, and stamping ~10–20 pieces); a typical office batch takes ~10–20 minutes for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise dexterous manipulation of thin envelopes, operating specialized machines (postage meter, envelope sealer) with varied controls, and reliable feeding of floppy paper items — all well beyond current humanoid gripper reliability and machine-interfacing ability.

task: Clean or maintain photoprocessing or darkroom equipment, using ultrasonic equipment or cleaning and rinsing solutions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning/maintaining a set of darkroom/photoprocessing equipment. A competent technician typically spends ~20–40 min on chemical rinsing, ultrasonic bath use, and drying.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise handling of delicate optical/chemical equipment, controlled use of ultrasonic baths and specific chemical solutions, fine dexterity, and chemical safety judgment — far beyond current humanoid robot generalization and manipulation capability.

task: Inspect proposed sites to identify structural elements of land areas or other important site information, such as soil condition, existing landscaping, or the proximity of water management facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single site visit/inspection. A competent surveyor or site assessor typically spends 60–120 minutes walking, probing, documenting soil, drainage, vegetation, and nearby infrastructure at a typical parcel.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor/unstructured terrain navigation, expert soil probing, multi-factor environmental reasoning, and professional judgment. Current humanoids lack all-terrain mobility, soil-testing tools, and domain-expert interpretation needed for acceptable autonomous site assessment.

task: Prepare or incubate slides with cell cultures.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing and incubating one batch of slides. Includes seeding/coating slides, placing in incubator, and setting conditions. ~20–40 min of active work.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise pipetting of biological samples onto glass slides, sterile technique, fine liquid handling, and incubator operation. Current humanoids lack the sub-millimeter dexterity and sterile-field compliance needed for lab cell culture work.

task: Stack or arrange tested products for further processing, shipping, or packaging.
human_time_rationale: One instance = stacking/arranging a single batch of tested products (e.g., a tray or small lot). A competent worker handles this quickly in a structured production environment.
robot_success_rationale: Pick-and-place of uniform products on flat surfaces is well within 2026 robot capability. Variability in product shape, size, orientation, and stacking precision (without fixed jigs) reduces reliability. Perception and grasp planning handle moderate variation but struggle with edge cases.

task: Dump refuse or recyclable materials at disposal sites.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one truck unload at a disposal/recycling site: drive to tipping area, position vehicle, actuate dump mechanism, clear any jams, and exit. Typically 10–20 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating a refuse truck (driving, positioning, actuating hydraulic dump bed) on a busy, unstructured disposal site. Humanoid robots cannot drive or operate heavy vehicles in real traffic/site conditions; highly specialized controls and safety demands make autonomous completion near-impossible in 2026.

task: Construct or fabricate parts, using hand tools, according to specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = fabricating a single part with hand tools (e.g., cutting, shaping, filing metal/wood to spec). A typical part takes 30–60 min for a competent tradesperson.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity with varied hand tools (files, saws, chisels, clamps), interpreting specs, and adaptive force control. Current humanoids lack the precision, tool-switching fluency, and spec-reading integration for unstructured fabrication tasks.

task: Ticket, arrange, and display merchandise to promote sales.
human_time_rationale: One instance = ticketing, arranging, and displaying a batch of merchandise in one section/display area; a competent retail worker typically takes 30–60 minutes per section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (attaching price tickets), aesthetic judgment for display arrangement, varied object handling, and retail environment navigation. Current humanoids lack reliable fine-fingered tagging and creative merchandising judgment.

task: Instruct novices in climbing techniques, mountaineering, and wilderness survival, and demonstrate use of hunting, fishing, and climbing equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single instructional session covering climbing, mountaineering, survival, and equipment demos for a novice group; typically 2 hours in a field/gym setting.
robot_success_rationale: Requires adaptive pedagogy, real-time safety judgment, rough outdoor terrain mobility, fine dexterous equipment handling, and credible demonstration of climbing/fishing/hunting gear. Far beyond 2026 robot generalization and social capability.

task: Inspect government-owned equipment or materials in the possession of private contractors to ensure compliance with contracts or regulations or to prevent misuse.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one contractor site inspection visit; a competent government property inspector typically spends 60–120 min checking equipment condition, serial numbers, records, and compliance status.
robot_success_rationale: Requires cross-referencing contract documents, reading serial/asset tags in variable environments, judgment on compliance, and navigating unstructured contractor facilities. Current robots lack the cognitive reasoning, document handling, and regulatory judgment needed.

task: Perform routine maintenance on delivery vehicles, such as monitoring fluid levels and replenishing fuel.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full routine check on one delivery vehicle: checking oil, coolant, washer fluid, tire pressure, and refueling. A competent worker completes this in ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating fuel pumps, opening/closing vehicle hoods and caps, checking fluid dipsticks, and maneuvering around a large vehicle outdoors. Humanoids lack the specialized tool-use, perception, and force control for these vehicle-specific interactions; fluid handling and refueling nozzles are extremely difficult autonomously.

task: Check the forms that hold the concrete to see that they are properly constructed.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting formwork for a single concrete pour section; worker checks alignment, bracing, ties, and seals — typically 15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating active, cluttered construction sites (rough terrain, debris, rebar), visually assessing structural adequacy of bracing/ties/alignment, and exercising expert judgment — far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Deliver messages and items, such as newspapers, documents, and packages, between establishment departments and to other establishments and private homes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single delivery trip (picking up an item and delivering it to one destination). Typical inter-department or local delivery takes ~10–20 min including transit.
robot_success_rationale: Indoor flat-terrain navigation is feasible (~70–85%), but outdoor/multi-site trips, arbitrary door handling, item handoff to recipients, elevators, and varied terrain greatly reduce success. End-to-end autonomous delivery across diverse environments remains unreliable for current humanoids.

task: Clean aircraft structures, parts, or components, using aqueous, semi-aqueous, aliphatic hydrocarbon, or organic solvent cleaning products or techniques to reduce carbon or other harmful emissions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning a single aircraft structural part/component. A competent technician spends ~60 min selecting solvents, masking, scrubbing, rinsing, and inspecting one medium-sized part.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise chemical selection, masking sensitive areas, handling specialized solvents with safety compliance, and inspecting complex aerospace geometry. Current humanoids lack the dexterity, chemical handling protocols, and aerospace-specific tool-use needed for autonomous success.

task: Conduct inspections, using survey instruments, metering devices, tape measures, or test equipment.
human_time_rationale: One inspection instance (e.g., a room, unit, or site segment) using handheld instruments; a competent inspector typically spends 30–60 min per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Robots struggle to autonomously select, pick up, position, and read diverse handheld instruments (tape measures, meters, probes) across unstructured sites. Perception of readings, fine-grip tool use, and generalization to varied equipment are major bottlenecks.

task: Clean, shape, and polish fingernails and toenails, using files and nail polish.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full manicure + pedicure for one client: cleaning, filing/shaping, and polishing all 10 fingernails and 10 toenails. ~45 min is typical for a combined service.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity on curved, variable nail surfaces near sensitive skin; precise brush control for polish; tool-switching; adapting to each client's anatomy. Far beyond current humanoid manipulation capability.

task: Install equipment to improve the energy or operational efficiency of residential or commercial buildings.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing a single efficiency upgrade (e.g., smart thermostat, insulation panel, or LED retrofit) in one building zone; a competent technician averages ~4 hours including prep, install, and testing.
robot_success_rationale: Requires diverse tools, electrical/mechanical connections, navigating tight spaces (attics, crawlspaces), reading specs, and safety judgment. Far beyond 2026 humanoid generalization and dexterous tool-use capabilities.

task: Maintain cage security according to rules.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single security check of a cage (e.g., animal enclosure, detention cell, or secure storage cage): inspecting locks, latches, structural integrity, and access points.
robot_success_rationale: Requires visual inspection of latches/locks, testing mechanical integrity, and applying rule-based judgment. Current robots can navigate and perceive, but reliably checking varied hardware, detecting subtle damage, and responding to anomalies is still unreliable.

task: Open and close doors for passengers.
human_time_rationale: One instance = opening and closing a single door for one passenger; a competent human takes ~30 seconds including approaching, opening, waiting, and closing.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can grasp handles at ~40–70% but must also track passenger timing, hold door open safely, and close it smoothly—requiring social awareness, precise timing, and reliable mobility in busy entryways, all of which remain weak.

task: Produce rough concrete surface, using broom.
human_time_rationale: One instance = brooming a freshly poured concrete slab of typical residential/commercial pour (~20–30 sq ft section). A skilled finisher takes ~10–20 min per section.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise timing (concrete must be at exact workability window), consistent broom pressure/angle on a wet, uneven surface, bimanual tool control, and outdoor/unstructured terrain navigation. Far beyond 2026 robot capability.

task: Remove diseased tissue, using surgical instruments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single surgical excision procedure. A competent surgeon typically spends 30–90 minutes on tissue debridement or resection, median ~60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity, sterile technique, real-time haptic feedback, anatomical judgment, and adaptive decision-making far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. Fine surgical work is rated <5%.

task: Open or close information facilities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = opening or closing a single information facility (e.g., a library or visitor center): unlocking doors, switching lights/signs, securing/enabling public access points. ~15 min per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires unlocking doors, toggling breakers/signage, checking alarms, and contextual judgment about facility state. Door-opening is feasible (~50%), but multi-step facility management, key use, and coordinating utility systems far exceed current robot generalization.

task: Provide interpretation and transcription of regular classroom materials through Braille and sign language.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one standard class session (~60 min). A human interpreter/transcriber provides real-time Braille or sign language support throughout a lesson.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fluent, real-time American Sign Language production and Braille transcription, deep language understanding, classroom context awareness, and adaptive communication — all far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities. No frontier robot demonstrates this.

task: Test agricultural machinery and equipment to ensure adequate performance.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one piece of agricultural machinery (e.g., a tractor or combine). A competent technician spends ~30–60 min running checks, test cycles, and documenting results.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating large, specialized agricultural vehicles and equipment in varied outdoor terrain, interpreting gauges/controls, and making performance judgments. Current humanoids cannot drive or operate heavy farm machinery autonomously at acceptable quality.

task: Provide assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one person with a single need (e.g., setting up a device, escorting to restroom). Median across device setup, facility navigation, and physical assistance.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social understanding, safe physical contact with vulnerable individuals, fine dexterity for assistive devices, unstructured environment navigation, and real-time adaptive caregiving — well beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Restrain violent, potentially violent, or suicidal patients by verbal or physical means as required.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one restraint episode (verbal de-escalation through physical restraint and stabilization). Median duration from onset to patient calm/secured is ~15–25 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires real-time social/emotional perception, nuanced verbal de-escalation, safe physical contact with an unpredictable human, and legal/ethical accountability. Current humanoids lack the social cognition, safe force control, and situational judgment needed. High injury/harm risk makes autonomous action unacceptable.

task: Perform equipment safety checks prior to departure.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full pre-departure safety check of one vehicle/piece of equipment (e.g., truck, bus, or heavy machine). A competent operator typically takes 10–20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires walking around equipment, visually inspecting lights, tires, fluids, brakes, and mirrors, reading gauges, and logging results. Current robots lack reliable fluid-level reading, gauge interpretation, and generalized inspection checklists across varied equipment types.

task: Explain and demonstrate safety procedures and safety equipment use.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single safety demonstration session (e.g., for a new worker or group), covering key procedures and PPE use. Typically 20–40 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires verbal communication, dynamic instruction, audience interaction, correct donning/doffing of varied PPE, and contextual adaptation. Current humanoids lack reliable speech generation, instructional coherence, and social interaction needed for this role.

task: Identify diseased or undesirable trees and remove them, using power saws or hand saws.
human_time_rationale: One instance = identifying and felling one tree. Includes assessment, setup, cutting, and basic limbing. Median time for a competent arborist is ~1–1.5 hours.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured terrain navigation, expert disease diagnosis via visual inspection, safe directional felling with chainsaws, and judgment under high-stakes hazard conditions. Current humanoids lack all of these capabilities reliably.

task: Expose dental diagnostic x-rays.
human_time_rationale: One instance = full-mouth or single-series X-ray session for one patient: positioning sensor/film, aligning cone, and exposing all views (~15 min for a competent dental radiographer).
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise intraoral sensor placement on a cooperative patient, fine dexterity with positioning arms/cones, radiation safety compliance, and patient communication. No current humanoid robot has the specialized dexterity, certified medical tool operation, or patient-interaction capability needed.

task: Install or repair accessories.
human_time_rationale: One instance = installing or repairing a single accessory (e.g., a bracket, hook, handle, or trim piece). A competent worker typically takes 20–45 minutes depending on complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Highly variable task requiring tool use (screwdrivers, drills), fine alignment, and contextual judgment about accessory type/location. Current humanoids lack reliable fine dexterity and adaptive tool-use for unstructured repair/installation scenarios.

task: Package finished products and prepare them for shipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = packaging a single finished product and labeling/sealing it for shipment; a competent worker in a fulfillment/manufacturing setting takes ~10–15 min per unit.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick-and-place items into boxes but struggle with varied packaging formats, sealing tape, inserting packing materials, applying labels accurately, and handling diverse product shapes without dropping or misaligning.

task: Train patients in the use of orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single patient training session covering fitting, demonstration, and supervised practice of one orthopedic/prosthetic device.
robot_success_rationale: Requires adaptive verbal instruction, reading patient pain/discomfort cues, fine dexterous fitting adjustments, and clinical judgment. Far beyond current humanoid capability in unstructured caregiving and nuanced human interaction.

task: Treat exposure of pulp by pulp capping, removal of pulp from pulp chamber, or root canal, using dental instruments.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one tooth treated via pulp capping, pulpotomy, or root canal. Root canals average 60–90 min; pulp capping ~30 min; blended median ~75 min for a competent dentist.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sub-millimeter precision inside a small oral cavity, real-time tactile feedback, sterile technique, patient management, and specialized dental instruments. Far beyond current humanoid dexterity and safety thresholds.

task: Set up medical laboratory equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a single piece of lab equipment (e.g., centrifuge, analyzer, or microscope): unpacking, positioning, connecting cables/tubing, calibrating, and verifying readiness. ~45 min median.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity for fragile, precision components; reading equipment-specific manuals; connecting delicate tubing/cables; running calibration software. Far beyond current humanoid generalization and manipulation fidelity.

task: Weigh materials, products, containers, or samples to verify packaging weights or ingredient quantities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = weighing a single item/container. A worker picks it up, places it on a scale, reads the result, and logs or verifies it — roughly 1–3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Pick-and-place to a scale is within humanoid capability (~70–80%), but reading a scale display, interpreting results, and reliably handling varied container shapes/weights reduces success. Logging/decision-making adds further uncertainty.

task: Organize and label materials and display children's work in a manner appropriate for their sizes and perceptual skills.
human_time_rationale: One instance = organizing/labeling materials and displaying one classroom set of children's work. A competent teacher/aide typically takes 20–30 minutes per session.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading/interpreting children's developmental levels, aesthetic/pedagogical judgment, fine manipulation of paper/labels, wall mounting, and adapting display height to child sizes — far beyond current robot generalization and cognitive capability.

task: Provide food and beverage services to guests.
human_time_rationale: One instance = serving one table/guest order, including taking orders, delivering food/drinks, and checking in. Typical restaurant turn averages ~15 min of active service per table visit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigation in crowded spaces, tray balancing, social interaction, order-taking, and adapting to guest needs. Current humanoids lack reliable fluid/tray manipulation, conversational guest interaction, and safe navigation in dynamic restaurant environments.

task: Perform various cash handling tasks, such as collecting payments, making bank deposits, or managing petty cash.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single cash handling transaction cycle (e.g., collecting a payment, counting/reconciling, and logging it). A competent cashier/clerk takes ~15–25 min on average.
robot_success_rationale: Handling paper bills and coins requires extreme fine dexterity; operating POS terminals, safes, or bank deposit systems is highly tool/environment-specific. Counting, sorting, and verifying currency remains beyond reliable autonomous robot capability in 2026. Cognitive accuracy requirements are also very high.

task: Observe and document activities of individuals to detect unlawful acts or to obtain evidence for cases, using binoculars and still or video cameras.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single surveillance session. A competent investigator/detective typically conducts 1–3 hour stakeouts; 120 min is a reasonable median unit billed/logged by a manager.
robot_success_rationale: Requires covert mobility in unstructured outdoor/urban environments, sustained contextual judgment, legal/ethical decision-making, and operating cameras/binoculars discreetly. Current humanoids lack stealth, situational awareness, and autonomous evidence-gathering cognition for real-world surveillance.

task: Mix cement, sand, and water to produce concrete, grout, or slurry, using hoe, trowel, tamper, scraper, or concrete-mixing machine.
human_time_rationale: One instance = mixing one batch (~1–2 cubic feet) of concrete/grout/slurry by hand or small machine; a competent worker measures, combines, and mixes to proper consistency in ~15–25 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires controlled force with tools (hoe, trowel), sensing mix consistency (visual + tactile), and adapting technique — well beyond current robot generalization. Operating a mixing machine adds tool-use and mobility complexity. Consistency judgement is a key failure point.

task: Pilot airplanes or drive land and water vehicles to transport tourists to activity or tour sites.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one tourist transport trip (e.g., a typical tour shuttle, boat ride, or short flight to a site), estimated at ~60 minutes including boarding and transit.
robot_success_rationale: Piloting aircraft, driving boats, or land vehicles in dynamic public/outdoor environments is far beyond current humanoid robot capability. No 2026 general-purpose robot can operate these vehicles safely and autonomously at transport quality.

task: Provide emergency first aid and summon medical assistance when necessary.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single emergency first-aid event (e.g., CPR, wound dressing, calling EMS) from onset to handoff; ~15 min is a typical median for a trained responder.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex, adaptive physical interventions (CPR compressions, bandaging, airway management) plus situational judgment and reliable voice/phone-based EMS contact. Current humanoids lack medical dexterity, force control, and reliable emergency communication integration.

task: Work closely with photographers, fashion coordinators, directors, producers, stylists, make-up artists, other models, and clients to produce the desired looks, and to finish photo shoots on schedule.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full photo shoot session. A typical shoot runs 3–5 hours including setup, direction, posing, and coordination across multiple collaborators.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social collaboration, creative expression, dynamic posing, emotional communication, and real-time interpretation of artistic direction — far beyond any current robot's social, expressive, or creative generalization capabilities.

task: Design, weld, and fabricate parts, using blueprints or other mechanical plans.
human_time_rationale: One instance = designing, welding, and fabricating a single part or small assembly from blueprints. A typical job-shop fabrication task takes 2–4 hours for a skilled tradesperson.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading blueprints, operating welding equipment with precise heat/arc control, repositioning heavy stock, and fine dexterous manipulation. No 2026 general-purpose humanoid can safely operate welding torches or CNC equipment autonomously in an unstructured fab shop.

task: Treat bone, muscle, and joint disorders affecting the feet and ankles.
human_time_rationale: One patient consultation/treatment session by a podiatrist or orthopedic specialist: assessment, diagnosis, and treatment (e.g., injection, casting, manual therapy) for a foot/ankle disorder.
robot_success_rationale: Requires expert clinical judgment, fine dexterous manipulation on sensitive anatomy, patient communication, and medical tool use (syringes, scalpels, imaging). Far beyond current humanoid robot medical capability.

task: Prepare treatment rooms for patient examinations, keeping the rooms neat and clean.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one treatment room between patients: wiping surfaces, replacing paper, restocking supplies, tidying equipment. Typically 8–12 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires navigating a cluttered clinical room, wiping varied surfaces, replacing exam table paper, restocking small supplies, and recognizing cleanliness standards. Dexterity, contextual judgment, and safety compliance in a medical setting remain well beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = assisting one student with a single need (e.g., setting up a device, escorting to restroom, configuring software). Typically 10–20 minutes per interaction.
robot_success_rationale: Requires nuanced social interaction, individualized assessment, configuring diverse assistive tech, and safe physical caregiving. Current robots lack reliable dexterity, generalization across devices, and safe unstructured human assistance needed here.

task: Cut or break flashing from materials or products.
human_time_rationale: One instance = removing flashing from a single part. A competent worker trims/breaks flashing from one molded or cast part in ~2–4 minutes using hand tools or cutters.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise force control and tool use (cutters, knives) along irregular seam lines on varied part geometries. Perception of thin flashing edges and dexterous trimming without damaging the part exceeds reliable 2026 robot capability.

task: Scrape decayed matter from cavities in trees and fill holes with cement to promote healing and to prevent further deterioration.
human_time_rationale: One instance = treating one tree cavity. Scraping decay, cleaning the cavity, and packing/smoothing cement takes ~30–60 min for a competent arborist.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor unstructured terrain around a tree, precise scraping of irregular organic cavities, fine force-controlled tool use, and applying/smoothing cement in confined spaces. Far beyond current general-purpose robot capabilities.

task: Stock shelves with products.
human_time_rationale: One instance = stocking one shelf section (e.g., one bay) with a cart of products. A competent retail worker takes ~25–35 minutes to unbox, orient, and place items neatly.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick and place boxes but struggle with varied product shapes, orientations, label-facing, tight shelf spacing, and navigating a live retail aisle. Generalization across SKUs and unstructured environments remains limited in 2026.

task: Collect blood, tissue, or other laboratory specimens, log the specimens, and prepare them for testing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting a specimen from one patient, logging it, and preparing it for testing. A competent phlebotomist/lab tech typically completes this in ~10–20 minutes end-to-end.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine dexterity (venipuncture, tube labeling), sterile technique, patient interaction/compliance, and precise multi-step lab prep. Far beyond current humanoid robot capabilities in unstructured clinical settings.

task: Spread roofing paper on surface of foundation, and spread concrete onto roofing paper with trowel to form terrazzo base.
human_time_rationale: One instance = laying roofing paper and spreading concrete base over a standard room section (~10 sq ft). Skilled terrazzo worker takes ~45 min including paper placement and troweling.
robot_success_rationale: Requires coordinated two-hand manipulation of large flexible roofing paper, precise troweling of wet concrete to uniform depth/flatness, and sustained force control on uneven ground — far beyond current humanoid dexterity and full-body coordination.

task: Furnish change persons with a money bank at the start of each shift.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up one cashier/change person with a money bank (counting, bagging, and handing over cash drawer) at shift start; typically takes ~5 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires handling loose currency (highly deformable, fine dexterity), counting bills/coins accurately, secure cash accountability procedures, and interacting with cash drawers — well beyond current humanoid robot capability.

task: Operate skidders, bulldozers, or other prime movers to pull a variety of scarification or site preparation equipment over areas to be regenerated.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one forestry site area (e.g., a few acres). A competent operator typically spends a half-day shift running equipment over a defined regeneration plot.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy forestry machinery (skidders, bulldozers) across rough, uneven, unpredictable terrain. No humanoid robot can drive or control such specialized heavy equipment autonomously in unstructured outdoor forest environments.

task: Spread, level, and smooth concrete, using rake, shovel, hand or power trowel, hand or power screed, and float.
human_time_rationale: One instance = finishing a standard concrete pour (e.g., ~100 sq ft slab). A skilled finisher spreads, screeds, and trowels in roughly 45–90 minutes depending on size and conditions.
robot_success_rationale: Requires coordinated whole-body force application with multiple large tools (rake, screed, trowel, float) on a fluid, time-sensitive material outdoors. Current humanoids lack the strength, proprioceptive force control, gait stability on wet concrete, and multi-tool sequencing needed.

task: Circulate among potential customers or travel by foot, truck, automobile, or bicycle to deliver or sell merchandise or services.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a full route/shift (e.g., a delivery or sales route), typically 3–5 hours including travel, customer stops, and transactions.
robot_success_rationale: Requires driving/cycling on public roads (humanoids cannot operate vehicles), navigating to unknown customers, conducting sales interactions, and handling transactions — far beyond current autonomous robot capabilities.

task: Cut out damaged areas, drill holes for reinforcing rods, and position reinforcing rods to repair concrete, using power saw and drill.
human_time_rationale: One instance = repairing a single damaged concrete section. A competent worker spends ~60 min cutting, drilling, and positioning rebar for a typical repair patch.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor/unstructured terrain mobility, precise power-saw and drill operation on irregular concrete surfaces, heavy tool force control, and accurate rebar positioning — well beyond current humanoid dexterity and generalization limits.

task: Design, make, or fit prosthodontic appliances, such as space maintainers, bridges, or dentures, or write fabrication instructions or prescriptions for denturists or dental technicians.
human_time_rationale: One instance = designing/fitting one prosthetic appliance for one patient. Includes exam, impression, fitting, and adjustment — typically 45–90 minutes of chair/bench time.
robot_success_rationale: Requires extreme fine dexterity inside a patient's mouth, clinical judgment, oral anatomy expertise, complex tool use (impression materials, articulators), and patient interaction. Far beyond 2026 robot capabilities.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one duty shift (e.g., one cafeteria period, one bus loading event, or one library assistance session), typically lasting 30–60 minutes for a school aide.
robot_success_rationale: Requires complex social interaction, dynamic crowd management, nuanced judgment (child safety, behavioral monitoring), and multi-environment mobility. Current humanoids lack the social cognition and authority needed for supervision roles. Very low reliability.

task: Build wooden molds, and clamp molds around area to be repaired, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = building and clamping a single wooden mold around one repair area. A competent carpenter measures, cuts, fits, and clamps the mold in roughly 30–60 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires measuring irregular repair geometry, precise wood cutting with saws, fitting custom-shaped mold pieces, and applying clamps with calibrated force — highly dexterous, multi-step, unstructured carpentry far beyond current humanoid capability.

task: Sort and distribute mail.
human_time_rationale: One instance = sorting and distributing a typical batch of incoming mail (e.g., for an office floor). Competent worker reads labels, sorts by recipient/department, and delivers to mailboxes/desks in ~20 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires reading handwritten/printed addresses (OCR in varied conditions), navigating complex indoor routes, opening mailboxes, and handing off mail correctly. Locomotion is feasible but address reading, sorting logic, and last-inch delivery to correct slots/desks challenge current robots significantly.

task: Test selected products at specified stages in the production process for performance characteristics or adherence to specifications.
human_time_rationale: One instance = testing one product sample at one production stage. Includes setup, running tests, and recording results. Typical quality-control inspection cycle.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can handle and position samples but struggle with connecting test instruments, interpreting multi-modal readings, adapting to varied test setups, and making accept/reject judgments across diverse products and specs.

task: Clean and tidy hotel lounge.
human_time_rationale: One instance = cleaning one hotel lounge area: wiping surfaces, tidying furniture, vacuuming/mopping floors, emptying bins. Typical competent housekeeper takes ~20–30 min.
robot_success_rationale: Basic sweeping/mopping is feasible (~40–70%), but rearranging varied furniture, wiping irregular surfaces, tidying scattered items, and navigating a cluttered semi-public space requires robust perception and manipulation generalisation well beyond current frontier robots.

task: Monitor cardiac patients, using electrocardiography devices, such as a holter monitor.
human_time_rationale: One instance = setting up a Holter monitor on one patient, verifying electrode placement, and confirming signal quality. A competent technician typically takes 20–40 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise electrode placement on human skin, clinical judgment about signal quality, patient communication, and safe handling of a living patient. Current robots lack the dexterity, medical sensing integration, and caregiving generalization needed. Unstructured caregiving anchor ~5–15%, but medical precision and patient safety lower this further.

task: Perform rough-ins, repair and replace fixtures and water heaters, and locate, repair, or remove leaking or broken pipes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single plumbing job (e.g., replacing one fixture or repairing one pipe section); competent plumber averages ~90 min per discrete task.
robot_success_rationale: Requires high-force torquing, soldering/cutting pipes, diagnosing hidden leaks, navigating tight confined spaces, and using specialized plumbing tools — far beyond 2026 humanoid dexterity and generalization limits.

task: Maintain campsites or recreational areas, replenishing firewood or other supplies and cleaning kitchens or restrooms.
human_time_rationale: One instance = completing a full maintenance round of a campsite/rec area: restocking firewood, replenishing supplies, and cleaning kitchen/restroom facilities. A competent camp worker typically takes ~1 hour per site.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor terrain navigation, hauling heavy/irregular firewood, unlocking and cleaning restrooms with varied fixtures, and restocking supplies — a complex multi-step outdoor task with highly unstructured environments, heavy loads, and strong generalization demands far beyond 2026 frontier robots.

task: Move levers, depress foot pedals, and turn dials to operate power machinery, such as power shovels, stripping shovels, scraper loaders, or backhoes.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one full operator shift (8 hours) running power machinery (shovel, backhoe, scraper, etc.) on a job site; managers measure productivity per shift.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoid robots cannot operate cab-based heavy machinery; controls require seated operation, high-force pedals/levers, and real-time terrain judgment. No commercial humanoid can safely drive or excavate autonomously in unstructured job-site conditions as of 2026.

task: Apply muriatic acid to clean surface, and rinse with water.
human_time_rationale: One instance = applying muriatic acid to a typical surface (e.g. concrete slab or tile), waiting for reaction, then rinsing. Includes prep/PPE donning and neutralization rinse.
robot_success_rationale: Requires safely handling a hazardous corrosive liquid (controlled pour, splash avoidance), using PPE, covering surface evenly, then rinsing. Current robots lack safe chemical handling protocols, fine liquid control, and hazard-aware autonomy for caustic materials.

task: Manage wildlife on and around airport grounds.
human_time_rationale: One instance = a single wildlife management patrol/intervention shift covering airport grounds; competent wildlife officers typically spend ~90 min per patrol circuit.
robot_success_rationale: Requires outdoor rough-terrain locomotion, species identification, hazing/dispersal techniques, legal judgment, coordination with ATC, and use of specialized tools (pyrotechnics, traps). Far beyond 2026 humanoid capabilities.

task: Cut timbers, lumber, or paneling to specified dimensions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = measuring, marking, and cutting a single piece to spec. Includes measuring, marking, positioning, cutting, and verifying dimensions; ~8 min per piece for a competent worker.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise measurement, marking, material handling of heavy/long stock, and operating power saws (miter, table, circular). Current humanoids lack safe tool operation, sufficient strength, and precision for acceptable cut quality.

task: Inspect construction projects to analyze engineering problems, using test equipment or drilling machinery.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inspecting a single construction site or project phase. A competent engineer typically spends 1–3 hours on-site conducting tests, operating equipment, and analyzing findings.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating specialized drilling machinery and test equipment, navigating rough/unstructured outdoor construction terrain, making expert engineering judgments, and integrating complex multi-modal data — all far beyond 2026 humanoid robot capabilities.

task: Observe signal lights on switchboards, and dial or press buttons to make connections.
human_time_rationale: One instance = observing a signal light and completing one connection via dial/button press. A competent telephone/switchboard operator handles this in ~1–3 minutes.
robot_success_rationale: Requires perceiving small indicator lights, interpreting switchboard state, and pressing/dialing precise controls. Vision and fine manipulation are borderline; generalizing across varied switchboard layouts and real-time signal monitoring is challenging for current humanoids.

task: Prepare land for cultivated crops, orchards, or vineyards by plowing, discing, leveling, or contouring.
human_time_rationale: One instance = preparing one field (~10–20 acres) using a tractor with implements. A competent operator typically takes 3–5 hours depending on field size and operation.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating large agricultural tractors with multiple implement attachments across uneven outdoor terrain. Humanoid robots cannot drive or operate farm machinery in unstructured field conditions; no viable 2026 general-purpose robot capability exists for this.

task: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
human_time_rationale: One instance = one duty shift (e.g., cafeteria monitoring period, a bus loading/unloading event, or a library assistance session), typically 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Requires social interaction, crowd management, verbal communication, context-aware judgment, and dynamic unstructured environments with children. Current humanoids lack reliable social/verbal autonomy, safety compliance, and supervisory decision-making in chaotic school settings.

task: Thread cables through derrick pulleys, using hand tools.
human_time_rationale: One instance = threading cables through the pulley system of a single derrick. A skilled rigger typically takes 30–60 min depending on derrick size and cable count.
robot_success_rationale: Requires climbing or working at height on a derrick, precise routing of stiff/heavy cables through narrow sheave grooves, and forceful hand-tool use in unstructured outdoor environments — far beyond current humanoid capabilities.

task: Clean and sterilize instruments and dispose of contaminated supplies.
