robot_success_rationale: Robots can carry spray/dust equipment and navigate rooms, but struggle with: precise coverage patterns, height variation (ceiling to floor), avoiding obstacles/people, chemical safety protocols, nozzle calibration, and recognizing when application is complete. Perception and manipulation need improvement.

task: Place vaccines in drinking water, inject vaccines, or dust air with vaccine powder to protect animals from diseases.
human_time_rationale: Median time to vaccinate one animal or treat one water/air batch via one chosen method (mixing, injection, or dusting). Time varies by animal type and method.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise dose measurement, safe animal handling, sterile technique, and species-specific knowledge. Water/air dosing is easier (~40–50%); injection demands dexterity and animal restraint (~10–20%). Overall integration poor due to safety/quality risks.

task: Develop methods and apparatus for securing representative plant, animal, aquatic, or soil samples.
human_time_rationale: One instance = developing/designing and prototyping methods and apparatus for a specific sample type. A trained technician would need 30–60 min for design iteration, documentation, and initial apparatus construction.
robot_success_rationale: Requires creative R&D, design reasoning, prototyping, and specialized material selection. Robots lack creative problem-solving, independent experimental design, and ability to iterate on novel apparatus without human direction. Fine manipulation of sample media is marginal.

task: Move control settings to make necessary adjustments on equipment units affecting speeds of chemical reactions, quality, or yields.
human_time_rationale: One instance = adjusting control settings on one chemical equipment unit (e.g., reactor, autoclave, pump). Includes locating controls, reading current state, and making necessary parameter adjustments.
robot_success_rationale: Requires fine-grain perception of analog/digital displays, precise button-pressing/dial-turning, and understanding process parameters and setpoints. Humanoids lack reliable visual inspection of small displays and fine control precision. Domain knowledge of chemistry needed.

task: Submit samples of products to government laboratories for testing, as required.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting, packing, labeling, and shipping one batch of product samples to a government lab. Includes documentation and facility travel time.
robot_success_rationale: Requires identifying correct products, careful packing per regulations, accurate labeling with compliance data, and coordinating with logistics/shipping. Humanoids lack domain knowledge of regulatory requirements, proper sample handling protocols, and reliable fine manipulation of documentation/sealing.

task: Spray or inject vegetation with insecticides to kill insects or to protect against disease or with herbicides to reduce competing vegetation.
human_time_rationale: One instance = spraying/injecting one field or crop section. Includes setup, application, and moving equipment; typical agricultural acre takes 30–60 min.
robot_success_rationale: Robots lack robust outdoor terrain mobility, reliable plant detection/targeting in variable crop canopy, safe chemical handling and pressure system control, and regulatory compliance for pesticide application. Heavy weather/mud conditions, uneven terrain degrade performance.

task: Analyze samples of biological material for chemical content or reaction.
human_time_rationale: Analyzing a single biological sample (e.g., blood, tissue, culture) for chemical content/reaction using lab equipment; includes setup, incubation, measurement, and documentation.
robot_success_rationale: Robots lack fine dexterity for pipetting, vial manipulation, and sensitive instrument operation. Sample preparation, reagent handling, and precise measurement require high precision and context-awareness. Lab safety/containment constraints also significant.

task: Inventory and distribute nuclear, biological, and chemical detection and contamination equipment, providing instruction in its maintenance and use.
human_time_rationale: One instance = inventorying, distributing, and instructing a team on NBC equipment maintenance/use at one site; includes counting/logging equipment, explaining procedures, demonstrating use. Estimate 2 hours for a comprehensive session.
robot_success_rationale: Task requires specialized domain knowledge, safe handling of hazardous detection equipment, technical instruction, and adaptive social interaction. Robots lack credible safety authority, cannot reliably teach or verify trainee understanding. Perception of NBC equipment is adequate, but instruction delivery and safety accountability demand human expertise.

task: Collect and deliver sample solutions for laboratory analysis.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collect sample(s) from designated location(s), place in container(s), transport to lab. Typical small-scale task assuming samples are pre-identified and nearby.
robot_success_rationale: Robots handle pick-and-place of containers well (~70%), but sample collection often requires careful handling of fragile glassware, precise liquid transfer, contamination avoidance, and navigation in lab environments with obstacles. Generalization to varied sample types/locations limited.

task: Spray trees to treat diseased or unhealthy trees, including mixing chemicals and calibrating spray equipment.
human_time_rationale: One instance: mix chemicals, calibrate spray equipment, and treat one tree or small group (5–10 trees). Includes safety setup and cleanup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires: outdoor terrain navigation (uneven ground, low success ~30–50%), precise chemical handling and mixing (safety-critical, <10%), equipment calibration with fine adjustments (<10%), sustained spraying on tall/varied tree geometry. Multiple failure modes. Unsafe autonomous deployment.

task: Crossbreed animals with existing strains or cross strains to obtain new combinations of desirable characteristics.
human_time_rationale: Selecting animals, facilitating breeding (natural or assisted), monitoring pregnancy, documenting genetics. One instance = one successful breeding event or mating season outcome.
robot_success_rationale: Requires live animal handling, behavioral interpretation, knowledge of reproductive biology, safety judgment, and unpredictable live animal responses. Humanoids lack animal handling expertise, fine tactile feedback for safe restraint, and breeding judgment.

task: Mix specified materials or chemicals, and dump solutions, powders, or seeds into planter or sprayer machinery.
human_time_rationale: Mixing materials/chemicals and loading into planter/sprayer machinery for one batch or tank fill. Includes measuring, combining, and pouring.
robot_success_rationale: Requires accurate liquid/powder measurement, safe chemical handling, and precision pouring into variable machinery openings. Poor generalization across tank shapes, chemical hazards, and spill/splash risks. Limited by dexterity and safety constraints.

task: Prepare and package environmental samples for shipping or testing.
human_time_rationale: Collecting, labeling, sealing samples in containers, filling shipping forms; one instance = preparing a batch of 5–10 samples for transport or lab analysis.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise liquid/solid handling, reading labels, sealing containers without contamination, and careful packaging. Fine dexterity and strict protocol adherence present significant barriers; perception of sample integrity is limited.

task: Collect samples of hazardous materials or arrange for sample collection.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collecting hazmat samples from a site (location survey, 3–5 samples, documentation, packaging). Assumes trained hazmat technician; time includes safety protocols, containment, labeling.
robot_success_rationale: Hazmat collection requires precise chemical identification, safe containment, regulatory compliance, and handling of unknown/reactive substances. Robots lack reliable chemical sensing, dexterous fine-sampling capability, and ability to assess safety hazards autonomously. Risk of contamination or exposure makes autonomous operation infeasible.

task: Collect air samples from planes or ships over land or sea to study atmospheric composition.
human_time_rationale: One instance = deploying and collecting air samples during one flight/voyage segment. Includes setup, sample extraction, and equipment securing. Time varies by mission but 45 min is typical for a single sampling operation mid-flight or voyage.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoids cannot pilot aircraft or operate ships. Task requires vehicle operation in dynamic, high-altitude or maritime environments. Even if deployed as payload, sampling equipment integration and in-situ operation in pressurized cabins or marine decks remain beyond current robot autonomy and manipulation capability.

task: Process refined feedstock with additives in fermentation or reaction process vessels.
human_time_rationale: One instance = prepare, dose, and monitor one batch through feedstock addition and initial reaction phase. Includes measurement, mixing, temperature checks.
robot_success_rationale: Requires precise dosing of liquids/powders into vessels, reading gauges/displays, monitoring reaction parameters, and responding to dynamic conditions. Limited dexterity for small valve/lid manipulation; poor generalization across vessel types and additive handling.

task: Cover areas to specified depths with pesticides, applying knowledge of weather conditions, droplet sizes, elevation-to-distance ratios, and obstructions.
human_time_rationale: One instance = spraying pesticide over a typical field (20-40 acres). Includes setup, calibration, application, and cleanup. Time varies with field size, weather, and obstacles.
robot_success_rationale: Requires operating heavy spraying equipment (boom spray, drift control), dynamic weather sensing, precise droplet/pressure calibration, and real-time obstacle avoidance. Humanoids cannot operate or steer agricultural vehicles; liability/chemical exposure risks prohibitive. Task fundamentally vehicle-dependent.

task: Clean and remove blockages from infested areas to facilitate spraying procedures and provide drainage, using brooms, mops, shovels, or rakes.
human_time_rationale: Cleaning and clearing a typical infested area (warehouse, basement, or outdoor space) of debris, blockages, and waste to prepare for pest control spraying. One instance = one room or bounded outdoor zone.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can sweep/mop flat surfaces (~40–70%), but infested areas are unpredictable, cluttered, and may contain hazardous materials. Removing blockages requires spatial reasoning, tool adaptation, and navigating unstructured obstacles. Limited generalization and safety concerns reduce success.

task: Attach hoses or nozzles to machines, using wrenches and pliers, and make adjustments to obtain the proper dispersion of spray.
human_time_rationale: One instance = attaching one hose/nozzle assembly, securing with wrenches/pliers, and testing spray pattern. Typical agricultural or industrial spray equipment setup.
robot_success_rationale: Requires tool-use (wrenches, pliers), fine force control to avoid cross-threading, and visual assessment of spray dispersion. Current robots struggle with precise torque, tool manipulation in constrained spaces, and spray pattern verification.

task: Obtain specimens, cultivating, isolating, and identifying microorganisms for analysis.
human_time_rationale: Full cycle: specimen collection (~15 min), culture setup (~30 min), incubation monitoring (~60 min over days), isolation via plating (~45 min), identification via staining/microscopy (~30 min). One instance = complete analysis of one specimen type.
robot_success_rationale: Requires sterile technique, fine manipulation of petri dishes/pipettes, microscopy interpretation, and domain knowledge. Humanoids lack precision for aseptic handling, cannot reliably read microscope imagery for identification, and cannot autonomously make culturing decisions. Extremely specialized task.

task: Start motors and engage machinery, such as sprayer agitators or pumps or portable spray equipment.
human_time_rationale: Starting a sprayer, pump, or agitator typically involves locating the control (button, switch, key), verifying settings, and engaging it—a straightforward manual task taking 1–3 minutes per instance.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoids can locate and press buttons/switches on familiar equipment with ~50–70% accuracy, but identifying correct control, safety checks, and verifying successful engagement on varied sprayer/pump models remains challenging. Risk of error on non-standard equipment reduces confidence.

task: Connect hoses and nozzles selected according to terrain, distribution pattern requirements, types of infestations, and velocities.
human_time_rationale: One instance = selecting and connecting 2-4 hose/nozzle combinations for a pest control spray rig setup; includes visual inspection and hand-tightening.
robot_success_rationale: Robots can pick and thread simple connectors; challenge is interpreting terrain/infestation requirements to select correct nozzle type, then ensure proper sealing without leaks. Requires conditional reasoning and fine dexterous work.

task: Drive trucks or other heavy equipment to convey contaminated waste to designated sea or ground locations.
human_time_rationale: Typical hazardous waste transport cycle: loading, driving to designated site (often 30–60 min drive), unloading, compliance documentation. One instance = one full trip from origin to disposal site.
robot_success_rationale: Humanoids cannot operate heavy trucks or specialized waste equipment on public roads. Requires vehicle operation, navigation in traffic, legal licensure, regulatory compliance, and safety responsibility. Current robots lack capability for autonomous driving in uncontrolled environments.

task: Collect samples from animals, plants, or products and route them to laboratories for microbiological assessment, ingredient verification, or other testing.
human_time_rationale: One instance = collect and properly label samples from multiple sources (animals, plants, or products), package safely, and route to lab. Typical field/facility task takes 30–60 minutes depending on sample count and location complexity.
robot_success_rationale: Limited dexterity for precise sampling (swabs, tissue extraction, sterile technique); weak object recognition for diverse product/plant/animal identification; cannot navigate to varied external locations reliably; cannot handle contamination protocols or safety hazards (biohazard routing). Logistics/packaging feasible but sampling itself is critical bottleneck.

task: Apply poisons along roadsides and in animal burrows to eliminate unwanted roadside vegetation and rodents.
human_time_rationale: One instance = treating 1–2 miles of roadside plus nearby burrows; includes mixing, application via sprayer/spreader, safety protocols.
robot_success_rationale: Requires safe handling of toxic chemicals, precise application near infrastructure, navigation of rough/outdoor terrain, identification of burrow locations, and regulatory compliance. Current robots lack chemical containment, fine control, and safety certifications for hazmat work.
